You are an AgentsFlow external read-only reviewer.
Start from zero prior conversation context. Do not use or assume any forked orchestrator context. Review only the provided packet. Do not request repository access. Do not modify files. Do not run tests. Do not execute scripts. Do not produce patches. Do not update evidence. Return exactly one schema-valid reviewer-report JSON object and no markdown fence. Do not return prose outside JSON. If there are no findings, return an empty findings array and put residual uncertainty in summary or self_declared_limitations.

All findings must be candidate-unvalidated. Report missing mandatory evidence. Report plausible P0/P1 blockers even outside a focused role. When you mark a finding P0/P1, include the concrete blocker path: which contract, accepted decision, gate policy, authority boundary, safety rule or mandatory evidence requirement is at risk; what evidence supports it; and what acceptance consequence follows if it is not fixed. Risk-surface or Failure Path Matrix membership alone is not severity. The main/orchestrating agent validates relevance before findings affect workflow decisions.

Your reviewer-report JSON must include review_context with run_id, material_change_id, review_packet_path and reviewer_instance_id copied from the review packet when present. Do not invent those values.

Resolved reviewer role contract:
name: generalist
kind: reviewer_role
purpose: Apply the common review rubric without a specialized focus zone.
primary_focus:
- contract and accepted-decision consistency
- verification evidence and missing mandatory checks
- scope boundaries and non-goals
- obvious architecture, reliability, safety or workflow risks
must_report:
- Any plausible P0/P1 blocker, even if it does not fit a narrower rubric section.
- Missing mandatory evidence.
- Contradictions between contract, diff, gate report and accepted decisions.
forbidden_actions:
- run_tests
- run_scripts
- modify_files
- create_patch

    "PR merge readiness evaluator rejects review packets whose material_change_id does not match the evaluated report material_change_id.",

    {
      "path": "AGENTS.md",
      "size_bytes": 11014,
      "content": "# AGENTS.md\n\nThese instructions apply to AI coding agents working in this repository.\n\n## Language\n\nRepository artifacts should be primarily in English for portability across coding agents and future users. Russian handoff prompts are allowed when the user asks for them, but repository docs should default to English.\n\n## Prime directive\n\nDo not expand scope. Implement accepted decisions. Make AgentsFlow usable for v0.2 MVP.\n\nDo not treat workflows as monolithic prompts. Preserve the modular philosophy:\n\n```text\nskills + scripts + templates + packs + profiles → workflows\n```\n\nA workflow composes capabilities. It should not duplicate all instructions from the skills it invokes.\n\n## Source of truth\n\nBefore changing workflow design, read:\n\n1. latest checkpoint in `docs/checkpoints/`;\n2. relevant ADRs in `docs/adr/`;\n3. `docs/philosophy.md`;\n4. `docs/workflow-model.md`;\n5. `docs/mvp-ready-workflow-standard.md`;\n6. this `AGENTS.md`.\n\nFor a broader orientation path, use README.md's \"Suggested review path\" plus the\nprimary e2e example at `examples/e2e/minimal-python-project/`.\n\nPreserve accepted decisions unless the user explicitly approves changing them.\n\n## v0.2 MVP scope\n\nApplication workflow:\n\n```text\nproject-initialization\n```\n\nMVP supported target workflow:\n\n```text\nbig-feature-contract-first\n```\n\nv0.2 utility workflow:\n\n```text\nreview-only-fusion\npr-merge-readiness\n```\n\nNon-MVP workflows remain reference/experimental and schema-valid only:\n\n```text\nagentic-system-hardening\nbugfix-regression-capture\nnew-project-spec-first\nprompt-behavior-eval\nsafe-refactor\nresearch-to-ADR\n```\n\n`project-initialization` is broad enough to support multiple modes, but the v0.2\nsupported application path is `prepare-workflow -> big-feature-contract-first`.\nOther initialization modes remain documented/reference unless explicitly used by\nan accepted slice.\n\nPrimary e2e example:\n\n```text\nexamples/e2e/minimal-python-project/\n```\n\nCMake FetchContent / FetchPackage support is a documented pattern/skeleton only in v0.2.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nBefore adding or changing a workflow:\n\n1. Define intent and MVP status.\n2. List invoked skills/scripts/templates/packs.\n3. Specify supported strictness profiles and review topology.\n4. Reference gate manifests rather than prose-only gate names.\n5. Identify risk surfaces that can affect gates, evidence, review topology or\n   failure-path coverage, using `docs/risk-and-strictness.md`.\n6. Keep project-specific commands out of upstream workflow definitions.\n7. If the workflow has an implementation phase, frame it with a preceding red-capture (failing-test) phase and a following green-verify phase (ADR-0017).\n\nBefore adding a skill:\n\n1. Create `skills/<name>/SKILL.md`.\n2. Create `skills/<name>/skill.yaml`.\n3. Define inputs, outputs, dependencies and compatible workflows.\n\nBefore adding a script:\n\n1. Put deterministic logic in `scripts/<name>.py` or a scoped subdirectory.\n2. Put its manifest in `scripts/contracts/<name>.yaml`.\n3. Prefer JSON/text outputs that can be used as evidence.\n4. Do not hide non-deterministic model judgment inside deterministic scripts.\n\n## Gate and binding rules\n\n- Do not treat BDD/Gherkin scenarios as executable gates by themselves.\n- Required acceptance scenarios need `*.bindings.yaml` behavior bindings.\n- Do not hard-code project-specific commands into upstream workflow definitions.\n- A real workflow-run gate is executable only after it is bound to a deterministic project-level runner.\n- Upstream gate manifests are gate contracts/templates plus generic validation helpers.\n- An implementation phase must be framed by a red-capture phase (tests run against the not-yet-implemented state, failing run captured) and a green-verify phase (same tests re-run, passing run captured). `validate_repo.py` rejects workflow definitions whose implementation phase is missing this structural framing. Full run-artifact proof of the actual red and green executions is still workflow evidence, not a schema-only fact. See `docs/adr/ADR-0017-test-framed-implementation-phase.md`.\n- Refactor-only implementation may use a pre-change `baseline_capture` before `change_type: refactor`; this is not a substitute for red-capture when new behavior is implemented.\n\n## Project application / initialization rules\n\nWhen applying AgentsFlow to a concrete project:\n\n- Treat `.agentsflow/upstream` as a pinned upstream dependency and do not edit it during normal workflows.\n- First-stage dependency modes are Git submodule or CMake FetchContent / FetchPackage-style dependency.\n- Create project-specific bindings and executable gates in the project overlay, not in upstream.\n- Start project initialization with an explicit project intake / research assignment. Unknown-project discovery uses the standard exploratory assignment, not an empty prompt.\n- Analyze code, docs, ADRs, agent instructions, process artifacts and Markdown implementation history when present.\n- Separate raw observed facts from model-produced inventory and expert assessments.\n- Mark inferred fields with provenance, confidence and human-confirmation needs.\n- Explicitly identify project domain(s), separate domain assumptions from observed evidence, and ask whether the user has domain expertise that should constrain initialization decisions.\n- Before drafting the project overlay, conduct an agent-led human operating-decisions interview for gate policy, review topology, reviewer/model strategy, maximum review cycles, risk-surface policy, authority boundaries and evidence storage. Do not ask the human to manually fill a YAML/JSON file; the agent records the normalized `project-operating-decisions.yaml` artifact after the dialogue.\n- For contract-first implementation readiness, select feature-specific risk\n  surfaces, record required path classes, and create a Failure Path Matrix when\n  selected surfaces have denial, failure, timeout, rejection, persistence or\n  authority semantics. Bind those rows to behavior bindings, gate evidence or an\n  explicit human-approved deferral before red capture or implementation.\n- Human interaction is main-agent mediated. Review agents must not ask the human questions directly; they produce candidate findings, recommendations and questions for the main agent to synthesize. A workflow may pause for humans only at declared human-interaction phases or true blocking clarifications.\n- For legacy agent-system adoption, recommend `knowledge-extraction` as the\n  conservative default unless evidence supports another mode, but record the\n  human-confirmed decision before acting.\n- Documentation legacy adoption is a separate project-initialization decision.\n  The agent may recommend `preserve-as-is`, `knowledge-extraction`,\n  `rewrite-migration` or `archive-delete`, but must not select the mode without\n  explicit human confirmation. If `knowledge-extraction` is selected, record\n  extraction depth separately as `light`, `standard` or `deep`.\n- Do not rewrite `AGENTS.md` or activate a migration without human approval.\n\n## Review and fusion rules\n\n- Verification gates produce authoritative verification evidence.\n- Gate evidence must include structured command evidence and be fresh after the\n  latest material change when it supports acceptance.\n- Review agents are read-only and run after verification gates by default.\n- Default review is `homogeneous-dual`: two independent generalist reviewers\n  receive the same prompt, same review packet, same rubric and same output schema.\n- Primary review gates require at least two reviewers. Collision-control also uses\n  two fresh-context control reviewers: rejected or downgraded blocker-level\n  candidate findings from the same review cycle are recorded as one collision\n  batch and sent to those two control reviewers.\n- Heterogeneous review is explicit, not inferred. Reviewer role ids such as\n  `adversarial`, `architecture` or `verification` must resolve to role definitions\n  in `profiles/reviewer_roles/`; focus zones may overlap and do not prevent any\n  reviewer from reporting a plausible P0/P1 blocker outside its focus.\n- Review agents start from zero conversation context and must not be launched by\n  forking the main/orchestrating agent context. They receive only the review\n  packet and referenced artifacts declared by the workflow or project binding.\n- Review-agent findings are candidate findings, not authoritative truth.\n- The main/orchestrating agent must validate finding relevance before findings affect workflow decisions.\n- Fusion is read-only synthesis, not majority voting.\n- Review packets should include selected risk surfaces, Failure Path Matrix rows,\n  known blockers and latest green evidence after the latest material change.\n- Supplemental human-requested review is allowed after green evidence, but it\n  reopens the fix loop only for main-agent-validated P0/P1 findings or mandatory\n  evidence gaps.\n\n## External reviewer provider rules\n\n- Claude Code external reviewer provider is in v0.2 MVP scope.\n- External model reviewers must be invoked only through explicit project-bound wrappers.\n- For Claude Code CLI external review, v0.2 permits subscription-local usage only.\n- API-key based Claude usage is forbidden. Wrappers must fail if any configured\n  forbidden Claude API/proxy environment variable is present.\n- Claude API/proxy environment routes are also forbidden by the provider baseline:\n  `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK` and\n  `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX`.\n- External reviewer outputs are candidate findings, not authoritative truth.\n- External reviewers do not replace verification gates and do not modify files or run tests by default.\n- Store review packets, normalized reviewer report and invocation metadata as\n  run evidence. Store raw provider output only when explicitly non-sensitive;\n  otherwise store a redacted artifact, summary or pointer.\n\n## Validation\n\n```bash\npython3 -m venv .venv\n. .venv/bin/activate\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\npython3 scripts/validate_repo.py --root .\npython3 -m pytest -q\n\n# external reviewer wrapper smoke test (no live Claude call):\npython3 scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py \\\n  --provider claude-code \\\n  --config examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/claude-code.yaml \\\n  --input examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/review-packet.architecture.json \\\n  --mock-response examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/mock-raw-output.json \\\n  --output /tmp/reviewer-report.claude-architecture.json\n```\n\n## Evidence discipline\n\nWhen completing a task, provide an acceptance proof:\n\n- changed files;\n- relevant contract(s);\n- tests/scripts run;\n- scenario coverage;\n- boundary check result;\n- known limitations;\n- unresolved design questions.\n\n## Non-goals for v0.2\n\nDo not introduce:\n\n- full CLI/package distribution;\n- generic multi-provider reviewer runtime;\n- API-key Claude usage;\n- implementation agents as first-class actors;\n- full haft/quint-code integration;\n- formal TLA+/Quint baseline;\n- UI, database, cloud service or agent runtime.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "README.md",
      "size_bytes": 7457,
      "content": "# AgentsFlow\n\nVersion: v0.2 MVP readiness snapshot\n\n> Workflow kit and reference methodology for controlled agent-assisted development.\n\nAgentsFlow is a text-first, tool-light workflow kit. It makes agent-assisted development explicit, reviewable and reproducible through workflows, skills, scripts, templates, schemas, project overlays, gates, evidence reports, reviewer reports and fusion reports.\n\nAgentsFlow is **not** an agent runtime, execution platform, UI, database, or replacement for coding harnesses such as Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Cline or Roo.\n\n## Core model\n\n```text\nWorkflows compose.\nSkills guide.\nScripts verify.\nTemplates structure.\nSchemas validate.\nPacks specialize.\nProfiles tune.\nArtifacts carry evidence.\n```\n\nPrimary abstraction: `Workflow`.\n\nReusable building blocks:\n\n- `skills/` — agent-facing procedures;\n- `scripts/` — deterministic checks and automation;\n- `templates/` — artifact shapes;\n- `schemas/` — machine-readable validation;\n- `packs/` — domain/project rules;\n- `profiles/` — strictness and review topology knobs.\n\nArtifacts include task contracts, plans, behavior bindings, gate reports, evidence reports, reviewer reports, finding-validation reports and fusion reports.\n\n## v0.2 MVP boundary\n\nApplication workflow:\n\n```text\nproject-initialization\n```\n\nMVP supported target workflow:\n\n```text\nbig-feature-contract-first\n```\n\nv0.2 utility workflows:\n\n```text\nreview-only-fusion\npr-merge-readiness\n```\n\nNon-MVP workflows remain reference/experimental and schema-valid only:\n\n```text\nagentic-system-hardening\nbugfix-regression-capture\nnew-project-spec-first\nprompt-behavior-eval\nsafe-refactor\nresearch-to-ADR\n```\n\nThe supported v0.2 application path is:\n\n```text\nproject-initialization.prepare-workflow -> big-feature-contract-first\n```\n\nPrimary end-to-end example target:\n\n```text\nexamples/e2e/minimal-python-project/\n```\n\n## Project application model\n\nAgentsFlow is applied to a concrete project through three layers:\n\n```text\nAgentsFlow upstream      — pinned workflow kit / methodology\nProject overlay/binding  — project-specific paths, gates, tools, policies\nWorkflow run             — task-specific contracts, plans, evidence and reports\n```\n\nProject initialization includes an agent-led human operating-decisions interview.\nThe agent asks about gate policy, reviewer count/model strategy, review-cycle\nlimits, authority boundaries and evidence storage, then records the normalized\n`project-operating-decisions.yaml` artifact. The human is not asked to manually\nfill a YAML/JSON file.\n\nThe v0.2 project overlay uses one canonical shape: flat `.agentsflow/project.yaml`,\nstructured `.agentsflow/workflows/*.binding.yaml`, and upstream pinning in\n`.agentsflow/agentsflow.lock.yaml`.\n\nHuman interaction is workflow-scoped and main-agent mediated. The main agent may\npause at declared decision phases, record questions in `human-questions.yaml`,\nrecord answers in `human-decisions.yaml`, and then resume. Review agents do not\nask humans questions directly.\n\nFirst-stage dependency modes:\n\n- Git submodule;\n- CMake FetchContent / FetchPackage-style dependency.\n\nCLI/package distribution is future work.\n\n## Gate and behavior rules\n\n- Upstream gates are gate contracts/templates, not project-executable gates.\n- A real gate is executable only after project binding maps it to deterministic project-level runners, commands, tools and evidence sources.\n- BDD/Gherkin scenarios are behavior specifications, not executable gates.\n- Required acceptance scenarios must be bound to executable checks through `*.bindings.yaml`.\n- A workflow phase of `kind: implementation` must be framed by a pre-implementation red-capture phase (contract scenarios turned into executable tests, run against the not-yet-implemented state, failing run captured) and a post-implementation green-verify phase (same tests re-run, passing run captured). `validate_repo.py` enforces the phase topology; run-artifact validation of the actual failing/passing evidence pair remains future work. See `docs/adr/ADR-0017-test-framed-implementation-phase.md`.\n\n## Review rules\n\n- Verification gates produce authoritative verification evidence.\n- Review agents are read-only by default and run after the verification gate.\n- Review-agent findings are candidate findings until the main/orchestrating agent validates relevance.\n- Fusion is read-only synthesis, not majority voting.\n\n## External reviewer provider\n\nv0.2 includes a minimal Claude Code external reviewer provider:\n\n- Claude Code CLI only;\n- subscription-local only;\n- API-key/proxy usage forbidden;\n- wrapper must fail if the configured forbidden Claude API/proxy environment variables are present;\n- Codex-launched Claude reviewer runs require escalated sandbox access for local\n  subscription auth. Stdin packet transport disables Claude Code tools with\n  `--tools \"\"`; file prompt transport may use only `--tools Read` for the\n  generated prompt file in an isolated temporary directory;\n- review packet in, normalized reviewer-report out;\n- normalized report and invocation metadata stored as evidence, with raw output\n  stored only when explicitly non-sensitive;\n- findings remain candidate/unvalidated.\n\nSee:\n\n- `docs/external-reviewer-provider-model.md`\n- `docs/pr-merge-readiness.md`\n- `docs/adr/ADR-0016-external-reviewer-provider-interface.md`\n- `examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/`\n\n## Quick validation\n\n```bash\npython3 -m venv .venv\n. .venv/bin/activate\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\npython3 scripts/validate_repo.py --root .\npython3 -m pytest -q\n```\n\nExample checks:\n\n```bash\npython3 scripts/contract_lint.py --contract examples/memory-policy/Docs/contracts/memory-policy.contract.md\npython3 scripts/gherkin_lint.py --contract examples/memory-policy/Docs/contracts/memory-policy.contract.md\npython3 scripts/bdd_binding_check.py --bindings examples/memory-policy/Docs/contracts/memory-policy.bindings.yaml\npython3 scripts/evidence_validate.py --evidence examples/memory-policy/evidence-report.md\n```\n\nExternal reviewer wrapper smoke test without calling Claude:\n\n```bash\npython3 scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py \\\n  --provider claude-code \\\n  --config examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/claude-code.yaml \\\n  --input examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/review-packet.architecture.json \\\n  --mock-response examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/mock-raw-output.json \\\n  --output /tmp/reviewer-report.claude-architecture.json\n```\n\n## Suggested review path\n\nStart here:\n\n1. `docs/checkpoints/checkpoint-2026-06-20-documentation-legacy-adoption-confirmation.md`\n2. `docs/checkpoints/checkpoint-2026-06-19-v0.2-supported-path.md`\n3. `docs/checkpoints/checkpoint-2026-06-19-v0.2-expert-assessment-output-contract.md`\n4. `docs/checkpoints/checkpoint-2026-06-18-v0.2.0-slice2.md`\n5. `docs/retrospectives/documentation-consistency-review-2026-06-17-v0.1.13.md`\n6. `docs/philosophy.md`\n7. `docs/workflow-model.md`\n8. `docs/mvp-ready-workflow-standard.md`\n9. `docs/project-application-model.md`\n10. `docs/project-initialization-model.md`\n11. `docs/enforcement-boundary.md`\n12. `docs/human-interaction-protocol.md`\n13. `docs/gate-executability-model.md`\n14. `docs/behavior-binding-model.md`\n15. `docs/review-control-model.md`\n16. `docs/review-prompt-contract.md`\n17. `docs/external-reviewer-provider-model.md`\n\n## Development rule for coding agents\n\nDo not expand scope. Implement accepted decisions. Make AgentsFlow usable for the v0.2 MVP path.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "docs/external-reviewer-provider-model.md",
      "size_bytes": 11019,
      "content": "# External Reviewer Provider Model\n\nAgentsFlow may use external model providers as **reviewer providers**. In v0.2 this is a project-bound capability, not a top-level runtime abstraction.\n\nThe initial MVP provider is **Claude Code CLI**, invoked from another harness such as Codex as an independent read-only reviewer.\n\n## Core decision\n\nExternal model reviewers must be invoked only through explicit project-bound wrappers.\n\nA wrapper receives a bounded **review packet** and returns a normalized `reviewer-report.json` that conforms to the AgentsFlow reviewer-report schema.\n\nExternal reviewers:\n\n- are read-only by default;\n- start from fresh zero conversation context;\n- must not receive a forked main-agent/orchestrator conversation;\n- receive review packets rather than unrestricted repository authority;\n- do not run verification gates;\n- do not run tests in v0.2;\n- do not modify files;\n- do not produce patches;\n- produce candidate findings only;\n- must pass main/orchestrating-agent relevance validation before their findings affect the workflow decision.\n\n## MVP provider: Claude Code CLI\n\nFor v0.2, AgentsFlow implements a minimal Claude Code external reviewer provider:\n\n```text\nreview packet\n→ preparation evidence\n→ project-bound Claude wrapper\n→ raw Claude output\n→ normalized reviewer-report.json\n→ reviewer invocation metadata\n```\n\nThe MVP implementation target is intentionally narrow:\n\n- provider: `claude-code` only;\n- billing/auth: subscription-local Claude Code CLI only;\n- API-key usage: forbidden;\n- launch mode: Codex invocations require escalated sandbox access so Claude Code\n  can see subscription-local auth/keychain state;\n- Claude permission mode: `default`. Do not use Claude Code `plan` mode for\n  reviewer invocations because plan mode may route the deliverable into\n  Claude-managed plan artifacts instead of returning schema-bound JSON to the\n  wrapper;\n- tool mode: Claude Code tools are disabled for stdin prompt transport. For\n  file prompt transport, only the `Read` tool is allowed and only so Claude can\n  read the generated review prompt file from its isolated temporary reviewer\n  directory;\n- output: normalized reviewer report + invocation metadata, plus raw output\n  only when the provider config explicitly preserves non-sensitive raw output;\n- normalization trace: source path/hash in the reviewer report, output hash in\n  invocation metadata;\n- no multi-provider runtime;\n- no write-enabled external reviewers;\n- no CI/enterprise API-key mode.\n\n## API-key usage policy\n\nFor the v0.2 MVP, API-key based Claude usage is **forbidden**.\n\nRationale:\n\n- API-key usage can create uncontrolled or unexpectedly high cost.\n- The intended first use case is a local personal workflow using an already-authenticated Claude Code CLI subscription session.\n- AgentsFlow must not silently switch a review provider from subscription-local mode to API-key mode.\n\nProvider wrappers must fail fast if API-key usage is detected.\n\nMinimum required guardrails for Claude Code CLI wrappers:\n\n- `expected_billing_mode: subscription-local`\n- `forbid_api_key_usage: true`\n- fail if any of these environment variables are present:\n  `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`,\n  `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK`, `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX`;\n- do not use API-key-only execution modes;\n- run through an escalated Codex sandbox when invoked from Codex;\n- use Claude Code `--permission-mode default`;\n- use `--tools \"\"` for stdin packet transport, or `--tools Read` only for file\n  prompt transport where the temporary prompt file is the sole added directory;\n- record billing/auth mode in invocation metadata;\n- record stdout/stderr/exit code and normalized output;\n- validate output schema before passing it to finding validation or fusion.\n\nAPI-key mode is not a future default. It may only be reconsidered as a separate explicit enterprise/CI design decision.\n\n## Interface\n\n```text\nExternal Reviewer Provider =\n  project-bound executable adapter\n  that receives a review packet\n  and returns normalized reviewer-report.json\n```\n\nExample command shape:\n\n```bash\n.agentsflow/scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py \\\n  --provider claude-code \\\n  --config .agentsflow/external-reviewers/claude-code.yaml \\\n  --input Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/review-packet.architecture.json \\\n  --output Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/reviewer-report.claude-architecture.json\n```\n\n## Review-set assignment layer\n\nProvider config is not the review topology. A review gate first declares its\nreviewer instances and roles in the review prompt contract, then optionally\nbinds those instances to providers through `reviewer_assignments`.\n\nFor v0.2 this binding is intentionally small:\n\n- `reviewer_set` declares reviewer ids and role contracts;\n- `provider_policy` declares whether external reviewers and model diversity are\n  allowed or required;\n- `reviewer_assignments` maps each reviewer id to a provider, model family and\n  output paths;\n- `run_review_set.py` dispatches external assignments through\n  `run_external_reviewer.py`. External subprocesses are started asynchronously\n  inside one review set before the runner waits for results; internal reviewers\n  remain report-present artifacts that the runner verifies.\n- external reviewer collection is bounded by a per-reviewer timeout\n  (`--external-reviewer-timeout-seconds`, default 900). A timed-out reviewer is\n  killed and recorded in the invocation set, while already completed external\n  reviewer evidence is preserved.\n- completed runs store `review_invocation_set` evidence linking assignments to\n  normalized reports, raw external output and invocation metadata.\n- run-scope provider-assigned gates store `review_artifact_preparation`\n  evidence before invocation, so packets and prompts are tied to explicit input\n  artifacts and worktree status.\n- mock responses are smoke-test evidence only; completed review gates require\n  external invocation metadata with `execution_mode: real`.\n- completed external evidence must be bound to the current packet, prompt,\n  contract, role contract, rubric, output schema and normalized reviewer report\n  hashes, with `exit_code: 0`; when raw provider output is preserved as\n  non-sensitive, its path/hash must also match, and otherwise a redacted,\n  summary or pointer artifact is recorded by the readiness report.\n- normalized reviewer reports may include `normalization` source trace, but\n  the normalized report's own output hash is recorded outside the report to\n  avoid self-referential hashes.\n\nThis keeps Claude Code as one provider behind the existing wrapper while\nallowing a single review gate to mix internal Codex reports and external Claude\nreports. Adding another provider should add a provider adapter and config\nschema support, not a parallel review workflow.\n\nIn practice, artifact assembly is deterministic for the v0.2 provider path:\n`prepare_review_set_artifacts.py` materializes declared packets, prompts and\npreparation evidence. Model reviewers return structured findings and summaries;\nthey are not responsible for inventing file paths or evidence hashes.\n\nExample assignment shape:\n\n```yaml\nprovider_policy:\n  allow_external_reviewers: true\n  require_model_diversity: true\n  min_distinct_provider_model_families: 2\n  unsupported_provider_behavior: config_blocker\n\nreviewer_assignments:\n  - reviewer: generalist-a\n    provider: internal-agent\n    model_family: codex\n    packet_path: Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/review-packet.generalist-a.json\n    report_path: Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/reviewer-report.codex-generalist-a.json\n  - reviewer: generalist-b\n    provider: claude-code\n    model_family: opus\n    provider_config: .agentsflow/external-reviewers/claude-code.yaml\n    packet_path: Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/review-packet.generalist-b.json\n    report_path: Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/reviewer-report.claude-generalist-b.json\n    raw_output_path: Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/reviewer-report.claude-generalist-b.raw.json\n    invocation_metadata_path: Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/reviewer-invocation.claude-generalist-b.json\n```\n\n## Review packet\n\nThe main/orchestrating agent prepares a bounded review packet. The external reviewer should review the packet, not freely re-orchestrate the project.\n\nA review packet should contain:\n\n- AgentsFlow version;\n- workflow and run id;\n- reviewer role and review goal;\n- context policy: `start_mode: fresh_context` and `fork_conversation_context: false`;\n- review prompt contract reference;\n- resolved reviewer role contract reference;\n- task contract or reviewed artifact;\n- plan, diff summary or target artifact summary;\n- changed files, if applicable;\n- verification gate report, if applicable;\n- evidence summary;\n- accepted ADRs and project rules;\n- project agent instructions such as `AGENTS.md`, when present;\n- forbidden actions;\n- output schema reference.\n\nAgentsFlow v0.2 does not require a provider-specific instruction file such as\n`CLAUDE.md`. External providers should receive the same bounded project rules as\nother reviewers through explicit review-packet artifacts. If a project already\nhas `AGENTS.md`, the orchestrating agent may include it directly in the packet\nor reference it with a recorded hash. A provider-specific instruction file, when\npresent, is just another declared packet artifact and must not silently override\nthe run's review prompt contract.\n\n## Claude adapter responsibilities\n\nThe Claude adapter must:\n\n1. load provider config;\n2. load and validate review packet;\n3. verify subscription-local mode and reject API-key usage;\n4. render the provider prompt;\n5. invoke Claude Code CLI in non-interactive print mode;\n6. capture requested model/effort, provider-reported model usage and process\n   metadata; capture raw output only when `normalization.preserve_raw_output`\n   is explicitly true for non-sensitive evidence;\n7. parse and normalize `reviewer-report.json`;\n8. record normalization source trace;\n9. validate schema;\n10. mark findings as candidate/unvalidated;\n11. store invocation metadata, including the normalized output hash.\n\n## Wrapper output artifacts\n\nFor each external review invocation, store:\n\n```text\nreview-packet.<role>.json\nreviewer-report.<provider>-<role>.json\nreviewer-invocation.<provider>-<role>.json\n```\n\nWhen `normalization.preserve_raw_output: true` is explicitly selected for\nnon-sensitive output, also store:\n\n```text\nreviewer-report.<provider>-<role>.raw.json\n```\n\n## Non-goals\n\nThe external reviewer provider model does not introduce:\n\n- native Codex/Claude cross-agent messaging;\n- API-key billing;\n- a general agent runtime;\n- verification authority;\n- write-enabled external reviewers;\n- direct fusion without main-agent finding validation.\n\n## Relationship to review/fusion\n\nExternal provider reports enter the same pipeline as any other reviewer report:\n\n```text\nreview packet\n→ external reviewer wrapper\n→ candidate reviewer report\n→ main-agent finding validation\n→ fusion synthesis\n→ final decision support\n```\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "docs/human-interaction-protocol.md",
      "size_bytes": 10501,
      "content": "# Human Interaction Protocol\n\n## Status\n\nAccepted for v0.2.\n\n## Purpose\n\nAgentsFlow workflows may require human decisions. In v0.2 this is not a runtime\nor UI feature. It is a workflow protocol for the main/orchestrating agent.\n\nThe human explicitly starts a workflow. The main/orchestrating agent drives the\nworkflow phases, performs normal repository work, and may pause only at declared\nhuman decision points or blocking clarifications.\n\n## Core rule\n\n```text\nHuman interaction is mediated by the main/orchestrating agent.\nReview agents do not ask the human questions directly.\n```\n\n## Human-mediated gates\n\nA `human_mediated_gate` is a workflow control point where the human makes or\nconfirms the exit decision after the main/orchestrating agent has synthesized the\nrelevant evidence, reviewer outputs, options and consequences.\n\nIt is different from:\n\n```text\ndeterministic_gate\n  A runner evaluates declared checks/evidence and emits a gate report.\n\nreview_gate\n  Review agents emit candidate findings and the main agent validates relevance.\n```\n\nThe human does not manually fill YAML. The main/orchestrating agent asks the\ndecision question in dialogue, then records the normalized result in\n`human-decisions.yaml` and updates affected artifacts.\n\nFor example, a pre-red-capture plan decision may be modeled as:\n\n```text\ntechnical_plan -> deterministic plan_gate -> plan review reports\n-> main-agent synthesis -> human_mediated_gate decision -> red_capture\n```\n\nThe exact phase shape must be declared by the workflow or project binding. A\nphase-transition checker must not infer this gate from conversation alone.\n\nReview agents may produce:\n\n```text\n- candidate findings;\n- risks;\n- recommended gates;\n- decision options;\n- questions_for_human.\n```\n\nThe main/orchestrating agent synthesizes those inputs into a decision prompt,\nasks the human, records the answer, and resumes the workflow.\n\n## Workflow states\n\nUse these run states for human interaction:\n\n```text\nrunning\npaused_waiting_for_human\nresumed\ncompleted\nblocked\n```\n\n`paused_waiting_for_human` means the workflow is intentionally stopped at a\ndeclared decision point. The agent must not silently choose a confirmed human\nanswer while paused.\n\n## When the agent may ask\n\nThe main/orchestrating agent may ask the human only when:\n\n```text\n- required intake is missing or the invocation mode is unclear;\n- analysis is blocked by missing human-owned context;\n- a declared human-owned decision is required;\n- a material design fork affects scope, ADR alignment, risk posture, contract,\n  gate, review, evidence, authority or workflow-design for the active workflow;\n- final approval is required.\n```\n\nOtherwise the agent should continue autonomously: read files, run allowed\nscripts, structure inventory, launch read-only review agents, draft artifacts and\nrun validators.\n\n## Question artifact\n\nQuestions are recorded in `human-questions.yaml` under the workflow run directory.\nEach question is a decision prompt, not a request to fill YAML.\n\nRequired shape:\n\n```yaml\nversion: 1\nrun_id: \"YYYY-MM-DD-task-slug\"\nquestions:\n  - decision_id: review_policy.model_diversity\n    phase_id: operating_decisions_interview\n    status: open\n    classification: blocking-material\n    grouping: operating-decisions\n    question: Should review use different models or harnesses when available?\n    basis:\n      observed:\n        - Existing project has no external reviewer policy.\n      reviewer_recommendations:\n        - Verification reviewer recommends independent review for high-risk work.\n    options:\n      - id: same-model\n        label: Same model is allowed\n        impact: Lower setup complexity.\n      - id: diverse-models\n        label: Prefer different model/harness\n        impact: More independence; requires provider setup and review-prompt-contract reviewer assignments.\n      - id: unresolved\n        label: Leave unresolved\n        impact: Overlay remains draft-only.\n    default:\n      id: same-model\n      allowed: false\n    answer_required: true\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - project-operating-decisions.yaml\n      - .agentsflow/project.yaml\n```\n\n## Decision artifact\n\nHuman answers are recorded in `human-decisions.yaml` under the workflow run\ndirectory.\n\nRequired shape:\n\n```yaml\nversion: 1\nrun_id: \"YYYY-MM-DD-task-slug\"\ndecisions:\n  - decision_id: review_policy.model_diversity\n    phase_id: operating_decisions_interview\n    question_ref: review_policy.model_diversity\n    answer: diverse-models\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: \"Use model diversity for high-risk reviews.\"\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - project-operating-decisions.yaml\n```\n\nWhen `review_policy.model_diversity` is confirmed as `diverse-models`,\n`project-operating-decisions.yaml` records the provider strategy policy and each\nreview gate must encode concrete provider/model-family assignments in\n`review-prompt-contract.yaml`. The human decision selects the policy; the agent\nrecords the normalized run artifacts.\n\nAllowed decision statuses:\n\n```text\nconfirmed\ndefaulted\nunresolved\nrejected\nsuperseded\nexplicitly_deferred_with_constraints\n```\n\n`explicitly_deferred_with_constraints` requires a `deferral_constraints` object\nwith at least one stated constraint. It is the only allowed resume path for a\nblocking-material decision that is intentionally deferred instead of resolved.\n\n## Resume rule\n\nAfter the human answers, the main/orchestrating agent:\n\n```text\n1. records the answer in `human-decisions.yaml`;\n2. updates the target normalized artifact, if applicable;\n3. marks unresolved items explicitly when the answer is incomplete;\n4. resumes from the paused phase;\n5. does not re-run earlier phases unless the answer invalidates their inputs.\n```\n\n## Project initialization\n\n`project-initialization` declares these human-pause phases:\n\n```text\nread_project_intake\ndocumentation_disposition_decision\nlegacy_adoption_mode_decision\noperating_decisions_interview\ntarget_workflow_context_decision_packet\nhuman_approval\n```\n\nOnly `operating_decisions_interview` and `human_approval` are always human-owned\ndecision points for normal existing-project onboarding. `read_project_intake` and\n`legacy_adoption_mode_decision` are conditional: they pause when required context\nis missing or when legacy agent/process artifacts create human-owned decisions.\n`documentation_disposition_decision` is conditional for existing-project modes:\nit classifies current documentation and Markdown implementation history before\nlegacy adoption, target-workflow readiness, or overlay drafting. It must not\ndelete, rewrite or silently de-authorize existing documentation without explicit\nhuman approval. It must also ask the human to choose the documentation legacy\nadoption mode. The agent may recommend `preserve-as-is`,\n`knowledge-extraction`, `rewrite-migration` or `archive-delete`, but it must not\nselect the mode without human confirmation; this decision is not defaultable by\nthe agent. If the human accepts the agent's recommendation, the workflow records\nthat as an answered human decision. When `knowledge-extraction` is selected, the\nagent must also record the human-confirmed extraction depth as `light`,\n`standard` or `deep`.\n`target_workflow_context_decision_packet` is conditional for `prepare-workflow`:\nit captures missing target-workflow gate, review, evidence or authority context,\nplus material scope, ADR, risk-surface, Failure Path Matrix, contract, gate,\nreview, evidence, authority or workflow-design forks discovered during\ntarget-workflow preparation, as a run-level decision packet. It does not\nnormalize those answers into\n`project-operating-decisions.yaml` unless the human explicitly chooses onboarding\nor persistent policy activation.\n\n### Human-mediated design decision checkpoints\n\nA material design fork is a human-mediated checkpoint, not an informal side\nconversation and not a review-agent gate. The main/orchestrating agent pauses,\ngroups the decision options, records questions in `human-questions.yaml`, records\nanswers in `human-decisions.yaml`, updates the affected run artifacts, and then\nresumes the workflow.\n\nThe checkpoint may exit only when:\n\n```text\n- blocking-material decisions are confirmed or explicitly deferred with stated constraints;\n- decision packet and preflight run artifacts are updated;\n- unresolved nonblocking questions are recorded as defaults, limitations or follow-ups;\n- no unresolved design decision blocks the next workflow gate.\n```\n\nTarget workflow binding/readiness handoff artifacts are drafted only after\n`target_workflow_readiness_gate` accepts the operating context.\n\n## Big-feature contract-first\n\n`big-feature-contract-first` may pause for the human only when the main agent\nrecords one of these conditions:\n\n```text\nunresolved blocking-material question\nmaterial design fork affecting scope, ADR alignment, risk posture, contract,\ngate, review, evidence or authority\nscope or task-contract amendment\naccepted decision or ADR conflict\nexhausted review cycles\nfinal human acceptance required by project policy\n```\n\nOpen questions in `task.contract.md` are classified as:\n\n```text\nblocking-material\nnonblocking-follow-up\nnonblocking-known-limitation\nout-of-scope\n```\n\nOnly `blocking-material` questions pause the workflow by default. Nonblocking\nquestions must show the proposed default or follow-up handling in the grouped\ndecision packet, and unanswered nonblocking questions are recorded as defaulted,\nknown limitations or follow-ups rather than silently disappearing.\n\nThe grouped decision packet is still a dialogue with the agent, not a request for\nthe human to edit YAML. The main agent asks the grouped questions, records\nanswers in `human-decisions.yaml`, updates the task contract or run artifacts,\nand resumes from the paused phase.\n\nBefore `big-feature-contract-first` exits `contract_acceptance`, the grouped\ndecision packet must be structured as a design decision review. The main agent\nfirst presents the open decision inventory, then reviews each blocking or\nmaterial decision with options, tradeoffs, recommended path, rationale and the\nexact human acceptance question. Red-capture may start only after blocking\ndecisions are accepted, changed, or explicitly deferred with residual risk in\n`human-decisions.yaml`.\n\n## Non-goals\n\nThis protocol does not introduce:\n\n```text\n- a UI;\n- a database;\n- a long-running service;\n- automatic reminders;\n- autonomous monitoring;\n- review-agent-to-human conversations.\n```\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "docs/mvp-ready-workflow-standard.md",
      "size_bytes": 15248,
      "content": "# MVP-Ready Workflow Standard\n\n## Status\n\nAccepted for AgentsFlow v0.2 and updated in v0.1.13.\n\n## v0.2 MVP scope\n\nAgentsFlow v0.2 has one application/onboarding workflow, one supported target\nworkflow and utility workflows for review/fusion and PR merge readiness.\n\nApplication workflow:\n\n```text\nproject-initialization\n```\n\nMVP supported target workflow:\n\n```text\nbig-feature-contract-first\n```\n\nv0.2 utility workflows:\n\n```text\nreview-only-fusion\npr-merge-readiness\n```\n\nNon-MVP workflows remain in the repository as reference/experimental workflows and must remain schema-valid only:\n\n```text\nagentic-system-hardening\nbugfix-regression-capture\nnew-project-spec-first\nprompt-behavior-eval\nsafe-refactor\nresearch-to-ADR\n```\n\nThe supported v0.2 end-to-end path is:\n\n```text\nproject-initialization.prepare-workflow -> big-feature-contract-first\n```\n\n## MVP-ready definition\n\nA workflow is MVP-ready when it is not just a prose idea but a reproducible workflow definition usable by a main/orchestrating agent and a human.\n\nThe MVP supported target workflow must have:\n\n- validated `workflow.yaml`;\n- explicit phase sequence;\n- declared inputs and outputs;\n- declared artifact templates;\n- referenced upstream gate contracts;\n- deterministic gate runner interface and project-binding requirements;\n- review-cycle policy where applicable;\n- behavior bindings for required acceptance scenarios where applicable;\n- selected risk surfaces, required path classes and Failure Path Matrix coverage\n  where the task contract selects control, persistence, failure or authority\n  risks;\n- at least one validated example or project-overlay example;\n- repo-validation coverage;\n- for workflows with an implementation phase, a red-capture (failing-test) phase before implementation and a green-verify phase after it (ADR-0017).\n\nGuarantee strength must follow `docs/enforcement-boundary.md`: do not describe a\nworkflow property as script-enforced unless a validator or test actually checks it.\n\n## v0.2 Definition of Done\n\nAgentsFlow v0.2 is done when:\n\n- repository validation passes;\n- tests pass;\n- the MVP supported target workflow is schema-valid;\n- project-initialization path is coherent;\n- project overlay model is represented;\n- project-bound gates are represented;\n- behavior bindings are represented;\n- legacy adoption is represented;\n- Claude Code external reviewer provider minimally works in subscription-local mode;\n- one end-to-end example exists under `examples/e2e/minimal-python-project/`;\n- `AGENTS.md` is usable by coding agents;\n- non-MVP workflows remain reference/experimental and schema-valid only.\n\nThe project overlay model uses one canonical v0.2 shape: flat\n`.agentsflow/project.yaml`, structured `.agentsflow/workflows/*.binding.yaml`,\nand upstream pinning in `.agentsflow/agentsflow.lock.yaml`.\n\nHuman interaction in v0.2 is main-agent mediated. Workflows that need human\ndecisions declare pause-capable phases and record questions/answers as run\nartifacts; review agents do not question humans directly.\n\nWhen a workflow says \"gate\", it must not hide who has authority. Deterministic\ngates are runner-decided, review gates are reviewer-output plus relevance\nvalidation, and human-mediated gates require main-agent synthesis plus a\nrecorded human decision.\n\nWorkflow runs may use `phase_guard` in `workflow-run.yaml` as a lightweight\nphase pointer. The guard records the current phase, allowed next phases and\nallowed outputs. It is deliberately smaller than a workflow runtime: v0.2\nvalidators check the declared run artifacts against the current phase's allowed\noutputs, with draft artifacts accepted only in draft-labeled top-level artifact\nslots, so future-phase artifacts are not silently promoted during project\ninitialization or other phased workflows.\n\n## DoD interpretation\n\nSoft DoD terms are interpreted as operational checks:\n\n- `represented` means Doc + Instance + Wired: a model document in `docs/`, a\n  concrete artifact instance (schema + template + at least one `examples/`\n  instance), and a reference from at least one MVP `workflow.yaml` or project\n  binding.\n- `project-initialization path is coherent` means every\n  `workflows/project-initialization/workflow.yaml` output has a corresponding\n  template, concrete example, or explicit deferred/optional status.\n- `Claude Code external reviewer provider minimally works` means the automated\n  mock smoke test passes through the project-bound wrapper without a live Claude\n  call. This is the CI-safe MVP check.\n- PR merge readiness may require additional operator evidence from a live\n  subscription-local Claude Code review gate. That evidence should be recorded as\n  a readiness artifact, not as a mandatory repository test, because it depends on\n  local Claude subscription auth and an escalated Codex sandbox.\n- `AGENTS.md is usable by coding agents` means it contains source-of-truth order,\n  explicit v0.2 scope, scope-expansion prohibition, validation commands, and\n  accepted-decision pointers.\n\n## Primary e2e example\n\nThe primary v0.2 end-to-end example is:\n\n```text\nexamples/e2e/minimal-python-project/\n```\n\nCMake FetchContent / FetchPackage-style usage is documented as a pattern/skeleton only in v0.2. A complete CMake e2e example is not part of the MVP.\n\n## Claude external reviewer provider requirement\n\nThe Claude provider is in v0.2 MVP scope, but narrowly:\n\n- Claude Code CLI only;\n- subscription-local only;\n- API-key usage forbidden;\n- configured Claude API/proxy environment variables must fail fast;\n- project-bound wrapper only;\n- review packet in, normalized reviewer report out;\n- invocation metadata stored, with raw output stored only when explicitly\n  non-sensitive;\n- Codex-launched live runs use escalated sandbox access for subscription-local\n  auth. Stdin packet transport disables Claude Code tools with `--tools \"\"`;\n  file prompt transport may use only `--tools Read` for the generated prompt\n  file in an isolated temporary directory;\n- provider/model diversity is proved through assignment, invocation-set and\n  reviewer-report evidence, not by role names alone;\n- findings remain candidate/unvalidated.\n\n## Release / PR readiness note\n\nv0.2 MVP readiness and PR merge readiness are related but not identical. MVP\nreadiness is the repository baseline above: schemas, examples, validators, tests\nand CI-safe smoke checks. PR readiness is a concrete branch decision and should\nadd branch-scoped evidence such as:\n\n- clean worktree and intended commit range;\n- repository validation and tests;\n- source-of-truth documentation consistency check;\n- review gate evidence, including live external reviewer evidence when available;\n- relevance validation for P0/P1 candidate findings;\n- human merge decision and post-merge verification plan.\n\nThis repository defines `pr-merge-readiness` as a dedicated utility workflow for\nthat branch-scoped decision. It stays small and composes existing validation,\nreview, finding-validation and human-decision artifacts instead of becoming a\ngeneral release-management runtime.\n\n## Workflow-specific notes\n\n### project-initialization\n\nMandatory application workflow with mode-gated continuations:\n\n```text\nintake with intent_mode -> raw scan -> structured inventory -> domain identification\n-> schema-bound triad expert assessment -> documentation disposition for existing-project modes\n-> mode-specific exit or continuation\n```\n\nExpert assessment role reports are strict JSON artifacts bound to\n`schemas/project-assessment.schema.json`. Synthesis is not allowed until all\nrequired role reports are schema-valid; Markdown or prose-only role output is\nrejected, rerun or paused instead of normalized as authoritative evidence.\n\nMode-specific continuations:\n\n```text\nunknown-discovery\n  stop after scan, inventory, assessments and questions unless the human asks to continue\n\nrisk-domain-assessment\n  stop after domain/risk assessment and domain-expertise questions unless the human asks to continue\n\nadoption-onboarding\n  documentation disposition with explicit human-confirmed documentation adoption mode\n  -> legacy adoption decision/migration plan when legacy artifacts are in scope\n  -> human operating-decisions interview -> draft overlay/bindings/gates\n  -> validate draft overlay -> initialization review -> finding validation\n  -> initialization report -> human approval\n\nprepare-workflow\n  require target_workflow -> documentation disposition with explicit human-confirmed documentation adoption mode\n  -> confirm sufficient target workflow operating context\n  -> capture missing target workflow decisions or material design forks as a run-level decision packet\n  -> target workflow readiness gate\n  -> draft target workflow binding/readiness handoff only when ready\n  -> initialization review -> finding validation\n\nlegacy-cleanup\n  documentation disposition with explicit human-confirmed documentation adoption mode -> legacy adoption decision\n  -> migration/quarantine plan -> draft active instruction map\n  -> human approval before activation\n```\n\nOverlay drafting, validation and approval are not universal initialization\noutputs. They apply to adoption-onboarding, legacy activation, or\nprepare-workflow binding/policy activation. `prepare-workflow` requires\n`target_workflow: big-feature-contract-first` and enough operating context for\nthat workflow: project or draft binding, gate policy, review policy, evidence\nlocation and human-owned decisions. Material design forks discovered while\npreparing that target workflow are handled as human-mediated checkpoints and\nrecorded in the same run-level decision packet before readiness validation.\nUnresolved blocking-material forks block readiness unless explicitly deferred\nwith stated constraints.\n\n### big-feature-contract-first\n\nReference end-to-end development workflow:\n\n```text\nintake -> operating context preflight -> repository grounding -> contract\n-> behavior bindings -> plan gate when effective strictness requires it\n-> contract acceptance as a human-mediated design decision review\n-> red capture (contract scenarios as executable tests, failing run)\n-> implementation -> verification gate (green re-run) -> review\n-> fusion -> finding validation -> final decision\n```\n\n`big-feature-contract-first` declares a workflow default for its normal depth.\nProject bindings inherit that default unless they explicitly record a lighter or\nheavier strictness override with a reason.\n\nIf a project requires agentic review of the plan followed by human approval\nbefore red capture, model it explicitly as a human-mediated gate or a declared\nproject-bound policy. Do not treat that step as an implicit side effect of\n`plan_gate`.\n\nThe built-in `contract_acceptance` phase is a human-mediated design decision\nreview, not a blanket approval prompt. The main agent presents an open decision\ninventory, then discusses each blocking or material decision with options,\ntradeoffs, recommended path, rationale and the exact acceptance question.\nBlocking decisions must be accepted, changed or explicitly deferred with\nresidual risk before red capture starts.\n\nOpen questions are classified in the task contract. Unresolved\n`blocking-material` questions pause the workflow; nonblocking questions are\nrecorded as defaults, limitations or follow-up items. Post-review fixes are\nclassified as material or non-material before deciding whether to rerun review.\n\nThe contract phase selects the feature-specific risk surfaces from\n`docs/risk-and-strictness.md` or the project overlay. When selected surfaces have\nfailure, denial, timeout, persistence or authority semantics, the contract must\ninclude a Failure Path Matrix before red capture starts. Behavior bindings and\ngate evidence classify checks with the corresponding risk surface and path\nclass. A selected path class may be deferred only with explicit residual-risk\nlanguage and human approval when required by project policy.\n\nReview topology is derived from the task's selected risk surfaces and recorded\nexplicitly. The default remains `homogeneous-dual`; focused or heterogeneous\nreview is used only when the risk profile justifies it and reviewer roles resolve\nto existing role contracts.\n\nVerification and review evidence must be fresh for the latest material change.\nChanges to the task contract, selected risk surfaces, Failure Path Matrix,\nbehavior bindings, gate/review policy, mandatory evidence or affected\nimplementation behavior invalidate prior evidence for the changed scope. The run\nrecords the material change and refreshes green verification and review according\nto the review-cycle policy.\n\nSupplemental human-requested review is allowed after green evidence, but its\nfindings remain candidate findings. It reopens the fix loop only for\nmain-agent-validated P0/P1 findings or mandatory evidence gaps; otherwise it is\nrecorded without forcing another primary review cycle.\n\n### bugfix-regression-capture\n\nReference/next workflow in v0.2. It remains schema-valid, but is not a supported\n`prepare-workflow.target_workflow` and is not part of the v0.2 end-to-end pilot.\n\nLightweight bugfix shape:\n\n```text\nbug intake -> reproduction/diagnosis -> regression scenario -> minimal fix plan\n-> implementation -> regression verification gate -> evidence -> review\n-> finding validation -> final decision\n```\n\nDefault rule:\n\n```text\nNo fix without captured regression unless the workflow explicitly records why reproduction is impossible.\n```\n\nThis reproduce-before-fix rule is the bugfix instance of the test-framed\nimplementation discipline (ADR-0017): the failing (red) run is captured before the\nfix, and the regression gate confirms the green re-run after.\n\n### review-only-fusion\n\nv0.2 utility workflow. It supports review-control modeling and can be invoked as\na utility pattern, but is not a supported `prepare-workflow.target_workflow`.\n\nReview utility shape:\n\n```text\nexisting artifact/evidence -> evidence availability gate -> independent read-only reviews\n-> fusion -> finding validation -> final decision support\n```\n\nIt does not run implementation checks itself. If implementation evidence is required but missing, it returns `needs-verification-evidence`.\n\n### new-project-spec-first\n\nReference/next workflow in v0.2. It remains schema-valid, but is not a supported\n`prepare-workflow.target_workflow`. The v0.2 pilot targets an existing project\nthrough `project-initialization.prepare-workflow -> big-feature-contract-first`,\nnot greenfield project specification.\n\nSpecification workflow shape:\n\n```text\nproblem framing -> target system spec -> enabling system spec -> term map\n-> research brief -> options/decision contracts -> architecture sketch\n-> initial contracts -> roadmap -> spec review gate -> spec review\n-> finding validation -> final specification package\n```\n\n## Language and artifact policy\n\nRepository docs are primarily English for portability across coding agents and future users. Russian handoff prompts are allowed outside the repository or in clearly marked handoff artifacts.\n\nWorkflow run artifacts live under:\n\n```text\nDocs/agentsflow/runs/\n```\n\nReports and summaries are committed to the repository. Heavy raw logs are optional and may be gitignored.\n\nSchemas are strict for core artifacts and more permissive for exploratory assessments.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "docs/pr-merge-readiness.md",
      "size_bytes": 5612,
      "content": "# PR Merge Readiness\n\n`pr-merge-readiness` is a v0.2 utility workflow for deciding whether a branch is\nready to open, accept or merge as a pull request.\n\nIt is not a release platform and does not mutate Git hosts. It composes existing\nAgentsFlow evidence, review, fusion, finding-validation and human-decision\nartifacts into a branch-scoped readiness report.\n\n## Scope\n\nThe workflow records:\n\n- branch, base branch, commit range and worktree state;\n- the readiness run id and current `material_change_id`;\n- deterministic check evidence, such as repository validation and tests;\n- review packet and reviewer report references;\n- external reviewer evidence, including live-vs-mock Claude distinction;\n- finding validation and collision-control evidence for rejected or downgraded\n  P0/P1 candidate findings;\n- stale review detection when material changes postdate review packets;\n- human merge decision status.\n\nAccepted merge-ready status requires a recorded human decision. Green checks and\nreview evidence alone produce `awaiting_human_decision`, not acceptance.\nAn accepted human decision must point to a human-authored decision record with a\nmatching `run_id`, `decision_id`, `answered_by: human`, `status: confirmed` and\naccepted answer. For `merge.acceptance`, the decision record must also bind the\naccepted artifact by `material_change_id` and the exact readiness report\n`report_hash`. An in-band `{status: accepted}` field is not sufficient.\n\nThe readiness evaluator treats these surfaces as blocking:\n\n- any deterministic check whose `status` is not `pass`;\n- any review whose `status` is not `pass` or `pass-with-notes`;\n- malformed or stale review timestamps;\n- review packets whose `run_id` or `material_change_id` do not match the\n  evaluated readiness report;\n- omitted provider-mirrored target review topology entries;\n- missing live Claude invocation evidence for a Claude-backed review;\n- mock or failed external reviewer invocation metadata when live Claude evidence\n  is required;\n- live Claude invocation hashes that do not match the current packet, prompt,\n  role contract, prompt contract, rubric, output schema or normalized report;\n- accepted or `needs-more-evidence` P0/P1 candidate findings;\n- P0/P1 source findings represented as lower-severity or duplicate candidate\n  findings without blocker-grade handling;\n- rejected or downgraded P0/P1 candidate findings without completed\n  collision-control evidence from two control reviewers that explicitly address\n  the same collision batch and disputed finding, support the orchestrator\n  disposition, and complete after the collision-control prompt is prepared;\n- sensitive raw external output without redaction or non-sensitive declaration;\n- required live external evidence whose raw output is declared `not_persisted`\n  instead of non-sensitive raw output or a redacted/summary/pointer artifact;\n- live Claude invocation metadata whose recorded raw output path or hash does\n  not match the local run evidence when raw output is explicitly persisted as\n  non-sensitive;\n- live Claude invocation or reviewer-report normalization metadata that records\n  a raw output path while the readiness report declares redacted, summary or\n  pointer persistence;\n- redacted, summary or pointer raw-output evidence without a concrete artifact\n  path and hash;\n- self-application reports that claim the bootstrap run proves itself.\n\n## Review Model\n\nThe target review topology is `heterogeneous-variable` with provider-mirrored\ntopic pairs:\n\n| Topic | Role | Providers |\n|---|---|---|\n| `verification-evidence` | `verification` | `internal-agent`, `claude-code` |\n| `architecture-process` | `architecture` | `internal-agent`, `claude-code` |\n| `adversarial-authority` | `adversarial` | `internal-agent`, `claude-code` |\n\nThe mirrored pair shape is a target workflow policy. A workflow used to develop\n`pr-merge-readiness` may choose a smaller development review gate when its own\ncontract records that decision.\n\n## Report Validation\n\nReadiness reports use:\n\n```text\nschemas/pr-merge-readiness-report.schema.json\ntemplates/pr-merge-readiness-report.json\n```\n\nThe deterministic repository validator checks example readiness reports through\n`scripts/repo_validation/pr_merge_readiness.py`.\n\nThe human merge decision is content-bound, not only path-bound. The\n`human-decisions.yaml#merge.acceptance` record must reference the evaluated\nreadiness report, the current `material_change_id` and the report's SHA-256 hash\nusing `report_hash: sha256:<64-hex-digest>`.\n\nEach review packet must be anchored to the evaluated readiness report: packet\n`run_id` must match report `run_id`, and packet `material_change_id` must match\nreport `material_change_id`. Internal reviewer reports must also carry matching\n`review_context` for the same run, material change, packet path and reviewer id.\n\nThe evaluator is intentionally small. It checks declared artifacts and computes\nthe readiness state from the report. It validates external reviewer invocation\nmetadata shape for live Claude evidence, but it does not run CI, launch\nreviewers, call GitHub/GitLab or perform merges.\n\nReports marked with `fixture.not_real_readiness_evidence: true` are schema and\nevaluator fixtures only. The evaluator returns `incomplete` for them rather than\nreal merge readiness.\n\n## Self-Application Boundary\n\nWhen AgentsFlow applies this workflow to itself, the report must not claim\ncyclic self-proof. A bootstrap run may produce and validate the workflow, but the\nfirst real use of `pr-merge-readiness` is a later acceptance or merge readiness\nrun over an already implemented slice.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "docs/review-fusion-model.md",
      "size_bytes": 7780,
      "content": "# Review and Fusion Model\n\n## Goal\n\nMulti-model review and fusion reduce correlated blind spots in agent-assisted development.\n\nThey are not a replacement for deterministic checks or explicit contracts.\n\n\n## Execution order\n\nReview and fusion run after the verification gate.\n\n```text\nimplementation/artifact\n  ↓\nverification gate: runs tests, scripts, checks, and evidence validation\n  ↓\nread-only review agents: inspect gate artifacts and classify issues\n  ↓\nfusion: synthesizes reviewer reports and preserves candidate blockers\n  ↓\nmain-agent relevance validation: accepts/rejects/escalates findings\n```\n\nReview agents are read-only. They must not run tests, execute scripts, modify files,\nor generate patches. If a reviewer believes more checks are needed, it reports\n`needs-additional-verification`; the workflow or human decides whether to re-run\nthe verification gate. Reviewer findings are candidate findings, not authoritative\ntruth; the main/orchestrating agent must validate relevance before treating them\nas accepted issues.\n\nSee `docs/review-control-model.md` for the shared control model and `docs/review-agent-interaction-protocol.md` for blocking definitions, loop exit criteria, and the relevance-validation decision matrix.\n\n## Fusion is optional and workflow-selected\n\nFusion is not the global default. A workflow/profile may select no review,\n`homogeneous-dual`, `homogeneous-plus-focused`, `heterogeneous-variable`, or the\nnon-primary `collision-control` exception. The topology is configuration, not\nphilosophy.\n\nIf review is enabled as a primary review gate, the minimum reviewer count is two.\nCollision-control review is also batched through two reviewers: when one or more\nblocker-level candidate findings are rejected or downgraded by the\nmain/orchestrating agent in the same review cycle, the workflow creates one\ncollision batch and sends that focused packet to two fresh-context control\nreviewers.\n\n## Review topology\n\nReview topology is metadata selected by a workflow/profile.\n\nExamples:\n\n- `none`\n- `homogeneous-dual`\n- `homogeneous-plus-focused`\n- `heterogeneous-variable`\n- `collision-control` for rejected or downgraded blocker collision batches only, not as a primary gate\n\nReviewers start from fresh zero conversation context. They must not receive a\nforked main-agent/orchestrator conversation. Their input is the review packet and\nreferenced artifacts declared by the workflow or project binding.\n\n## Reviewer roles\n\n### Architecture reviewer\n\nChecks:\n\n- ADR consistency;\n- modularity;\n- boundaries;\n- architecture drift;\n- hidden coupling;\n- overengineering.\n\n### Verification reviewer\n\nChecks:\n\n- test adequacy;\n- impact map;\n- evidence quality;\n- weakened tests;\n- missing regression cases.\n\n### Adversarial reviewer\n\nChecks:\n\n- scope creep;\n- ambiguous requirements;\n- hidden failure modes;\n- prompt/policy bypass;\n- ungrounded claims;\n- false completion.\n\n## Fusion rules\n\nFusion is decision support, not final truth. It preserves reviewer findings and\ndisagreements, but the main/orchestrating agent must validate finding relevance\nbefore accepted changes are required.\n\nFusion receives:\n\n- task contract;\n- diff or artifact;\n- test/script results;\n- evidence report;\n- independent reviewer reports.\n- prior finding validation reports when this is a later review cycle.\n\nFusion outputs:\n\n- consensus points;\n- disagreements;\n- candidate blocking issues;\n- non-blocking issues;\n- required changes;\n- human-decision items.\n\n## Reusable finding-control pipeline\n\nReview/fusion/finding-validation is a reusable gate-control block. Workflows\nselect the review topology and provide the contract, evidence, risk surfaces,\nFailure Path Matrix and reviewer assignments; they should not duplicate the\nfinding-control procedure locally.\n\nAfter reviewer reports arrive, the shared pipeline is:\n\n1. Mechanical intake: check that expected reviewer reports exist, are\n   schema-valid, fresh for the latest material change and match the declared\n   reviewer assignment, provider, model family, role and topic where applicable.\n2. Canonical finding extraction: preserve each source finding and add normalized\n   triage metadata such as provider, model, topic, role, severity, evidence\n   references, risk surface and FPM row when available.\n3. Duplicate / related / conflict grouping: group true duplicates without\n   losing max severity, mark related findings that share an area but differ in\n   mechanism, and preserve conflicts as disagreements requiring validation.\n4. Topic-pair comparison: when a topology uses mirrored topics or providers,\n   compare findings inside the same topic pair before drawing cross-topic\n   conclusions.\n5. Fusion report: surface consensus, disagreements, candidate blockers,\n   mandatory evidence gaps and validation priorities.\n6. Main-agent relevance validation: accept, reject, downgrade, mark duplicate,\n   request more evidence or escalate findings against contract, diff/artifact,\n   evidence, accepted decisions, scope and non-goals.\n7. Collision-control: if the orchestrator rejects or downgrades P0/P1 candidate\n   findings, batch those collisions and send them to two fresh-context control\n   reviewers before final triage.\n8. Review-cycle decision: exit only when there are no validated blockers, no\n   unresolved P0/P1 candidates and no mandatory evidence gaps.\n\n## Authority boundary\n\nThis reusable block must not confuse automatic gates with human-in-the-loop\nauthority. Deterministic automation may validate report structure, schema,\nfreshness and declared evidence references. Fusion provides decision support.\nThe main/orchestrating agent validates candidate findings. Human-mediated gates\nremain human-owned and require normalized human decisions before the workflow\nclaims human acceptance or merge readiness.\n\n## Non-negotiable rule\n\nFusion must not erase a P0/P1 candidate issue by majority vote.\n\nIf one reviewer finds a plausible blocking issue, fusion must surface it explicitly.\nThe main/orchestrating agent may later reject or downgrade it only with a recorded\nrelevance-validation reason.\n\n\n## Review cycle exit\n\nFusion may recommend a cycle decision, but the default AgentsFlow exit criterion is\nvalidated by the main/orchestrating agent:\n\n```text\nno_validated_blocking_findings\n```\n\nRepeated review agents are not rerun when all P0/P1 candidate findings have been\nvalidated and no validated blockers or mandatory evidence gaps remain. P2/P3/NOTE\nfindings can become follow-up work without forcing another review cycle.\n\n## Result states\n\n- `pass`\n- `pass-with-notes`\n- `needs-changes`\n- `needs-verification-evidence`\n- `human-decision-required`\n- `blocked`\n\n## Design note\n\nIn early versions, fusion can be prompt/manual. Automation should come after report formats stabilize.\n\n## Fusion agent semantics\n\nIn AgentsFlow, Fusion Agent is a read-only synthesis actor, not a default\norchestrator. It consumes already-produced artifacts and reports:\n\n- schema-bound reviewer-report JSON artifacts;\n- verification gate reports;\n- evidence bundles;\n- workflow context and accepted decisions.\n- prior finding validation reports when available from earlier cycles.\n\nIt produces decision support: consensus, disagreement, blocker summary,\nnon-blocking concerns, and human-decision items.\n\nMarkdown reviewer summaries or raw reviewer transcripts are optional sidecars.\nFusion may reference them for orientation or traceability, but normalized JSON\nreviewer-report artifacts are the source reports consumed by gates and finding\nfusion.\n\nBy default, Fusion Agent must not launch additional reviewers, run verification\ngates, run tests, call tools, or modify artifacts. It may recommend additional\nreview or verification, but the main/orchestrating agent owns the actual workflow\norchestration.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "docs/workflow-model.md",
      "size_bytes": 9775,
      "content": "# Workflow Model\n\n## Definition\n\nA workflow is a named, reusable process for a class of agent-assisted development work.\n\nA workflow answers:\n\n- What is the goal of this work?\n- What artifacts are expected?\n- Which skills are invoked?\n- Which scripts are mandatory or optional?\n- Which templates should be used?\n- Which domain packs can parameterize it?\n- What default strictness and supported override values apply?\n- Which review topologies make sense?\n- Which risk surfaces can change gate, evidence or review depth?\n\n## Workflow is the primary abstraction\n\nStrictness is not the primary abstraction. A workflow owns its normal depth through\n`default_strictness`; a project binding or run may explicitly override it only\nwith a reason.\n\nExample:\n\n```yaml\nworkflow: prompt-behavior-eval\ndomain_pack: coding-agent\ndefault_strictness: L3\neffective_strictness: L3\nreview_topology: heterogeneous-variable\n```\n\nThe workflow defines the type of work and its baseline gate/review/evidence\ndepth. The effective strictness controls conditional gates for a concrete\nbinding or run. Humans should not be asked to choose an `L*` value by default;\nthe main agent should inherit the workflow default and ask only when project risk\nor task constraints justify an override.\n\nThe number of strictness labels is not a goal. v0.2 remains compatible with the\ncurrent `L*` identifiers, but the model should also support a smaller future\ntaxonomy such as standard/elevated/critical without changing the workflow\nboundary.\n\nWhen a workflow uses the term \"gate\", it should be clear which authority mode is\nintended:\n\n```text\ndeterministic_gate\n  A project-bound runner decides from declared checks/evidence.\n\nreview_gate\n  Reviewer reports are relevance-validated before the workflow exits.\n\nhuman_mediated_gate\n  The main agent presents synthesized evidence/options and records the human\n  decision before proceeding.\n```\n\nThe distinction matters most around planning. A `plan_gate` can be a\ndeterministic check that the plan packet is grounded and complete; an agentic\nreview of that plan followed by human approval is a separate\n`human_mediated_gate` unless the workflow or project binding explicitly models\nit as part of the plan gate's required evidence and decision policy.\n\nWhen a human-mediated gate asks for design acceptance before implementation or\nred capture, the main agent should not ask for blanket approval of a whole\npacket. It should first present an open decision inventory, then discuss each\nblocking or material decision with options, tradeoffs, a recommended path,\nrationale and a concrete acceptance question before recording the normalized\nhuman decision.\n\n## Workflow manifest shape\n\nSee `schemas/workflow.schema.json` and `templates/workflow.yaml`.\n\nMinimal fields:\n\n```yaml\nname: big-feature-contract-first\nversion: 0.1\nintent: \"Design and implement a large feature through a contract-first process.\"\ndefault_strictness: L3\nentry_criteria: []\noutputs: []\nuses:\n  skills: []\n  scripts: []\n  templates: []\nsupported_profiles:\n  strictness: []\n  review_topologies: []\n```\n\nA phase sequence carries one structural constraint: any phase of `kind:\nimplementation` must be preceded by a red-capture (failing-test) phase and followed\nby a green-verify phase; `validate_repo.py` enforces this workflow topology with\n`test_framing` markers (ADR-0017). Refactor-only workflows may use\n`baseline_capture` before `change_type: refactor`, because the pre-change\nbehavior-preservation baseline is expected to pass rather than fail.\n\nWorkflow phases may also declare `human_interaction`. This does not create a\nruntime; it tells the main/orchestrating agent when a workflow run may enter\n`paused_waiting_for_human`, which question/decision artifacts to update, and which\nresume states are allowed. See `docs/human-interaction-protocol.md`.\n\nWorkflow runs may declare a lightweight `phase_guard` in `workflow-run.yaml`.\nThis is not a workflow engine. It is a run-state pointer for the\nmain/orchestrating agent:\n\n- `current_phase`;\n- `completed_phases`;\n- `allowed_next_phases`;\n- `allowed_outputs`;\n- `draft_artifacts`;\n- `forbidden_outputs_until_phase_exit`;\n- `blocked` / `blocked_by`.\n\nWhen `phase_guard` is present, repository validation checks declared run\nartifacts against the current phase's allowed outputs. `draft_artifacts` may\nappear only in draft-labeled top-level `artifacts` slots; evidence, output and\nreport ledger fields require `allowed_outputs`. This prevents an agent from\nmaking a future-phase artifact look authoritative before the current phase\nexits. The guard is intentionally protocol-level in v0.2: it validates the run\nartifact ledger, not every file that may exist on disk.\n\n## Risk surfaces across workflow levels\n\nRisk surfaces are defined in `docs/risk-and-strictness.md` and flow through the\nsame three-level model as other workflow policy:\n\n```text\nWorkflow Definition\n  May name common surfaces that usually matter for this workflow class.\n\nProject Binding / Overlay\n  Selects project-default surfaces, project-local surfaces, required path\n  classes, review escalation rules and evidence storage/freshness policy.\n\nWorkflow Run\n  Selects the concrete feature surfaces, records a Failure Path Matrix, binds\n  path classes to checks/evidence, and invalidates stale evidence after material\n  changes.\n```\n\nUpstream workflows must not hard-code project-specific commands or local risk\nsurface names. They may require that a project or run declare the selected\nsurfaces and bind them to project-level gates.\n\nEvidence freshness is run-scoped. A workflow run should record the latest\n`material_change_id`, the green verification evidence produced after that change,\nand the review packet prepared from that evidence.
