Review/fusion evidence before\nthe latest material change is not authoritative for the changed scope.\n\n## Current workflows\n\n| Workflow | v0.2 status | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| `big-feature-contract-first` | supported target | Implement a large feature through contract, BDD scenarios, impact map, verification, evidence. |\n| `review-only-fusion` | utility | Run independent review and fusion on an existing artifact or diff. |\n| `pr-merge-readiness` | utility | Decide whether a branch has enough validation, review, finding-validation and human approval evidence to open, accept or merge as a PR. |\n| `new-project-spec-first` | reference/next | Start a new project with problem framing, specs, ADR seeds, and initial contracts. |\n| `bugfix-regression-capture` | reference/next | Reproduce a bug, fix it, and convert it into a regression scenario. |\n| `agentic-system-hardening` | reference/experimental | Harden agent systems: prompts, tools, memory, context, policy, model router, traces. |\n| `prompt-behavior-eval` | reference/experimental | Evaluate changes to prompts/skills/instructions using behavioral scenarios. |\n| `safe-refactor` | reference/experimental | Perform scoped refactoring with boundary and regression protection. |\n| `research-to-ADR` | reference/experimental | Turn research into decision memos and ADRs. |\n\n## Candidate future workflows\n\nThe following are known candidate workflows, but they are not promoted into the\nv0.2 supported path:\n\n| Candidate workflow | Candidate status | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| `knowledge-extraction` | candidate/next | Extract normalized project knowledge from a project documentation corpus or knowledge base with provenance, confidence, human-confirmation boundaries and persistence scope. |\n\nCurrent `project-initialization` may already produce\n`project-knowledge-extraction.md` when the human selects documentation\n`knowledge-extraction`. That run-level artifact is not the same thing as a\nstandalone `knowledge-extraction` workflow. A future workflow should reuse the\nsame documentation-disposition and human-decision rules rather than creating a\nparallel abstraction.\n\nSee `docs/plans/v0.2-next-slices.md` for the current high-level sequencing note\ncovering release/PR readiness, a fresh Bro Tools Gateway TG-A dogfood run, and a\nfuture standalone knowledge-extraction workflow.\n\n## Workflow selection guidance\n\nFor v0.2 `prepare-workflow`, only `big-feature-contract-first` is a supported\ntarget workflow. Other workflows are utility, reference or experimental unless a\nfuture accepted decision promotes them into the supported target set.\n\nUse `new-project-spec-first` when the main risk is ambiguous initial direction.\n\nUse `big-feature-contract-first` when the main risk is scope creep or architecture drift during implementation.\n\nUse `agentic-system-hardening` when the main risk is runtime behavior of agents, tools, memory, or policy.\n\nUse `prompt-behavior-eval` when the main artifact is a prompt, instruction set, or skill behavior.\n\nUse `safe-refactor` when behavior should remain stable and scope boundaries matter.\n\nUse `bugfix-regression-capture` when a known failure must become a permanent scenario.\n\nUse `research-to-ADR` when implementation should not start until alternatives and decisions are explicit.\n\nUse `review-only-fusion` when the work is already done and the need is independent review.\n\n## Workflow definition, project binding and workflow run\n\nAgentsFlow separates three levels:\n\n```text\nWorkflow Definition\n  Universal upstream process shape under `workflows/`.\n\nProject Binding / Overlay\n  Project-specific mapping from workflow requirements to repo paths, commands,\n  gates, instruments, evidence sources and local rules.\n\nWorkflow Run\n  Concrete task instance with task contract, plan, behavior bindings, gate reports,\n  evidence bundle, reviewer reports, finding validation and fusion report.\n```\n\nUpstream workflow definitions must not be edited for each project. Projects\nshould create `.agentsflow/` overlays and workflow binding files.\n"
    },

      "path": "examples/e2e/minimal-python-project/Docs/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-17-add-calculator/review-prompts/generalist-a.md",
      "size_bytes": 4017,
      "content": "You are an AgentsFlow external read-only reviewer.\nStart from zero prior conversation context. Do not use or assume any forked orchestrator context. Review only the provided packet. Do not request repository access. Do not modify files. Do not run tests. Do not execute scripts. Do not produce patches. Do not update evidence. Prioritize substantive findings over output serialization. Return schema-valid reviewer-report JSON when you can do so without losing clarity; otherwise return clear structured findings for orchestrator normalization before gate use.\n\nAll findings must be candidate-unvalidated. Report missing mandatory evidence. Report plausible P0/P1 blockers even outside a focused role. The main/orchestrating agent validates relevance before findings affect workflow decisions.\n\nYour reviewer-report JSON must include review_context with run_id, material_change_id, review_packet_path and reviewer_instance_id copied from the review packet when present. Do not invent those values.\n\nResolved reviewer role contract:\nname: generalist\nkind: reviewer_role\npurpose: Apply the common review rubric without a specialized focus zone.\nprimary_focus:\n- contract and accepted-decision consistency\n- verification evidence and missing mandatory checks\n- scope boundaries and non-goals\n- obvious architecture, reliability, safety or workflow risks\nmust_report:\n- Any plausible P0/P1 blocker, even if it does not fit a narrower rubric section.\n- Missing mandatory evidence.\n- Contradictions between contract, diff, gate report and accepted decisions.\nforbidden_actions:\n- run_tests\n- run_scripts\n- modify_files\n- create_patch\n\nReview packet:\n{\n  \"agentsflow_version\": \"v0.2.0\",\n  \"workflow\": \"big-feature-contract-first\",\n  \"run_id\": \"2026-06-17-add-calculator\",\n  \"reviewer_role\": \"generalist\",\n  \"reviewer_instance_id\": \"generalist-a\",\n  \"review_goal\": \"Apply the \"same_packet\": true,\n    \"same_rubric\": true,\n    \"same_output_schema\": true\n  },\n  \"role_contract\": \"profiles/reviewer_roles/generalist.yaml\",\n  \"review_prompt_contract\": {\n       "path": "examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/claude-code.yaml",
      "size_bytes": 1787,
      "content": "# External reviewer provider configuration template.\n# Project overlays may adapt this file under `.agentsflow/external-reviewers/`.\n\nprovider: claude-code\nkind: external_reviewer_provider\nversion: 1\n\nbilling:\n  expected_mode: subscription-local\n  forbid_api_key_usage: true\n  fail_if_env_present:\n    - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\n    - ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN\n    - ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL\n    - CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK\n    - CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX\n  notes: >\n    API-key based Claude usage and alternate Claude API/proxy routes are\n    forbidden for the v0.2 MVP because they can create uncontrolled cost. This\n    provider is intended for local subscription Claude Code CLI usage only.\n\nexecution:\n  command: claude\n  mode: non_interactive_print\n  sandbox_mode: require_escalated\n  output_format: json\n  permission_mode: default\n  tools: \"\"\n  model: opus\n  effort: max\n  timeout_seconds: 900\n  max_turns: 3\n  use_bare_mode: false\n  no_session_persistence: true\n\ncontext_policy:\n  start_mode: fresh_context\n  fork_conversation_context: false\n  session_persistence: false\n  input_mode: review_packet\n\npermissions:\n  read_packet_only: true\n  write_files: false\n  run_tests: false\n  run_verification_instruments: false\n  run_tools: false\n  may_request_additional_verification: true\n\ninputs:\n  review_packet_schema: schemas/review-packet.schema.json\n\noutputs:\n  reviewer_report_schema: schemas/reviewer-report.schema.json\n  invocation_metadata_schema: schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json\n\nnormalization:\n  force_finding_status: candidate-unvalidated\n  require_schema_validation: true\n  # Example fixture output is non-sensitive. Project overlays should keep this\n  # false unless raw provider output has been explicitly classified as\n  # non-sensitive.\n  preserve_raw_output: true\n  record_trace: true\n"
    },

\"reviewer-report.claude-architecture.raw.json\",\n    \"source_hash\": \"sha256:<raw-provider-output>\",\n    \"output_path\": \"reviewer-report.claude-architecture.json\",\n    \"output_hash\":     {
      "path": "examples/pr-merge-readiness/README.md",
      "size_bytes": 794,
      "content": "# PR Merge Readiness Examples\n\nThis directory contains small artifacts for the `pr-merge-readiness` utility\nworkflow. The examples are validation fixtures, not a release automation system.\n\n`complete/pr-merge-readiness-report.json` is a complete validation fixture with\nrecorded human approval, deterministic check evidence, provider-mirrored review\nevidence and live-shaped Claude invocation metadata references. It is marked\nwith `fixture.not_real_readiness_evidence: true`, so the evaluator treats it as\nnon-mergeable fixture evidence. The report-facing raw-output evidence uses\nredacted summary artifacts, and the Claude invocation metadata records the raw\nprovider output hash without a persisted raw-output path. This directory is not\nproof that an actual live PR readiness run occurred.\n"
    },

verification_gate\n  - id: PRM-BHV-014\n    scenario: \"Collision-control reports must be structured reviewer evidence\"\n    source:\n      type: contract\n      path: task.contract.md\n      section: Behavioral Scenarios\n    required: true\n    severity_if_unverified: P1\n    checks:\n      - id: self-declared-control-reports-need-prompt-contract\n        type: test\n        runner: pytest\n        command: \".venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_pr_merge_readiness.py::test_self_declared_collision_control_reports_require_prompt_contract\"\n      - verification_gate\n  - id: REVIEW-SET-BHV-001\n    scenario: \"Review-set artifacts are prepared before live reviewer invocation\"\n    source:\n      type: contract\n      path: task.contract.md\n      section: Review Gate\n    required: true\n    severity_if_unverified: P1\n    checks:\n      - id: generated-packets-prompts-and-predeclared-invocation\n        type: test\n        runner: pytest\n        command: \".venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts_smoke.py::test_prepare_review_set_artifacts_generates_packets_prompts_and_evidence\"\n      - id: stale-embedded-snapshot-rejected\n        type: test\n        runner: pytest\n        command: \".venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts_smoke.py::test_prepare_review_set_artifacts_rejects_stale_embedded_file_snapshot\"\n      - id: run-artifacts-review-artifacts-scanned\n        type: test\n        runner: pytest\n        command: \".venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts_smoke.py::test_repository_validation_scans_run_artifacts_review_artifacts\"\n      - id: async-external-first-runner\n        type: test\n        runner: pytest\n        command: \".venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts_smoke.py::test_run_review_set_starts_external_reviewers_asynchronously\"\n      - id: timeout-preserves-completed-peer\n        type: test\n        runner: pytest\n        command: \".venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts_smoke.py::test_run_review_set_times_out_hung_external_without_losing_completed_peer\"\n    gates:\n      -     {
      "path": "run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/decision-contract.md",
      "size_bytes": 16163,
      "content": "# Design Decision Review: pr-merge-readiness First Slice\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis artifact is the design-review decision packet for the\n`pr-merge-readiness` bootstrap run. It does not ask for one blanket approval\nwithout context. It first lists the open decisions, then reviews each decision\nwith options, tradeoffs and a recommended path.\n\nHuman acceptance of this packet authorizes `red_capture`, not implementation\nwithout failing-test evidence.\n\n## Review Procedure\n\n1. Present the full open decision inventory.\n2. Review each decision independently.\n3. For each decision, show viable options, tradeoffs, recommendation and\n   rationale.\n4. Record explicit human acceptance or change requests.\n5. Only after accepted blocking decisions are recorded, enter `red_capture`.\n\n## Open Decision Inventory\n\n| ID | Decision | Blocking before red-capture? | Recommended path |\n|---|---|---:|---|\n| DDR-001 | Utility workflow scope and status | yes | v0.2 utility workflow, not primary application workflow |\n| DDR-002 | Build method for this workflow | yes | implement through `big-feature-contract-first` |\n| DDR-003 | Self-application artifact separation | yes | ignored `.agentsflow/` overlay plus `run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/...` run root |\n| DDR-004 | First implementation slice shape | yes | workflow + schema + template + example + validator + docs |\n| DDR-005 | Readiness authority model | yes | evidence report plus required human merge decision |\n| DDR-006 | Failure Path Matrix scope | yes | cover false readiness, missing/stale evidence, mock/live confusion, raw redaction and blocker triage |\n| DDR-007 | External Claude evidence policy | yes | normalized report and invocation metadata always; raw only when non-sensitive |\n| DDR-008 | Target workflow review gate topology | yes | heterogeneous-variable with provider-mirrored Codex/Claude topic pairs |\n| DDR-009 | Allowed and forbidden change boundaries | yes | narrow methodology paths; no release platform, PR mutation or provider rewrite |\n| DDR-010 | Readiness report field names and exact validator strictness | no | decide during red-capture from failing tests |\n\n## DDR-001: Utility Workflow Scope and Status\n\n### Problem\n\nAgentsFlow needs a repeatable way to judge whether a branch is ready for PR\nopening, PR acceptance or merge. This should not expand v0.2's primary workflow\nsurface.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Documentation-only checklist | Fast, no schema or validator work | Easy to drift; not enforceable; weak evidence story |\n| B | v0.2 utility workflow | Fits `review-only-fusion` precedent; gives a reusable gate without becoming an application workflow | Requires workflow/schema/template/test work |\n| C | Primary application workflow | First-class visibility | Expands v0.2 scope and competes with `project-initialization -> BFCF` |\n| D | Full release workflow | Could eventually cover release operations | Out of v0.2 scope; pulls in platform/runtime decisions |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option B: `pr-merge-readiness` as a v0.2 utility workflow.\n\n### Rationale\n\nThis keeps the supported application path narrow while giving the project an\nexplicit acceptance/merge gate. It avoids pretending that merge readiness is\njust prose, but also avoids turning v0.2 into a release platform.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs `pr-merge-readiness` accepted as a v0.2 utility workflow rather than a\nprimary application workflow or release platform?\n\n## DDR-002: Build Method for This Workflow\n\n### Problem\n\nAgentsFlow is applying itself to build a new workflow. The process must avoid\nad hoc edits and avoid cyclic proof.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Direct edits without workflow run | Fast | Undercuts the methodology; weak evidence trail |\n| B | Build through existing `big-feature-contract-first` | Dogfoods accepted workflow; contract, red/green and review are explicit | More ceremony |\n| C | Wait until `pr-merge-readiness` exists, then use it | Conceptually neat later | Cyclic for bootstrap; impossible before implementation |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option B for the bootstrap run.\n\n### Rationale\n\n`big-feature-contract-first` is already the supported feature workflow. The new\nworkflow must not prove itself before it exists, so the first full\n`pr-merge-readiness` use should happen in a later PR/merge acceptance run.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs it accepted that this first slice is built through BFCF and explicitly marked\nas a bootstrap, not a self-proof?\n\n## DDR-003: Self-Application Artifact Separation\n\n### Problem\n\nAgentsFlow is both methodology source and target project. Run evidence,\nproject overlay and methodology files can be confused.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Put run artifacts under `docs/` | Discoverable | Confuses evidence with methodology source |\n| B | Put run artifacts under `.agentsflow/` | Localized | `.agentsflow/` should be overlay/cache, not durable run history |\n| C | Put run artifacts under `Docs/agentsflow/runs/` | Matches ordinary target-project convention | Bad for this repo because `Docs` can collapse into existing `docs` on case-insensitive filesystems |\n| D | Use `run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/` | Clear separation from methodology source and overlay | Adds one repository-local artifact root |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option D for self-application run history, with `.agentsflow/` reserved for\nignored local overlay.\n\n### Rationale\n\nThis protects the source/evidence boundary with minimal machinery. Ordinary\ntarget projects can still use the canonical `Docs/agentsflow/runs/...` pattern;\nthis repository needs the special case because it already has lowercase\n`docs/`.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs the self-application storage boundary accepted: ignored `.agentsflow/`\noverlay, run evidence in `run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/...`, promoted\nmethodology changes only through explicit source diffs?\n\n## DDR-004: First Implementation Slice Shape\n\n### Problem\n\nA workflow can be documented but still not usable. The first slice must be\nsmall enough for v0.2 and concrete enough to validate.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Docs only | Cheapest | Not executable; weak DoD |\n| B | Workflow + docs | Better structure | Still no report contract or validator evidence |\n| C | Workflow + schema + template + example + validator + docs | Executable first slice; validates examples and report semantics | More files and tests |\n| D | Full runner/runtime integration | Most complete | Too much scope for v0.2 closure |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option C.\n\n### Rationale\n\nThe desired v0.2 UX needs a concrete readiness packet, not just workflow prose.\nThe schema/template/example/validator set is enough to make the workflow usable\nwithout creating a new runtime.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs the first slice accepted as workflow definition, readiness report schema,\ntemplate, examples, validator coverage and docs, with no platform mutation\nruntime?\n\n## DDR-005: Readiness Authority Model\n\n### Problem\n\nThe workflow evaluates evidence, but merge acceptance is a human authority\nboundary.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Green checks imply merge-ready | Simple | Removes human authority; dangerous false readiness |\n| B | Green checks produce `awaiting_human_decision` until recorded approval | Clear authority split | Requires one more decision artifact |\n| C | Workflow may auto-approve low-risk PRs | Convenient later | Out of scope and risky for v0.2 |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option B.\n\n### Rationale\n\nThe readiness workflow can say evidence is complete and no blockers are known.\nIt must not silently convert that into human merge approval. This also gives the\nfuture PR acceptance run a clean gate.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs it accepted that no report may claim accepted merge readiness without a\nrecorded human merge decision?\n\n## DDR-006: Failure Path Matrix Scope\n\n### Problem\n\nThe workflow must test likely failure modes, not only happy-path report\ngeneration.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Happy path only | Minimal | Misses the actual PR-readiness risks |\n| B | Generic broad risk list | Comprehensive-looking | Can become noisy and unfocused |\n| C | Focused FPM from this workflow's risk surfaces | Testable and tied to authority/evidence risks | Requires maintaining scenario-to-test bindings |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option C.\n\n### Rationale\n\nThe selected FPM covers false readiness, missing evidence, missing human\napproval, mock/live Claude confusion, sensitive raw output, rejected blocker\ntriage, stale review and cyclic self-proof. These are the risks most likely to\nmake a PR gate misleading. The same FPM also shapes review specialization:\nselected risk surfaces and failure paths are mapped into review topics and focus\nzones, while every reviewer remains responsible for reporting plausible P0/P1\nissues outside the assigned focus.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs the focused FPM accepted as the required red-capture target and\nreviewer-specialization input for this slice?\n\n## DDR-007: External Claude Evidence Policy\n\n### Problem\n\nLive Claude review is useful evidence, but raw external output can contain\nsensitive local context. Mock smoke evidence must not be confused with live\nreview.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Commit all raw Claude output | Maximum auditability | Sensitive leakage risk |\n| B | Commit normalized report and invocation metadata; commit raw only when non-sensitive | Good auditability with redaction boundary | Requires explicit redaction reason/pointer |\n| C | Store no live invocation evidence | Avoids leakage | Weak proof that live review occurred |\n| D | Treat mock smoke as equivalent to live | CI-friendly | Misleading and procedurally wrong |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option B.\n\n### Rationale\n\nThis preserves enough evidence to verify that live Claude was invoked and what\ncandidate findings were produced, while avoiding default raw-output commits.\nThe schema should distinguish mock, live, redacted and unavailable states.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs the Claude evidence policy accepted: normalized report and invocation\nmetadata always, raw output only when explicitly non-sensitive?\n\n## DDR-008: Target Workflow Review Gate Topology\n\n### Problem\n\nFuture `pr-merge-readiness` runs have different review concerns: verification\nevidence, methodology architecture and adversarial authority failures. A single\ngeneralist pair may miss important differences. This decision describes the\ntarget workflow's PR-readiness review topology, not the BFCF development review\ngate used to build the workflow.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Homogeneous dual generalist review | Simple and already standard | Under-focused for PR/merge readiness |\n| B | Heterogeneous review with one provider per topic | Focused | Provider disagreement is hard to interpret because topics differ |\n| C | Provider-mirrored topic pairs: each topic reviewed by Codex and Claude | Focused plus model diversity on same questions | Six reports; more expensive and slower |\n| D | Large unconstrained reviewer pool | Broad | Noisy; higher orchestration cost |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option C for PR/merge readiness.\n\n### Rationale\n\nMirroring makes provider disagreement meaningful: Codex and Claude both inspect\nverification/evidence, both inspect architecture/process and both inspect\nadversarial/authority. Fusion can compare disagreements within each topic\ninstead of mixing unrelated review scopes. The gate uses the shared\nreview/fusion/finding-validation control block rather than duplicating local\nrules: mechanical intake, canonical finding extraction,\nduplicate/related/conflict grouping, topic-pair comparison, fusion report,\norchestrator relevance validation, collision-control for rejected or downgraded\nP0/P1 candidates, and review-cycle decision. The block preserves authority\nboundaries: deterministic automation validates structure and evidence, fusion\nprovides decision support, the orchestrator validates candidate findings, and\nhuman-mediated gates remain human-owned.\n\nThis six-report gate is not automatically used to review the implementation of\n`pr-merge-readiness` itself. The current BFCF development run uses a separate,\nproportional development review gate after green verification: two generalists\n(Codex and Claude when local Claude is available) plus one Codex\nadversarial-authority specialist for the riskiest authority layer.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs the provider-mirrored heterogeneous gate accepted for future\n`pr-merge-readiness` runs, despite the six-report cost?\n\n## DDR-009: Allowed and Forbidden Change Boundaries\n\n### Problem\n\nThe slice must not sprawl into adjacent systems.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Broad edits wherever convenient | Flexible | Scope creep and methodology drift |\n| B | Narrow workflow/schema/template/example/docs/validator paths | Controlled and reviewable | Some future improvements deferred |\n| C | Split every adjacent issue into separate ADRs before implementation | Very controlled | Too slow for v0.2 closure |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option B.\n\n### Rationale\n\nThe current target is a usable PR-readiness utility, not a redesign of\nproject-initialization, BFCF, provider wrappers or release automation. Narrow\npaths reduce accidental methodology drift.\n\nOne approved exception is the minimal hardening of the existing reusable\nreview/fusion layer, so `pr-merge-readiness` can parameterize that block instead\nof copying fusion and finding-validation rules into its own workflow.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nAre the allowed/forbidden path and behavior boundaries accepted for this slice?\n\n## DDR-010: Field Names and Exact Validator Strictness\n\n### Problem\n\nThe report schema needs concrete fields, and raw-output redaction needs exact\nvalidation rules. Some details are best discovered by writing failing tests.\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | Decide every field before red-capture | Precise | Can become abstract bikeshedding |\n| B | Set semantic requirements now, derive exact fields through red-capture | Test-driven and pragmatic | Requires discipline during implementation |\n| C | Leave fields mostly free-form | Flexible | Weak validator and poor interoperability |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nUse Option B.\n\n### Rationale\n\nThe contract already fixes semantics: human decision required, evidence paths\nvalidated, live/mock distinction, stale review exclusion, redaction reason. The\nexact field names can be designed through failing tests without changing the\naccepted behavior.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nIs it accepted that exact report field names and validator strictness are\nnonblocking details to be fixed in red-capture tests before implementation?\n\n## Aggregate Recommendation\n\nProceed to `red_capture` only if DDR-001 through DDR-009 are accepted. DDR-010\nmay remain delegated to red-capture as long as the semantic requirements above\nremain binding.\n\n## Evidence\n\n- `human-decisions.yaml`\n- `human-questions.yaml`\n- `task.contract.md`\n- `behavior.bindings.yaml`\n- `impact-map.yaml`\n- `plan.md`\n- `plan-gate-report.md`\n\n## Freshness / Revisit Triggers\n\n- The branch gains material methodology changes after this packet.\n- The report schema cannot express one of the accepted FPM paths.\n- Live Claude invocation policy changes.\n- Reviewers find a P0/P1 issue with the decision model.\n- PR platform automation becomes an accepted v0.3 goal.\n\n## Rollback / Migration Notes\n\nRemove the utility workflow, schema/template/example, validator hooks and docs\nupdates. Historical run artifacts remain under `run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/`\nand do not become methodology source.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/evidence-report.md",
      "size_bytes": 7268,
      "content": "# Evidence Report\n\nRun: `2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness`\nWorkflow: `big-feature-contract-first`\nTarget feature: `pr-merge-readiness`\n\n## Changed Areas\n\n- Added `pr-merge-readiness` utility workflow manifest.\n- Added readiness report schema, template and complete example.\n- Added deterministic readiness report evaluator and repository validation hook.\n- Added focused red/green tests for readiness semantics, including failed\n  check/review statuses, live external invocation metadata, blocker finding\n  statuses, collision-control completeness, human decision record matching,\n  required review topology, reviewer-report source finding intake and Claude\n  invocation role/packet/prompt/schema/path/hash binding.\n- Added `run_review_set.py` external-reviewer heartbeat progress output on stderr\n  so long Claude invocations expose dispatch/running/completion state without\n  streaming provider stdout.\n- Added prepared review-packet schema validation coverage so missing required\n  packet fields are caught before live external reviewer invocation.\n- Added role-binding checks for required provider-mirrored PR review topics:\n  `required_reviews[].role`, review `role_contract_path`, reviewer-report\n  `reviewer.role` and Claude invocation `reviewer_role` must align.\n- Added `run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs` review-artifact scanning so\n  self-application run packets/contracts are validated like documented\n  `Docs/agentsflow/runs` artifacts.\n- Added collision-control prompt-contract provenance checks and predeclared\n  invocation-set preparation evidence for review-set runs.\n- Tightened collision-control readiness evidence so prompt contracts must be\n  schema-valid and both prompt/control report timestamps must not predate the\n  latest material change.\n- Tightened accepted human decision matching so it must target the exact\n  evaluated readiness report artifact, not only a basename, and duplicate or\n  superseded merge decisions fail closed.\n- Tightened required Claude review evidence so `required_live` misdeclaration\n  blocks accepted readiness.\n- Bound live Claude invocation metadata to wrapper identity, provider config\n  hash and the forbidden API/proxy environment baseline.\n- Aligned PR-readiness rubric hash validation with prompt-contract rubric hashes\n  emitted by the external reviewer wrapper.\n- Added stale embedded file snapshot detection to review artifact preparation.\n- Allowed concrete internal model labels, such as `gpt-5-codex`, to satisfy the\n  declared `codex` model family in review-set evidence.\n- Normalized string `requests_for_additional_verification` values from Claude\n  reviewer output into schema-compatible request objects.\n- Closed evaluator fail-open paths for unmapped blockers and mixed-timezone\n  review timestamps.\n- Added reviewer-report `review_context` binding so internal reviewer reports\n  must prove the current run/material change/review packet/reviewer instance,\n  preventing stale reports with matching reviewer ids from satisfying a gate.\n- Tightened that binding after development review so `run_id` and\n  `reviewer_instance_id` are enforced, not merely documented.\n- Anchored pr-merge-readiness review packets to the evaluated readiness report\n  `run_id` and `material_change_id`, blocking stale packet/report pairs that are\n  internally consistent but belong to another readiness run or material change.\n- Closed the post-review audit persistence blocker where required live external\n  evidence could declare `raw_output.persistence: not_persisted` and still\n  compute `accepted_merge_ready`.\n- Closed follow-on authority/evidence blockers where collision-control could\n  clear rejected/downgraded P0/P1 findings without a supporting control\n  conclusion, where control reports could predate the collision-control prompt,\n  where redacted live Claude output could be accepted without a concrete\n  artifact path/hash, where redacted live Claude evidence incorrectly required\n  raw provider output persistence, and where fixture reports could compute real\n  readiness.\n- Closed the accepted-decision consistency blocker where the external reviewer\n  wrapper always persisted raw provider output despite DDR-007; provider configs\n  now declare `normalization.preserve_raw_output`, the safe template default is\n  false, and the wrapper can normalize without writing raw output.\n- Closed follow-on raw-policy and review-set integration blockers where\n  redacted/summary/pointer evidence could still accept a persisted raw provider\n  output path, completed review-set validation still required a raw provider\n  output file despite `preserve_raw_output: false`, and live Claude readiness\n  evidence hash-bound but did not policy-validate the provider config.\n- Closed follow-on raw-source and human-decision replay blockers where\n  redacted/summary/pointer evidence could still preserve raw output through\n  invocation or reviewer-report `normalization.source_path`, and where a prior\n  human merge decision could be replayed after material report changes.\n- Migrated the canonical `pr-merge-readiness` example and reviewer invocation\n  template to redacted raw-output metadata without persisted raw paths.\n- Updated v0.2 documentation to classify `pr-merge-readiness` as a utility workflow.\n- Added `pr-merge-readiness` compatibility to existing evidence/reviewer/fusion skills used by the workflow.\n\n## Verification\n\n| Command | Result |\n|---|---|\n| `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_pr_merge_readiness.py -q` | passed, 65 passed |\n| `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts_smoke.py -q` | passed, 152 passed |\n| `.venv/bin/python scripts/validate_repo.py --root .` | passed |\n| `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` | passed, 217 passed |\n| `make check` | passed |\n| `.venv/bin/python scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py --provider claude-code --config examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/claude-code.yaml --input examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/review-packet.architecture.json --mock-response examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/mock-raw-output.json --output /tmp/reviewer-report.pr-merge-readiness-smoke.json` | passed |\n| `git diff --check` | passed |\n\n## Failure Path Matrix Coverage\n\n| FPM row | Binding | Status |\n|---|---|---|\n| `PRM-FPM-001` | `PRM-BHV-001` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-002` | `PRM-BHV-002` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-003` | `PRM-BHV-003` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-004` | `PRM-BHV-004` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-005` | `PRM-BHV-005` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-006` | `PRM-BHV-006` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-007` | `PRM-BHV-007` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-008` | `PRM-BHV-008` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-009` | `PRM-BHV-009` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-010` | `PRM-BHV-010` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-011` | `PRM-BHV-011` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-012` | `PRM-BHV-012` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-013` | `PRM-BHV-013` | covered |\n| `PRM-FPM-014` | `PRM-BHV-014` | covered |\n\n## Boundary Check\n\nNo PR mutation, release-management runtime, CI-provider abstraction, API-key\nClaude usage or external reviewer provider rewrite was added.\n\n## Known Limitations\n\n- The readiness evaluator validates recorded report artifacts; it does not run\n  CI, launch reviewers or merge branches.\n- The example contains fixture evidence. Live Claude evidence is required only\n  for real local readiness runs that request it.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/human-decisions.yaml",

      "content": "version: 1\nrun_id: 2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness\nartifact_role: run_decision_log\n\ndecisions:\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.identity\n    phase_id: operating_context_preflight\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.identity\n    answer: pr-merge-readiness\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: Use a name that covers both PR readiness and merge acceptance without implying a full release-management runtime.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - The FPM also\n      drives reviewer specialization by mapping risk surfaces and failure paths\n      into review topics and focus zones, while reviewers must still report\n      plausible P0/P1 issues outside their focus.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - decision-contract.md\n      - task.contract.md\n      - behavior.bindings.yaml\n      - review-prompt-contract artifacts\n      - workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml\n      - schemas/pr-merge-readiness-report.schema.json\n      - examples/pr-merge-readiness/**\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_007\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_007\n    answer: normalized_report_and_invocation_metadata_always_raw_only_when_non_sensitive\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: >-\n      Human accepted preserving live Claude auditability through normalized\n      reviewer reports and invocation metadata while avoiding default commits of\n      sensitive raw provider output. Mock smoke evidence must remain distinct\n      from live Claude review evidence.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - decision-contract.md\n      - task.contract.md\n      - behavior.bindings.yaml\n      - schemas/pr-merge-readiness-report.schema.json\n      - templates/pr-merge-readiness-report.*\n      - examples/pr-merge-readiness/**\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008\n    answer: heterogeneous_variable_provider_mirrored_topic_pairs\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: >-\n      Human accepted a provider-mirrored heterogeneous review gate for future\n      pr-merge-readiness runs. Verification/evidence, architecture/process and\n      adversarial/authority topics are each reviewed by both Codex and Claude;\n      fusion compares provider disagreement within topic pairs and must not use\n      majority vote to override P0/P1 candidate blockers. The accepted target\n      workflow gate includes mechanical report intake, canonical finding\n      extraction, duplicate/related/conflict grouping, topic-pair comparison,\n      fusion report, orchestrator relevance validation, collision-control for\n      rejected or downgraded P0/P1 candidates, and review-cycle decision.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - decision-contract.md\n      - run.yaml\n      - task.contract.md\n      - plan.md\n      - review-prompt-contract artifacts\n      - workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008_development_review_scope\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008_development_review_scope\n    answer: target_six_reviewer_gate_not_used_for_development_run_by_default\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: >-\n      Human corrected a scope ambiguity: the six-reviewer provider-mirrored\n      topology belongs to future pr-merge-readiness PR/merge readiness runs,\n      not to the BFCF development review gate used to build that workflow. The\n      current development run uses a separate proportional review gate after\n      green verification, with escalation only if evidence or human decision\n      requires it.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - run.yaml\n      - task.contract.md\n      - plan.md\n      - task-breakdown.md\n      - decision-contract.md\n      - plan-gate-report.md\n      - plan-gate-report.json\n      - .agentsflow/workflows/big-feature-contract-first.binding.yaml\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.development_review_authority_specialist\n    phase_id: red_capture\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.development_review_authority_specialist\n    answer: add_codex_adversarial_authority_specialist_to_development_review\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: >-\n      Human requested one additional Codex specialist for the riskiest layer of\n      the BFCF development review. The development review gate remains separate\n      from the target workflow's six-reviewer PR-readiness gate and now uses\n      three reviewers: Codex generalist, Claude generalist, and Codex\n      adversarial-authority specialist focused on false readiness,\n      human-owned decisions, automatic gate versus human-mediated authority,\n      mock-vs-live evidence claims and P0/P1 collision-control bypass.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - run.yaml\n      - task.contract.md\n      - plan.md\n      - task-breakdown.md\n      - decision-contract.md\n      - plan-gate-report.md\n      - plan-gate-report.json\n      - .agentsflow/workflows/big-feature-contract-first.binding.yaml\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.development_review_external_reviewer_blocker_fix\n    phase_id: development_review\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.development_review_external_reviewer_blocker_fix\n    answer: authorize_minimal_external_reviewer_blocker_fix\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: >-\n      Human authorized fixing the live Claude review gate after the accepted\n      development review attempt exposed provider/wrapper blockers. The\n      approved work is limited to preserving subscription-local Claude review,\n      enforcing read-only reviewer behavior, binding invocation metadata and\n      outputs, adding progress/timeout behavior, normalizing provider output\n      into the reviewer-report schema and preparing review artifacts\n      deterministically. It does not authorize a new provider, API-key Claude\n      usage, write-enabled external reviewers or a generic multi-provider\n      runtime.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - task.contract.md\n      - run.yaml\n      - scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py\n      - scripts/reviewers/providers/claude_code.py\n      - scripts/reviewers/run_review_set.py\n      - scripts/reviewers/prepare_review_set_artifacts.py\n      - scripts/reviewers/prompt_rendering.py\n      - scripts/reviewers/README.md\n      - schemas/reviewer-report.schema.json\n      - schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json\n      - schemas/review-invocation-set.schema.json\n      - schemas/external-review-provider.schema.json\n      - templates/reviewer-invocation.json\n      - templates/external-review-provider.yaml\n      - docs/external-reviewer-provider-model.md\n      - examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/**\n      - examples/e2e/minimal-python-project/Docs/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-17-add-calculator/review-prompt-contract.yaml\n      - examples/e2e/minimal-python-project/Docs/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-17-add-calculator/review-prompts/*.md\n      - tests/test_scripts_smoke.py\n      - run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/**\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008_reusable_fusion_block\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008_reusable_fusion_block\n    answer: strengthen_existing_reusable_review_fusion_layer\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: >-\n      Human accepted that the post-review fusion and finding-validation\n      procedure is a reusable review gate-control block, not a pr-merge-readiness\n      local rule. The current slice may minimally harden the existing\n      fusion-synthesis skill, review-fusion model and fusion/finding-validation\n      templates instead of duplicating the pipeline in each workflow. The block\n      must preserve authority boundaries: deterministic automation validates\n      structure and evidence, fusion provides decision support, the orchestrator\n      validates candidate findings, and human-mediated gates remain human-owned.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - docs/review-fusion-model.md\n      - skills/fusion-synthesis/SKILL.md\n      - skills/fusion-synthesis/skill.yaml\n      - templates/fusion-report.md\n      - templates/finding-validation-report.md\n      - tests/test_scripts_smoke.py\n      - decision-contract.md\n    evidence:\n      red_capture:\n        command: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts_smoke.py::test_review_fusion_reusable_pipeline_is_documented -q\n        result: failed_before_shared_review_fusion_layer_update\n        observed_failure: mechanical intake and related pipeline terms were not documented in the reusable layer\n      green:\n        command: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_scripts_smoke.py::test_review_fusion_reusable_pipeline_is_documented -q\n        result: passed_after_shared_review_fusion_layer_update\n      validation:\n        - command: .venv/bin/python scripts/validate_repo.py --root .\n          result: passed\n        - command: .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q\n          result: 149 passed\n        - command: make check\n          result: passed\n        - command: git diff --check\n          result: passed\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_009\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_009\n    answer: narrow_slice_boundaries_with_approved_methodology_hardening_exceptions\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: blocking-material\n    rationale: >-\n      Human accepted the allowed and forbidden change boundaries for the first\n      slice. The slice remains narrow around pr-merge-readiness workflow,\n      schema, template, examples, validator coverage, docs and run artifacts,\n      with explicit approved exceptions for minimal BFCF contract-acceptance\n      hardening and reusable review/fusion layer hardening.\n    affected_artifacts:\n      - decision-contract.md\n      - task.contract.md\n      - plan.md\n      - run.yaml\n  - decision_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_010\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    question_ref: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_010\n    answer: semantic_requirements_now_exact_fields_through_red_capture_tests\n    status: confirmed\n    answered_by: human\n    classification: nonblocking-follow-up\n    rationale: >-\n      Human accepted that exact pr-merge-readiness report field names and\n      validator strictness are nonblocking implementation details. question_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_007\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    classification: blocking-material\n    question: >-\n      Is the Claude evidence policy accepted: normalized report and invocation\n      metadata always, raw output only when explicitly non-sensitive?\n    proposed_default: normalized report and metadata always, raw only when non-sensitive\n    answer_status: answered\n  - question_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    classification: blocking-material\n    question: Is the provider-mirrored heterogeneous gate accepted for future pr-merge-readiness runs despite the six-report cost?\n    proposed_default: heterogeneous-variable provider-mirrored topic pairs\n    answer_status: answered\n  - question_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008_development_review_scope\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    classification: blocking-material\n    question: Is it accepted that the six-reviewer target PR-readiness gate is not automatically used for this BFCF development run?\n    proposed_default: separate proportional BFCF development review gate\n    answer_status: answered\n  - question_id: pr_merge_readiness.development_review_authority_specialist\n    phase_id: red_capture\n    classification: blocking-material\n    question: Should the BFCF development review gate add one Codex specialist for the riskiest authority/adversarial layer?\n    proposed_default: add Codex adversarial-authority specialist to the two generalists\n    answer_status: answered\n  - question_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_008_reusable_fusion_block\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    classification: blocking-material\n    question: Should the fusion/finding-validation pipeline be modeled as a reusable review gate-control block instead of duplicated inside pr-merge-readiness, while preserving automatic gate vs human-in-the-loop authority boundaries?\n    proposed_default: strengthen existing fusion-synthesis skill, review-fusion docs and report templates\n    answer_status: answered\n  - question_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_009\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    classification: blocking-material\n    question: >-\n      Are the allowed and forbidden change boundaries accepted for this slice,\n      including the approved minimal BFCF contract-acceptance hardening and\n      reusable review/fusion layer hardening?\n    proposed_default: narrow pr-merge-readiness slice with approved methodology hardening exceptions\n    answer_status: answered\n  - question_id: pr_merge_readiness.ddr_010\n    phase_id: contract_acceptance\n    classification: nonblocking-follow-up\n    question: Are exact report field names and validator strictness accepted as nonblocking details to be fixed through red-capture tests before implementation?\n    proposed_default: semantic requirements now, exact fields through red-capture tests\n    answer_status: answered\n\nopen_questions: []\n"

      "path": "run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/plan-gate-report.md",
      "size_bytes": 2111,
      "content": "# Plan Gate Report\n\nStatus: pass\nRun ID: `2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness`\nGenerated: 2026-06-21\n\n## Checks\n\n| Check | Command | Result |\n|---|---|---|\n| Contract lint | `.venv/bin/python scripts/contract_lint.py --contract run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/task.contract.md` | pass |\n| Gherkin lint | `.venv/bin/python scripts/gherkin_lint.py --contract run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/task.contract.md` | pass |\n| Impact map check | `.venv/bin/python scripts/impact_map_check.py --impact-map run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/impact-map.yaml` | pass |\n| Workflow run validation | `validate_workflow_run_artifact(.../run.yaml)` | pass |\n| Repository validation | `.venv/bin/python scripts/validate_repo.py --root .` | pass |\n| Whitespace check | `git diff --check` | pass |\n\n## Target Workflow Review Model\n\nThe target `pr-merge-readiness` workflow review gate is `heterogeneous-variable`\nwith provider-mirrored topic pairs:\n\n- verification/evidence: Codex + Claude;\n- architecture/process: Codex + Claude;\n- adversarial/authority: Codex + Claude.\n\n## Development Review Gate\n\nThe target workflow's six-reviewer gate is not automatically used to review this\nBFCF development run. After green verification, this run uses a proportional\nBFCF development review gate: two generalists (Codex and Claude when local\nClaude is available) plus one Codex adversarial-authority specialist focused on\nthe riskiest authority layer. Additional focused reviewers are added only if\nevidence or a human decision requires escalation.\n\n## Verdict\n\nThe bootstrap run has enough accepted contract, impact and technical-plan\nevidence to enter the human-mediated `contract_acceptance` phase. Passing this\nplan gate does not authorize red-capture by itself. Implementation of\n`pr-merge-readiness` has not started.\n\n## Next Phase\n\n`contract_acceptance`: present the contract, behavior bindings, Failure Path\nMatrix, impact map and technical plan to the human. Red-capture may start only\nafter the acceptance decision is recorded in `human-decisions.yaml`.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/plan.md",
      "size_bytes": 5876,
      "content": "# Technical Plan\n\n## Goal\n\nImplement `pr-merge-readiness` as a v0.2 utility workflow with a minimal\nexecutable contract: workflow definition, readiness report schema/template,\nexample artifacts, validator coverage, documentation integration and\nprovider-mirrored heterogeneous review-gate design for target PR-readiness runs.\n\n## Grounding Evidence\n\n- `docs/plans/v0.2-next-slices.md` records accepted name, status, first-slice\n  shape, Claude evidence policy and self-application storage policy.\n- `docs/project-binding-model.md` and ADR-0013 define methodology source,\n  project overlay and run artifact separation.\n- ADR-0016 and `docs/external-reviewer-provider-model.md` constrain external\n  reviewer evidence.\n- `profiles/review_topologies/heterogeneous-variable.yaml` and existing reviewer\n  roles support the selected topic-pair review model.\n- `big-feature-contract-first` requires human `contract_acceptance` before\n  red-capture.\n\n## Affected Areas\n\n- `workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml`\n- `schemas/pr-merge-readiness-report.schema.json`\n- `templates/pr-merge-readiness-report.*`\n- `examples/pr-merge-readiness/**`\n- `scripts/repo_validation/**`\n- `tests/test_pr_merge_readiness.py`\n- `docs/pr-merge-readiness.md`\n- `README.md`, `docs/workflow-model.md`,\n  `docs/mvp-ready-workflow-standard.md`\n\n## Proposed Steps\n\n1. After `contract_acceptance`, add red-capture tests for readiness-report\n   semantics and run them before implementation.\n2. Implement the report schema and validation helper.\n3. Add template and example artifacts covering complete evidence, missing\n   evidence, human approval, mock-vs-live Claude, raw redaction, stale review,\n   blocker collision-control and self-application guard.\n4. Add repo-validation hooks for the schema/example and any explicit\n   self-application run-artifact validation.\n5. Add `workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml` as a v0.2 utility workflow.\n6. Encode target workflow review assignment policy as `heterogeneous-variable` with three\n   provider-mirrored topic pairs: verification/evidence, architecture/process\n   and adversarial/authority.\n7. Update documentation without promoting the utility to a primary application\n   workflow.\n8. Run green verification and prepare the proportional BFCF development review\n   packet: Codex generalist, Claude generalist when available, and a Codex\n   adversarial-authority specialist for the riskiest authority layer. Do not\n   automatically launch the target workflow's six-reviewer PR-readiness gate\n   for this development run.\n\n## Scope Boundaries\n\nAllowed:\n\n- Add the new utility workflow, schema, template, example, focused tests and\n  documentation listed in the contract.\n- Add narrowly scoped validator support for `pr-merge-readiness`.\n- Encode provider-mirrored topic assignments in the workflow/binding artifacts.\n- Use a separate proportional development review gate for this BFCF run: two\n  generalists plus one Codex adversarial-authority specialist.\n\nForbidden without approval:\n\n- Changing `project-initialization` semantics.\n- Changing `big-feature-contract-first` semantics during this run.\n- Rewriting external reviewer wrapper/provider code.\n- Adding GitHub/GitLab mutation, release automation or generic CI provider\n  abstraction.\n- Using `Docs/agentsflow/runs/` for this self-application run.\n- Adding new reviewer roles when existing `verification`, `architecture` and\n  `adversarial` roles cover the review themes.\n\nApproved scope amendment:\n\n- The human approved minimal `big-feature-contract-first` hardening during\n  `contract_acceptance`: design acceptance must use a decision inventory and\n  per-decision option/tradeoff review before asking for acceptance. This\n  amendment is limited to the BFCF `contract_acceptance` contract,\n  `decision-contracting` skill/template, supporting docs, validator logic and\n  regression test.\n- The human approved minimal hardening of the existing reusable review/fusion\n  layer during `contract_acceptance`: the post-review pipeline should be a\n  shared gate-control block, not duplicated inside `pr-merge-readiness`. This\n  amendment is limited to `docs/review-fusion-model.md`,\n  `skills/fusion-synthesis/**`, `templates/fusion-report.md`,\n  `templates/finding-validation-report.md` and regression tests, while\n  preserving automatic gate versus human-in-the-loop authority boundaries.\n\n## Tests / Checks to Add or Run\n\n- Red: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_pr_merge_readiness.py -q`\n- Green: `.venv/bin/python scripts/validate_repo.py --root .`\n- Green: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q`\n- Green: `make check`\n- Green: external reviewer mock smoke through\n  `scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py`\n- Green: `git diff --check main`\n\n## Risks\n\n- Overbuilding into a release platform. Mitigation: schema/report only, no PR\n  mutation.\n- Confusing self-application run artifacts with methodology docs. Mitigation:\n  `run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/**` and artifact role labels.\n- Confusing mock Claude evidence with live review. Mitigation: explicit schema\n  fields and validator tests.\n- Review cost from six reviewers. Mitigation: the mirrored topic-pair gate is a\n  target `pr-merge-readiness` policy, not the default review gate for developing\n  this workflow. The BFCF development run uses a proportional\n  homogeneous-plus-focused review gate with two generalists and one Codex\n  authority specialist unless green evidence or human decision requires further\n  escalation.\n\n## Rollback Plan\n\nRemove `workflows/pr-merge-readiness/`, schema/template/example, focused\nvalidator hooks/tests and docs updates. Historical run artifacts remain under\n`run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/` and are not methodology source.\n\n## Open Questions\n\nNone blocking. Field names and validator strictness are implementation details\ninside the accepted contract and will be red-captured before implementation.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/red-capture-report.md",
      "size_bytes": 1871,
      "content": "# Red Capture Report\n\nRun: `2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness`\nWorkflow: `big-feature-contract-first`\nTarget feature: `pr-merge-readiness`\nPhase: `red_capture`\nStatus: `captured`\n\n## Command\n\n```bash\n.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_pr_merge_readiness.py -q\n```\n\n## Result\n\nFailed as expected before implementation.\n\nSummary:\n\n- `10 failed`\n- `repo_validation.pr_merge_readiness` is missing.\n- `workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml` is missing.\n- `examples/pr-merge-readiness/complete/pr-merge-readiness-report.json` is missing.\n\n## Covered Bindings\n\n- `PRM-BHV-001`: complete evidence can produce an accepted merge-ready decision only with a recorded human decision.\n- `PRM-BHV-002`: green evidence without a human merge decision remains `awaiting_human_decision`.\n- `PRM-BHV-003`: missing required evidence blocks readiness and records missing paths.\n- `PRM-BHV-004`: mock external review evidence is not treated as live Claude evidence.\n- `PRM-BHV-005`: redacted sensitive raw external review output requires a redaction reason.\n- `PRM-BHV-006`: rejected or downgraded P0/P1 findings require collision-control evidence.\n- `PRM-BHV-007`: review packets older than material changes are stale and excluded.\n- `PRM-BHV-008`: self-application bootstrap must not claim cyclic self-proof.\n\nAdditional structural expectations:\n\n- `pr-merge-readiness` has a workflow manifest marked as a v0.2 utility workflow.\n- Target workflow review policy uses provider-mirrored heterogeneous topic pairs.\n- A complete example readiness report validates through the deterministic repository validator.\n\n## Implementation Authorization\n\nThis report captures failing red evidence. The next phase may implement the\nnarrow accepted slice needed to make these tests pass without expanding into PR\nmutation, release automation, generic CI providers or external reviewer provider\nrewrites.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-21-pr-merge-readiness/repository-grounding-report.md",
      "size_bytes": 3401,
      "content": "# Repository Grounding Report\n\n## Scope\n\nInspected existing AgentsFlow workflow, project-binding, external-review,\nreview-topology, phase-transition and validation artifacts before planning\n`pr-merge-readiness`.\n\n## Evidence Sources\n\n| Source | Relevant facts | Confidence |\n|---|---|---:|\n| `AGENTS.md` | v0.2 primary path remains narrow; avoid scope expansion and keep workflows modular. | high |\n| `docs/project-binding-model.md` | Separates workflow definitions, project bindings and run artifacts. | high |\n| `profiles/review_topologies/heterogeneous-variable.yaml` | Supports three to eight explicit reviewer roles and focus zones. | high |\n| `profiles/reviewer_roles/{verification,architecture,adversarial}.yaml` | Existing role contracts map to the selected review topics. | high |\n| `docs/external-reviewer-provider-model.md` | External review evidence shape includes packet, raw output, normalized report and invocation metadata. | high |\n| `workflows/big-feature-contract-first/workflow.yaml` | BFCF requires human `contract_acceptance` after plan gate and before red-capture. | high |\n| `docs/plans/v0.2-next-slices.md` | Records accepted name/status/first-slice shape and self-application storage policy. | high |\n\n## Existing Contracts / ADRs\n\n- ADR-0010 gate executability rule.\n- ADR-0011 behavior binding rule.\n- ADR-0013 project application model.\n- ADR-0016 external reviewer provider interface.\n- ADR-0017 test-framed implementation phase.\n- ADR-0018 phase transition control.\n\n## Current Behavior / Architecture Notes\n\nObserved:\n\n- Repository validation already checks workflow definitions, schemas, examples,\n  selected run artifacts and external-reviewer provider fixtures.\n- `heterogeneous-variable` already exists and allows explicit focus zones.\n- Existing reviewer roles cover verification, architecture and adversarial\n  concerns without adding new roles.\n- Existing Claude provider support distinguishes subscription-local invocation\n  from mock smoke evidence.\n- `run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/**` is outside the lowercase `docs/`\n  methodology-source tree and avoids the self-application `Docs`/`docs`\n  collision.\n\nInference:\n\n- The first `pr-merge-readiness` slice should add a structured report and\n  validator checks, not a PR platform integration or release runtime.\n- Provider-mirrored topic pairs can be represented as review assignment policy\n  inside the workflow/binding layer without adding a new review topology.\n\n## Gaps And Assumptions\n\n| Gap or assumption | Blocking? | Required action |\n|---|---:|---|\n| Exact readiness report field names are not implemented yet. | no | Define minimal schema before red-capture tests. |\n| Validator discovery for `run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/**/run.yaml` is not currently part of `validate_repo`. | no | Add focused validation or keep explicit run validation evidence for bootstrap artifacts. |\n| Live Claude may be unavailable in CI. | no | Preserve mock smoke as CI-safe baseline and require explicit live/unavailable evidence in local readiness. |\n| Six-reviewer gate is heavier than the baseline. | no | Use it only for PR/merge readiness, where multiple risk themes justify provider mirroring. |\n\n## Plan-Gate Evidence Summary\n\nThe plan is grounded enough to enter plan gate after the impact and technical\nplan artifacts are created. Red-capture remains blocked until the later human\n`contract_acceptance` phase.\n"
    },

Post-review blocker-fix verification passed with 20 targeted PRM tests, 169 full pytest tests, repository validation, make check, mock external reviewer smoke and diff whitespace check.\n      - Second blocker-fix verification passed with 27 targeted PRM tests, 176 full pytest tests and repository validation after adding required review topology, reviewer-report source finding intake, Claude invocation binding and structured collision-control report checks.\n      - Fourth blocker-fix verification passed with 35 targeted PRM tests, 3 review-set runner/preparation targeted tests, 184 full pytest tests, repository validation, make check, mock external reviewer smoke and diff whitespace check after tightening target topology, final merge human-decision binding, source finding reviewer identity, Claude artifact hash binding, collision-control report relevance, prepared packet schema validity and external-reviewer/internal-wait progress heartbeat output.\n      - Fifth blocker-fix verification passed with 38 targeted PRM tests, 8 selected review-set/preparation/invocation-evidence tests, 188 full pytest tests, repository validation, make check, mock external reviewer smoke and diff whitespace check after adding review-role binding, run-artifacts validation coverage, collision-control prompt-contract provenance, predeclared invocation-set evidence and forbidden-action packet normalization.\n      - Sixth blocker-fix verification passed with 48 targeted PRM tests, 151 reviewer/tooling smoke tests, 199 full pytest tests, repository validation, make check, mock external reviewer smoke and diff whitespace check after fixing evaluator false-readiness paths, live Claude guardrail proof, internal model-family matching, rubric hash binding, stale embedded packet snapshot detection and internal reviewer report review-context binding.\n      - Seventh blocker-fix verification passed with 50 targeted PRM tests, 151 reviewer/tooling smoke tests, 201 full pytest tests, repository validation, make check, mock external reviewer smoke and diff whitespace check after tightening internal reviewer report context binding for run_id and reviewer_instance_id.\n      - Eighth blocker-fix verification passed with 51 targeted PRM tests, 151 reviewer/tooling smoke tests, 202 full pytest tests, repository validation, make check, mock external reviewer smoke and diff whitespace check after anchoring pr-merge-readiness review packets to the evaluated report run_id and material_change_id.\n      - Tenth blocker-fix verification passed with 65 targeted PRM tests, 152 reviewer/tooling smoke tests, 217 full pytest tests, repository validation, make check, mock external reviewer smoke and diff whitespace check after closing normalization raw-source bypasses, binding human merge approval to material/report hash and cleaning redacted raw-output examples.\n  -
