match assignment\")\n            invocation_report_path_ref = (\n                resolve_invocation_set_ref(\n                    invocation_entry.get(\"report_path\"), f\"review_invocation_set.reviewers[{reviewer}].report_path\"\n                )\n                if require_completed_assignment_outputs\n                else None\n            )\n            if invocation_report_path_ref:\n                resolved_invocation_report = invocation_report_path_ref.resolve()\n                if resolved_invocation_report in invocation_report_paths:\n                    errors.append(\n                        f\"{path}: review_invocation_set report_path must be unique; \"\n                        f\"{reviewer} reuses report for {invocation_report_paths[resolved_invocation_report]}\"\n                    )\n                else:\n                    invocation_report_paths[resolved_invocation_report] = reviewer\n            if report_path and invocation_report_path_ref and invocation_report_path_ref.resolve() != report_path.resolve():\n                errors.append(f\"{path}: review_invocation_set reviewer {reviewer} report_path must match assignment\")\n            if provider == \"claude-code\":\n                if require_completed_assignment_outputs:\n                    resolve_invocation_set_ref(\n                        invocation_entry.get(\"raw_output_path\"),\n                        f\"review_invocation_set.reviewers[{reviewer}].raw_output_path\",\n                        required=False,\n                    )\n                    resolve_invocation_set_ref(\n                        invocation_entry.get(\"invocation_metadata_path\"),\n                        f\"review_invocation_set.reviewers[{reviewer}].invocation_metadata_path\",\n                    )\n                if require_completed_assignment_outputs and str(invocation_entry.get(\"execution_mode\") or \"\") != \"real\":\n                    errors.append(\n                        f\"{path}: review_invocation_set reviewer {reviewer} execution_mode must be real for completed external evidence\"\n                    )\n            if provider_policy.get(\"require_model_diversity\") is True and evidence_model_family:\n                completed_provider_model_families.add(f\"{provider}/{evidence_model_family}\")\n            elif provider_policy.get(\"require_model_diversity\") is not True:\n                completed_provider_model_families.add(f\"{provider}/{model_family}\")\n\n    if invocation_set_completed and provider_policy.get(\"require_model_diversity\") is True:\n        minimum = int(provider_policy.get(\"min_distinct_provider_model_families\", 2))\n        invocation_families = set(str(item) for item in invocation_set.get(\"provider_model_families\", []) or [])\n        if len(completed_provider_model_families) < minimum or not completed_provider_model_families.issubset(invocation_families):\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: review_invocation_set does not prove the required provider/model diversity\"\n            )\n\n    identity = data.get(\"identity\", {}) or {}\n    if identity.get(\"review_profile\") == \"homogeneous-dual\" and packet_paths:\n        for packet_path in packet_paths:\n            if not (packet_path.parent / \"shared-content.json\").exists():\n                errors.append(f\"{path}: homogeneous-dual run review packet missing sibling shared-content.json: {packet_path}\")\n        shared_content_paths = {\n            packet_path.parent / \"shared-content.json\"\n            for packet_path in packet_paths\n            if (packet_path.parent / \"shared-content.json\").exists()\n        }\n        if len(shared_content_paths) != 1:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: homogeneous-dual run review packets must have exactly one sibling shared-content.json\"\n            )\n        else:\n            shared_content_path = next(iter(shared_content_paths))\n            shared_content_hash = sha256_file(shared_content_path)\n            shared_content = parse_json(shared_content_path)\n            if not isinstance(shared_content, dict):\n                errors.append(f\"{shared_content_path}: shared packet content must be a JSON object\")\n                shared_content = {}\n            excluded_envelope_fields = set(str(item) for item in (shared_content.get(\"excluded_envelope_fields\", []) or []))\n            allowed_excluded_envelope_fields = {\"review_packet_path\", \"reviewer_instance_id\", \"provider\"}\n            unexpected_excluded_fields = sorted(excluded_envelope_fields - allowed_excluded_envelope_fields)\n            if unexpected_excluded_fields:\n                errors.append(\n                    f\"{shared_content_path}: excluded_envelope_fields may only contain envelope fields: \"\n                    f\"{', '.join(sorted(allowed_excluded_envelope_fields))}; unexpected: {', '.join(unexpected_excluded_fields)}\"\n                )\n\n            def shared_packet_payload(packet_data: dict, *, sidecar: bool = False) -> dict:\n                return {\n                    key: value\n                    for key, value in packet_data.items()\n                    if key not in excluded_envelope_fields\n                    and not (sidecar and key == \"excluded_envelope_fields\")\n                }\n\n            expected_shared_packet = shared_packet_payload(shared_content, sidecar=True)\n            for idx, packet in enumerate(inputs.get(\"review_packets\", []) or []):\n                if isinstance(packet, dict):\n                    compare_hash(\n                        path,\n                        f\"inputs.review_packets[{idx}].shared_packet_content_hash\",\n                        packet.get(\"shared_packet_content_hash\"),\n                        shared_content_hash,\n                        errors,\n                    )\n                    packet_path = root / str(packet.get(\"path\", \"\"))\n                    packet_data = parse_json(packet_path) if packet_path.exists() else None\n                    if isinstance(packet_data, dict):\n                        if \"excluded_envelope_fields\" in packet_data:\n                            errors.append(\n                                f\"{path}: inputs.review_packets[{idx}] must not contain reserved shared-content metadata field excluded_envelope_fields\"\n                            )\n                        if shared_packet_payload(packet_data) != expected_shared_packet:\n                            errors.append(\n                                f\"{path}: inputs.review_packets[{idx}] content must match shared-content.json except excluded envelope fields\"\n                            )\n            for idx, prompt in enumerate(data.get(\"rendered_prompts\", []) or []):\n                if isinstance(prompt, dict):\n                    compare_hash(\n                        path,\n                        f\"rendered_prompts[{idx}].shared_packet_content_hash\",\n                        prompt.get(\"shared_packet_content_hash\"),\n                        shared_content_hash,\n                        errors,\n                    )\n\n    for idx, prompt in enumerate(data.get(\"rendered_prompts\", []) or []):\n        if not isinstance(prompt, dict):\n            continue\n        reviewer = str(prompt.get(\"reviewer\", \"\"))\n        reviewer_def = reviewers.get(reviewer)\n        if not reviewer_def:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: rendered_prompts[{idx}] reviewer not in reviewer_set: {reviewer}\")\n            continue\n        prompt_path = resolve_existing(prompt.get(\"prompt_path\"), f\"rendered_prompts[{idx}].prompt_path\")\n        if prompt_path:\n            compare_hash(path, f\"rendered_prompts[{idx}].prompt_hash\", prompt.get(\"prompt_hash\"), sha256_file(prompt_path), errors)\n        packet_hash = packet_hash_by_reviewer.get(reviewer)\n        if packet_hash:\n            compare_hash(path, f\"rendered_prompts[{idx}].packet_hash\", prompt.get(\"packet_hash\"), packet_hash, errors)\n        if output_schema_hash:\n            compare_hash(path, f\"rendered_prompts[{idx}].schema_hash\", prompt.get(\"schema_hash\"), output_schema_hash, errors)\n        compare_hash(path, f\"rendered_prompts[{idx}].rubric_hash\", prompt.get(\"rubric_hash\"), rubric_hash, errors)\n        role_path = resolve_existing(reviewer_def.get(\"role_contract\"), f\"reviewer_set.{reviewer}.role_contract\")\n        if role_path:\n            compare_hash(path, f\"rendered_prompts[{idx}].role_contract_hash\", prompt.get(\"role_contract_hash\"), sha256_file(role_path), errors)\n        packet_path = packet_path_by_reviewer.get(reviewer)\n        if prompt_path and role_path and packet_path:\n            packet_data = parse_json(packet_path)\n            role_data = parse_yaml(role_path)\n            if isinstance(packet_data, dict) and isinstance(role_data, dict):\n                expected_prompt = _render_expected_review_prompt(packet_data, role_data)\n                actual_prompt = prompt_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n                if actual_prompt != expected_prompt:\n                    errors.append(\n                        f\"{path}: rendered_prompts[{idx}].prompt_path content must match current packet and role contract\"\n                    )\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_review_packet_artifact(root: Path, path: Path, check_references: bool) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    schema = parse_json(root / \"schemas\" / \"review-packet.schema.json\")\n    data = parse_json(path)\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        return [f\"{path}: review packet must be a JSON object\"]\n    errors.extend(validate_against_schema(path, data, schema))\n    context_policy = data.get(\"context_policy\", {}) or {}\n    allowed = set(context_policy.get(\"allowed_context_sources\", []) or [])\n    if allowed != {\"review_packet\", \"referenced_artifacts\"}:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: allowed_context_sources must be exactly review_packet and referenced_artifacts\")\n    composition = str(data.get(\"composition\") or \"\")\n    reviewer_instance = str(data.get(\"reviewer_instance_id\") or \"\")\n    if composition == \"heterogeneous\" and not data.get(\"focus_zone\"):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: heterogeneous review packet must include focus_zone\")\n    if (\n        composition == \"homogeneous-plus-focused\"\n        and data.get(\"reviewer_role\") != \"generalist\"\n        and not data.get(\"focus_zone\")\n    ):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: homogeneous-plus-focused focused reviewer packet must include focus_zone\")\n    _validate_failure_path_matrix_surface_coverage(path, data, errors)\n    if not check_references:\n        return errors\n\n    verification_gate_report = data.get(\"verification_gate_report\")\n    verification_gate_ref = (\n        verification_gate_report.get(\"path\")\n        if isinstance(verification_gate_report, dict)\n        else None\n    )\n    if not verification_gate_ref:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: verification_gate_report.path is required\")\n    _validate_latest_green_gate_ref(root, path, data, errors, verification_gate_ref)\n\n    role_ref = data.get(\"role_contract\")\n    if role_ref:\n        role_ref_text = str(role_ref)\n        if Path(role_ref_text).is_absolute() or \"..\" in Path(role_ref_text).parts:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: role_contract must be a relative non-escaping path\")\n        if not role_ref_text.startswith(ROLE_CONTRACT_PREFIXES):\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: role_contract must resolve to profiles/reviewer_roles\")\n        role_path = root / str(role_ref)\n        if not role_path.exists():\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: role_contract does not exist: {role_ref}\")\n        else:\n            role_data = parse_yaml(role_path) or {}\n            if not isinstance(role_data, dict) or role_data.get(\"kind\") != \"reviewer_role\":\n                errors.append(f\"{path}: role_contract must point to a reviewer_role manifest\")\n            elif role_data.get(\"name\") != data.get(\"reviewer_role\"):\n                errors.append(f\"{path}: reviewer_role must match role_contract.name\")\n    prompt_contract = data.get(\"review_prompt_contract\", {}) or {}\n    prompt_contract_ref = prompt_contract.get(\"path\")\n    if prompt_contract_ref and (Path(str(prompt_contract_ref)).is_absolute() or \"..\" in Path(str(prompt_contract_ref)).parts):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: review_prompt_contract.path must be relative and non-escaping\")\n    prompt_contract_path = root / str(prompt_contract_ref) if prompt_contract_ref else None\n    if prompt_contract_ref and not prompt_contract_path.exists():\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: review_prompt_contract.path does not exist: {prompt_contract_ref}\")\n    elif prompt_contract_ref and prompt_contract_path:\n        contract_schema_ref = prompt_contract.get(\"schema\")\n        if contract_schema_ref and (\n            Path(str(contract_schema_ref)).is_absolute() or \"..\" in Path(str(contract_schema_ref)).parts\n        ):\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: review_prompt_contract.schema must be relative and non-escaping\")\n        contract_schema_path = root / str(contract_schema_ref)\n        if not contract_schema_ref or not contract_schema_path.exists():\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: review_prompt_contract.schema does not exist: {contract_schema_ref}\")\n        else:\n            contract = parse_yaml(prompt_contract_path) or {}\n            if not isinstance(contract, dict):\n                errors.append(f\"{prompt_contract_path}: review prompt contract must be a mapping\")\n            else:\n                contract_schema = parse_json(contract_schema_path)\n                if isinstance(contract_schema, dict):\n                    errors.extend(validate_against_schema(prompt_contract_path, contract, contract_schema))\n                errors.extend(validate_review_prompt_contract_invariants(root, prompt_contract_path, contract, True))\n                contract_verification_gate_ref = ((contract.get(\"inputs\") or {}).get(\"verification_gate_report\"))\n                if not contract_verification_gate_ref:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: review_prompt_contract inputs.verification_gate_report is required\")\n                _validate_latest_green_gate_ref(\n                    root,\n                    path,\n                    data,\n                    errors,\n                    contract_verification_gate_ref,\n                    \"review_prompt_contract inputs.verification_gate_report\",\n                )\n                identity = contract.get(\"identity\", {}) or {}\n                for key in [\"workflow\", \"run_id\", \"review_profile\", \"composition\"]:\n                    if str(identity.get(key)) != str(data.get(key)):\n                        errors.append(f\"{path}: {key} must match review_prompt_contract identity.{key}\")\n                reviewer_id = str(data.get(\"reviewer_instance_id\") or data.get(\"reviewer_role\") or \"\")\n                reviewer_set = contract.get(\"reviewer_set\", []) or []\n                matches = [\n                    reviewer\n                    for reviewer in reviewer_set\n                    if isinstance(reviewer, dict)\n                    and (\n                        reviewer.get(\"instance_id\") == reviewer_id\n                        or (\n                            not data.get(\"reviewer_instance_id\")\n                            and reviewer.get(\"role_id\") == data.get(\"reviewer_role\")\n                        )\n                    )\n                ]\n                if not matches:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: reviewer must exist in review_prompt_contract reviewer_set\")\n                elif matches[0].get(\"role_contract\") != role_ref:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: role_contract must match review_prompt_contract reviewer role_contract\")\n                contract_packets = ((contract.get(\"inputs\") or {}).get(\"review_packets\") or [])\n                packet_matches = [\n                    packet\n                    for packet in contract_packets\n                    if isinstance(packet, dict)\n                    and packet.get(\"reviewer\") == reviewer_id\n                    and (root / str(packet.get(\"path\", \"\"))).resolve() == path.resolve()\n                ]\n                if not packet_matches:\n                    errors.append(\n                        f\"{path}: review packet must be listed in review_prompt_contract inputs.review_packets with matching reviewer and path\"\n                    )\n                elif len(packet_matches) > 1:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: review packet has duplicate entries in review_prompt_contract inputs.review_packets\")\n                elif contract.get(\"artifact_scope\") == \"run\":\n                    compare_hash(\n                        path,\n                        \"review_prompt_contract packet_hash\",\n                        packet_matches[0].get(\"packet_hash\"),\n                        sha256_file(path),\n                        errors,\n                    )\n    output_schema = data.get(\"output_schema\")\n    if output_schema and not (root / str(output_schema)).exists():\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: output_schema does not exist: {output_schema}\")\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_review_prompt_contract_template(root: Path) -> list[str]:\n    schema = parse_json(root / \"schemas\" / \"review-prompt-contract.schema.json\")\n    path = root / \"templates\" / \"review-prompt-contract.yaml\"\n    data = parse_yaml(path) or {}\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        return [f\"{path}: review prompt contract template must be a mapping\"]\n    errors = validate_against_schema(path, data, schema)\n    errors.extend(validate_review_prompt_contract_invariants(root, path, data, False))\n    example = root / \"examples\" / \"external-reviewers\" / \"claude-code\" / \"review-prompt-contract.architecture.yaml\"\n    if example.exists():\n        example_data = parse_yaml(example) or {}\n        if not isinstance(example_data, dict):\n            errors.append(f\"{example}: review prompt contract example must be a mapping\")\n        else:\n            errors.extend(validate_against_schema(example, example_data, schema))\n            errors.extend(validate_review_prompt_contract_invariants(root, example, example_data, True))\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_reviewer_invocation_artifact(root: Path, path: Path) -> list[str]:\n    schema = parse_json(root / \"schemas\" / \"reviewer-invocation.schema.json\")\n    data = parse_json(path)\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        return [f\"{path}: reviewer invocation must be a JSON object\"]\n    return validate_against_schema(path, data, schema)\n\n\ndef validate_review_artifact_preparation_artifact(root: Path, path: Path) -> list[str]:\n    schema = parse_json(root / \"schemas\" / \"review-artifact-preparation.schema.json\")\n    data = parse_json(path)\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        return [f\"{path}: review artifact preparation must be a JSON object\"]\n    return validate_against_schema(path, data, schema)\n\n\ndef validate_evidence_probe_report_artifact(root: Path, path: Path) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    schema = parse_json(root / \"schemas\" / \"evidence-probe-report.schema.json\")\n    data = parse_json(path)\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        return [f\"{path}: evidence probe report must be a JSON object\"]\n    errors.extend(validate_against_schema(path, data, schema))\n    allowed_ids = {\n        str(item.get(\"id\"))\n        for item in data.get(\"allowed_instruments\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"id\")\n    }\n    for idx, command in enumerate(data.get(\"commands_run\", []) or []):\n        if not isinstance(command, dict):\n            continue\n        instrument_id = str(command.get(\"instrument_id\", \"\"))\n        if not instrument_id:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: commands_run[{idx}].instrument_id is required\")\n        elif instrument_id not in allowed_ids:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: commands_run[{idx}].instrument_id is not declared in allowed_instruments: {instrument_id}\"\n            )\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_reviewer_report_artifact(root: Path, path: Path) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    schema = parse_json(root / \"schemas\" / \"reviewer-report.schema.json\")\n    data = parse_json(path)\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        return [f\"{path}: reviewer report must be a JSON object\"]\n    errors.extend(validate_against_schema(path, data, schema))\n    _validate_reviewer_report_normalization(root, path, data, errors)\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_enabled_review_minimum(\n    path: Path,\n    review: object,\n    context: str,\n    reviewer_role_names: set[str] | None = None,\n) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    if not isinstance(review, dict) or not review:\n        return errors\n    topology = review.get(\"topology\")\n    if not topology or topology == \"none\":\n        return errors\n    topology_rules = {\n        \"homogeneous-dual\": (\"homogeneous\", 2, 2),\n        \"homogeneous-plus-focused\": (\"homogeneous-plus-focused\", 3, 8),\n        \"heterogeneous-variable\": (\"heterogeneous\", 3, 8),\n    }\n    expected_rule = topology_rules.get(str(topology))\n    if topology == \"single-reviewer\":\n        errors.append(\n            f\"{path}: {context}.topology single-reviewer is not valid for primary review gates; \"\n            \"enabled review gates require at least two reviewers\"\n        )\n    selected_surfaces = review.get(\"selected_risk_surfaces\")\n    if selected_surfaces is not None:\n        if not isinstance(selected_surfaces, list):\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.selected_risk_surfaces must be a list\")\n        else:\n            blank_surfaces = [\n                str(index)\n                for index, surface in enumerate(selected_surfaces)\n                if isinstance(surface, str) and not surface.strip()\n            ]\n            if blank_surfaces:\n                errors.append(\n                    f\"{path}: {context}.selected_risk_surfaces must not contain blank entries: {', '.join(blank_surfaces)}\"\n                )\n    reviewers = review.get(\"reviewers\")\n    if not isinstance(reviewers, list):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.reviewers must list at least two reviewers when review is enabled\")\n    elif len(reviewers) < 2:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.reviewers must include at least two reviewers when review is enabled\")\n    elif len(reviewers) > 8:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.reviewers must not include more than eight reviewers\")\n    context_policy = review.get(\"context_policy\")\n    if not isinstance(context_policy, dict):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.context_policy is required when review is enabled\")\n    else:\n        if context_policy.get(\"start_mode\") != \"fresh_context\":\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.context_policy.start_mode must be fresh_context\")\n        if context_policy.get(\"fork_conversation_context\") is not False:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.context_policy.fork_conversation_context must be false\")\n\n    composition = review.get(\"composition\")\n    if composition not in {\"homogeneous\", \"homogeneous-plus-focused\", \"heterogeneous\"}:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.composition must be homogeneous, homogeneous-plus-focused or heterogeneous\")\n    if expected_rule:\n        expected_composition, min_count, max_count = expected_rule\n        if composition != expected_composition:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: {context}.composition must be {expected_composition} for topology {topology}\"\n            )\n        if isinstance(reviewers, list) and not (min_count <= len(reviewers) <= max_count):\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: topology {topology} requires {min_count}-{max_count} reviewers\"\n            )\n    prompt_policy = review.get(\"prompt_policy\")\n    if not isinstance(prompt_policy, dict):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy is required when review is enabled\")\n    elif composition == \"homogeneous\":\n        for key in [\"same_prompt\", \"same_packet\", \"same_rubric\", \"same_output_schema\"]:\n            if prompt_policy.get(key) is not True:\n                errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy.{key} must be true for homogeneous review\")\n        if isinstance(reviewers, list) and len(reviewers) != 2:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: homogeneous review must use exactly two reviewers\")\n    elif composition == \"homogeneous-plus-focused\":\n        if isinstance(reviewers, list) and len(reviewers) < 3:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: homogeneous-plus-focused review must use at least three reviewers\")\n        if isinstance(reviewers, list):\n            baseline_missing = sorted({\"generalist-a\", \"generalist-b\"} - set(str(item) for item in reviewers))\n            if baseline_missing:\n                errors.append(f\"{path}: homogeneous-plus-focused missing baseline reviewers: {', '.join(baseline_missing)}\")\n        if prompt_policy.get(\"baseline_same_prompt\") is not True:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy.baseline_same_prompt must be true\")\n        if prompt_policy.get(\"focused_reviewers_require_explicit_focus_zone\") is not True:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy.focused_reviewers_require_explicit_focus_zone must be true\"\n            )\n        if prompt_policy.get(\"focus_zones_may_overlap\") is not True:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy.focus_zones_may_overlap must be true\")\n        if prompt_policy.get(\"all_reviewers_must_report_p0_p1_outside_focus\") is not True:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy.all_reviewers_must_report_p0_p1_outside_focus must be true\"\n            )\n    elif composition == \"heterogeneous\":\n        if isinstance(reviewers, list) and len(reviewers) < 3:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: heterogeneous review must use at least three reviewers\")\n        if isinstance(reviewers, list) and len(reviewers) > 8:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: heterogeneous review must use no more than eight reviewers\")\n        if prompt_policy.get(\"focus_prompts_required\") is not True:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy.focus_prompts_required must be true\")\n        if prompt_policy.get(\"focus_zones_may_overlap\") is not True:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy.focus_zones_may_overlap must be true\")\n        if prompt_policy.get(\"all_reviewers_must_report_p0_p1_outside_focus\") is not True:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: {context}.prompt_policy.all_reviewers_must_report_p0_p1_outside_focus must be true\"\n            )\n\n    focus_zones = review.get(\"focus_zones\", []) or []\n    if composition in {\"homogeneous-plus-focused\", \"heterogeneous\"}:\n        if not isinstance(focus_zones, list) or not focus_zones:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.focus_zones must list explicit focused reviewer roles\")\n        elif reviewer_role_names is not None:\n            reviewer_names = set(str(item) for item in reviewers) if isinstance(reviewers, list) else set()\n            seen_focus_reviewers: set[str] = set()\n            for idx, zone in enumerate(focus_zones):\n                if not isinstance(zone, dict):\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.focus_zones[{idx}] must be a mapping\")\n                    continue\n                reviewer = str(zone.get(\"reviewer\", \"\"))\n                if reviewer in seen_focus_reviewers:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.focus_zones[{idx}] duplicates reviewer: {reviewer}\")\n                seen_focus_reviewers.add(reviewer)\n                if reviewer and reviewer not in reviewer_names:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.focus_zones[{idx}] references non-reviewer: {reviewer}\")\n                role = zone.get(\"role\")\n                if role not in reviewer_role_names:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.focus_zones[{idx}] references unknown reviewer role: {role}\")\n                if not zone.get(\"primary_focus\"):\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: {context}.focus_zones[{idx}] must include primary_focus\")\n            if composition == \"heterogeneous\" and reviewer_names != seen_focus_reviewers:\n                missing = sorted(reviewer_names - seen_focus_reviewers)\n                extra = sorted(seen_focus_reviewers - reviewer_names)\n                if missing:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: heterogeneous focus_zones missing reviewers: {', '.join(missing)}\")\n                if extra:\n                    errors.append(f\"{path}: heterogeneous focus_zones include non-reviewers: {', '.join(extra)}\")\n            if composition == \"homogeneous-plus-focused\":\n                baseline = {\"generalist-a\", \"generalist-b\"}\n                focused_reviewers = reviewer_names - baseline\n                missing = sorted(focused_reviewers - seen_focus_reviewers)\n                if missing:\n                    errors.append(\n                        f\"{path}: homogeneous-plus-focused focus_zones missing focused reviewers: {', '.join(missing)}\"\n                    )\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_supported_review_topologies(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    supported = ((data.get(\"supported_profiles\") or {}).get(\"review_topologies\") or [])\n    if not isinstance(supported, list):\n        return errors\n    if \"single-reviewer\" in supported:\n        errors.append(\n            f\"{path}: supported_profiles.review_topologies must not include single-reviewer; \"\n            \"primary review gates require two or more reviewers\"\n        )\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_v02_review_control_phase_policy(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    if data.get(\"name\") not in V0_2_REVIEW_CONTROL_WORKFLOWS:\n        return []\n    has_review_phase = any(\n        isinstance(phase, dict) and phase.get(\"kind\") == \"review\"\n        for phase in data.get(\"phases\", []) or []\n    )\n    if has_review_phase and not isinstance(data.get(\"review\"), dict):\n        return [f\"{path}: v0.2 review-control workflow with review phase must declare top-level review policy\"]\n    return []\n\n\ndef validate_required_review_gate_order(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    required_by_workflow = {\n        \"big-feature-contract-first\": {\n            \"review_phase\": \"review\",\n            \"gate_phase\": \"verification_gate\",\n            \"gate\": \"verification_gate\",\n        },\n        \"review-only-fusion\": {\n            \"review_phase\": \"independent_review\",\n            \"gate_phase\": \"evidence_gate\",\n            \"gate\": \"evidence_gate\",\n        },\n    }\n    rule = required_by_workflow.get(str(data.get(\"name\")))\n    if not rule:\n        return []\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    phases = [\n        phase\n        for phase in data.get(\"phases\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(phase, dict)\n    ]\n    phase_by_id = {str(phase.get(\"id\")): phase for phase in phases if phase.get(\"id\")}\n    gate_phase = phase_by_id.get(rule[\"gate_phase\"])\n    review_phase = phase_by_id.get(rule[\"review_phase\"])\n    if not gate_phase:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: workflow {data.get('name')} must include phase {rule['gate_phase']}\")\n    else:\n        if gate_phase.get(\"kind\") not in {\"verification\", \"gate\"}:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: phase {rule['gate_phase']} must be kind verification or gate\")\n        if gate_phase.get(\"gate\") != rule[\"gate\"]:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: phase {rule['gate_phase']} must bind gate {rule['gate']}\")\n    if not review_phase:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: workflow {data.get('name')} must include review phase {rule['review_phase']}\")\n    else:\n        if review_phase.get(\"kind\") != \"review\":\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: phase {rule['review_phase']} must be kind review\")\n        runs_after = set(str(item) for item in review_phase.get(\"runs_after\", []) or [])\n        if rule[\"gate_phase\"] not in runs_after:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: review phase {rule['review_phase']} must run after {rule['gate_phase']}\"\n            )\n    if gate_phase and review_phase:\n        gate_index = phases.index(gate_phase)\n        review_index = phases.index(review_phase)\n        if gate_index >= review_index:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: phase {rule['gate_phase']} must appear before {rule['review_phase']}\")\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_review_fusion_validation_order(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    if data.get(\"name\") not in V0_2_REVIEW_CONTROL_WORKFLOWS:\n        return errors\n    phases = [\n        phase\n        for phase in data.get(\"phases\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(phase, dict)\n    ]\n    validation_phase = next((phase for phase in phases if phase.get(\"kind\") == \"finding_validation\"), None)\n    review_phases = [phase for phase in phases if phase.get(\"kind\") == \"review\"]\n    post_gate_review_phases = [\n        phase for phase in review_phases if phase.get(\"runs_after\")\n    ]\n    fusion_phases = [phase for phase in phases if phase.get(\"kind\") == \"fusion\"]\n    if not post_gate_review_phases and not fusion_phases:\n        return errors\n    if validation_phase is None:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: v0.2 review-control workflow with post-gate review or fusion must include finding_validation phase\")\n        return errors\n    if fusion_phases and not review_phases:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: v0.2 review-control workflow with fusion phase must include review phase\")\n        return errors\n    first_review_index = min(phases.index(phase) for phase in (post_gate_review_phases or review_phases))\n    validation_index = phases.index(validation_phase)\n    validation_runs_after = set(str(item) for item in validation_phase.get(\"runs_after\", []) or [])\n    if fusion_phases:\n        first_fusion_index = min(phases.index(phase) for phase in fusion_phases)\n        if not (first_review_index < first_fusion_index < validation_index):\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: review/fusion/validation order must be review -> fusion -> finding_validation\")\n        fusion_ids = {str(phase.get(\"id\")) for phase in fusion_phases if phase.get(\"id\")}\n        if fusion_ids and not (fusion_ids & validation_runs_after):\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: finding_validation phase must run after fusion\")\n    else:\n        if not (first_review_index < validation_index):\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: review/validation order must be review -> finding_validation\")\n        review_ids_for_validation = {str(phase.get(\"id\")) for phase in post_gate_review_phases if phase.get(\"id\")}\n        if review_ids_for_validation and not (review_ids_for_validation & validation_runs_after):\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: finding_validation phase must run after review\")\n    review_ids = {str(phase.get(\"id\")) for phase in review_phases if phase.get(\"id\")}\n    for fusion in fusion_phases:\n        runs_after = set(str(item) for item in fusion.get(\"runs_after\", []) or [])\n        if review_ids and not (review_ids & runs_after):\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: fusion phase {fusion.get('id')} must run after review\")\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_v02_review_control_materiality_policy(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    if data.get(\"name\") not in V0_2_REVIEW_CONTROL_WORKFLOWS:\n        return errors\n    review_cycle = data.get(\"review_cycle\")\n    if not isinstance(review_cycle, dict):\n        return errors\n    do_not_rerun = set(str(item) for item in review_cycle.get(\"do_not_rerun_on\", []) or [])\n    if \"nonblocking_findings_only\" in do_not_rerun:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: do_not_rerun_on must not use ambiguous nonblocking_findings_only\")\n    if \"nonblocking_findings_with_non_material_fixes_only\" not in do_not_rerun:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: do_not_rerun_on must include nonblocking_findings_with_non_material_fixes_only\")\n    materiality = review_cycle.get(\"materiality_classification\")\n    if not isinstance(materiality, dict):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: review_cycle.materiality_classification is required\")\n        return errors\n    if materiality.get(\"required_after_review_fixes\") is not True:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: materiality_classification.required_after_review_fixes must be true\")\n    if materiality.get(\"material_triggers_take_precedence_over_do_not_rerun\") is not True:\n        errors.append(\n            f\"{path}: materiality_classification.material_triggers_take_precedence_over_do_not_rerun must be true\"\n        )\n    if not materiality.get(\"material_if_changes\"):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: materiality_classification.material_if_changes is required\")\n    else:\n        material_if_changes = set(str(item) for item in materiality.get(\"material_if_changes\", []) or [])\n        required_material_triggers = {\n            \"selected_risk_surfaces_or_failure_path_matrix\",\n            \"review_packet_content\",\n        }\n        missing_triggers = sorted(required_material_triggers - material_if_changes)\n        if missing_triggers:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: materiality_classification.material_if_changes missing: {', '.join(missing_triggers)}\"\n            )\n    if not materiality.get(\"non_material_if_only\"):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: materiality_classification.non_material_if_only is required\")\n    controls = data.get(\"review_control_rules\", {}) or {}\n    if controls.get(\"post_fix_materiality_classification_required\") is not True:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: review_control_rules.post_fix_materiality_classification_required must be true\")\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_phase_skills_declared(\n    path: Path,\n    data: dict,\n    skill_manifests: dict[str, dict],\n) -> list[str]:\n    workflow_name = str(data.get(\"name\") or \"\")\n    uses_skills = set(str(item) for item in ((data.get(\"uses\") or {}).get(\"skills\", []) or []))\n    missing: set[str] = set()\n    incompatible: set[str] = set()\n    unknown: set[str] = set()\n    for phase in data.get(\"phases\", []) or []:\n        if not isinstance(phase, dict):\n            continue\n        for skill in phase.get(\"skills\", []) or []:\n            skill_name = str(skill)\n            if skill_name not in skill_manifests:\n                unknown.add(skill_name)\n                continue\n            if skill_name not in uses_skills:\n                missing.add(skill_name)\n            compatible = skill_manifests.get(skill_name, {}).get(\"compatible_workflows\") or []\n            if compatible and workflow_name not in compatible:\n                incompatible.add(skill_name)\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    if missing:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: phase skills missing from uses.skills: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}\")\n    if unknown:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: phase skills missing skill manifest: {', '.join(sorted(unknown))}\")\n    if incompatible:\n        errors.append(\n            f\"{path}: phase skills compatible_workflows missing {workflow_name}: {', '.join(sorted(incompatible))}\"\n        )\n    return errors\n"
    },

      "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .behavior_bindings import validate_behavior_binding, validate_behavior_binding_gate_refs\nfrom .collect import (\n    collect_active_review_topologies,\n    collect_gate_manifests,\n    collect_names,\n    collect_script_names,\n    collect_yaml_manifest_names,\n)\nfrom .common import (\n    parse_json,\n    parse_yaml,\n    validate_against_schema,\n    validate_no_duplicate_yaml_keys,\n    workflow_schema,\n)\nfrom .external_reviewers import validate_external_review_provider\nfrom .gates import validate_gate_manifest\nfrom .project_initialization import (\n    validate_project_assessment_synthesis_artifact,\n    validate_project_initialization_expert_assessment_contract,\n    validate_project_documentation_disposition_artifact,\n    validate_project_initialization_human_interaction,\n    validate_project_initialization_intent_mode_policy,\n    validate_project_initialization_operating_decisions,\n    validate_project_onboarding_assessment_skill_contract,\n)\nfrom .project_overlay import validate_project_overlay_example\nfrom .pr_merge_readiness import validate_pr_merge_readiness_report\nfrom .required_files import REQUIRED_FILES\nfrom .review import (\n    validate_enabled_review_minimum,\n    validate_evidence_probe_report_artifact,\n    validate_required_review_gate_order,\n    validate_review_fusion_validation_order,\n    validate_review_manifest_collection,\n    validate_review_packet_artifact,\n    validate_review_prompt_contract_invariants,\n    validate_review_prompt_contract_run_references,\n    validate_review_prompt_contract_template,\n    validate_phase_skills_declared,\n    validate_review_artifact_preparation_artifact,\n    validate_reviewer_invocation_artifact,\n    validate_reviewer_report_artifact,\n    validate_supported_review_topologies,\n    validate_upstream_review_cycle_policy,\n    validate_v02_review_control_materiality_policy,\n    validate_v02_review_control_phase_policy,\n)\nfrom .workflow_runs import validate_workflow_run_artifact\nfrom .workflows import (\n    validate_big_feature_plan_gate_policy,\n    validate_phase_scripts_declared,\n    validate_test_framed_implementation,\n    validate_workflow_default_strictness,\n)\n\n\ndef validate_repository(root: Path) -> list[str]:\n    root = root.resolve()\n    errors: list[str] = []\n\n    for p in root.rglob('*.yaml'):\n        errors.extend(validate_no_duplicate_yaml_keys(p))\n        try:\n            parse_yaml(p)\n        except ValueError as exc:\n            errors.append(str(exc))\n    for p in root.rglob('*.yml'):\n        errors.extend(validate_no_duplicate_yaml_keys(p))\n        try:\n            parse_yaml(p)\n        except ValueError as exc:\n            errors.append(str(exc))\n    for p in root.rglob('*.json'):\n        try:\n            parse_json(p)\n        except ValueError as exc:\n            errors.append(str(exc))\n\n    skills = collect_names(root, 'skills', 'skill.yaml')\n    skill_manifests = {\n        path.parent.name: parse_yaml(path) or {}\n        for path in (root / 'skills').glob('*/skill.yaml')\n    }\n    packs = collect_names(root, 'packs', 'pack.yaml')\n    scripts = collect_script_names(root)\n    templates = {p.name for p in (root / 'templates').glob('*') if p.is_file()}\n    topologies = collect_active_review_topologies(root)\n    reviewer_role_names = collect_yaml_manifest_names(root, 'profiles/reviewer_roles')\n    gates = collect_gate_manifests(root)\n    wf_schema = workflow_schema(root)\n    project_schema = parse_json(root / 'schemas' / 'project-binding.schema.json')\n    workflow_binding_schema = parse_json(root / 'schemas' / 'workflow-binding.schema.json')\n\n    for gate_path in gates.values():\n        errors.extend(validate_gate_manifest(root, gate_path))\n\n    for binding in root.rglob('*.bindings.yaml'):\n        errors.extend(validate_behavior_binding(binding))\n        errors.extend(validate_behavior_binding_gate_refs(binding, set(gates.keys())))\n\n    errors.extend(validate_review_manifest_collection(root))\n    errors.extend(validate_review_prompt_contract_template(root))\n    errors.extend(validate_review_packet_artifact(root, root / 'templates' / 'review-packet.json', False))\n    errors.extend(validate_evidence_probe_report_artifact(root, root / 'templates' / 'evidence-probe-report.json'))\n    errors.extend(\n        validate_review_packet_artifact(\n            root,\n            root / 'examples' / 'external-reviewers' / 'claude-code' / 'review-packet.architecture.json',\n            True,\n        )\n    )\n    errors.extend(validate_reviewer_invocation_artifact(root, root / 'templates' / 'reviewer-invocation.json'))\n    errors.extend(validate_review_artifact_preparation_artifact(root, root / 'templates' / 'review-artifact-preparation.json'))\n    errors.extend(\n        validate_reviewer_invocation_artifact(\n            root,\n            root / 'examples' / 'external-reviewers' / 'claude-code' / 'reviewer-invocation.claude-architecture.json',\n        )\n    )\n    errors.extend(validate_workflow_run_artifact(root, root / 'templates' / 'workflow-run.yaml'))\n    documentation_disposition_paths = {\n        root / 'templates' / 'project-documentation-disposition.yaml',\n        *root.glob('examples/**/project-documentation-disposition.yaml'),\n        *root.glob('Docs/agentsflow/runs/**/project-documentation-disposition.yaml'),\n        *root.glob('run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/**/project-documentation-disposition.yaml'),\n        *root.glob('examples/**/Docs/agentsflow/runs/**/project-documentation-disposition.yaml'),\n    }\n    for documentation_disposition in sorted(documentation_disposition_paths):\n        errors.extend(validate_project_documentation_disposition_artifact(root, documentation_disposition))\n    errors.extend(validate_project_onboarding_assessment_skill_contract(root))\n    errors.extend(\n        validate_project_assessment_synthesis_artifact(\n            root,\n            root / 'examples' / 'project-initialization' / 'project-assessment.json',\n        )\n    )\n    run_artifact_patterns = [\n        'Docs/agentsflow/runs/*/run.yaml',\n        'run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/*/run.yaml',\n        'examples/**/Docs/agentsflow/runs/*/run.yaml',\n    ]\n    for run_artifact in {\n        path\n        for pattern in run_artifact_patterns\n        for path in root.glob(pattern)\n    }:\n        errors.extend(validate_workflow_run_artifact(root, run_artifact))\n    review_prompt_contract_patterns = [\n        'Docs/agentsflow/runs/*/review-prompt-contract.yaml',\n        'run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/*/review-prompt-contract.yaml',\n        'examples/**/Docs/agentsflow/runs/*/review-prompt-contract.yaml',\n    ]\n    for prompt_contract in sorted({\n        path\n        for pattern in review_prompt_contract_patterns\n        for path in root.glob(pattern)\n    }):\n        schema = parse_json(root / 'schemas' / 'review-prompt-contract.schema.json')\n        data = parse_yaml(prompt_contract) or {}\n        if not isinstance(data, dict):\n            errors.append(f'{prompt_contract}: review prompt contract must be a mapping')\n        else:\n            errors.extend(validate_against_schema(prompt_contract, data, schema))\n            errors.extend(validate_review_prompt_contract_invariants(root, prompt_contract, data, True))\n            errors.extend(validate_review_prompt_contract_run_references(root, prompt_contract, data))\n    review_packet_patterns = [\n        'Docs/agentsflow/runs/*/review-packets/*.json',\n        'run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/*/review-packets/*.json',\n        'examples/**/Docs/agentsflow/runs/*/review-packets/*.json',\n    ]\n    for review_packet in sorted({\n        path\n        for pattern in review_packet_patterns\n        for path in root.glob(pattern)\n    }):\n        if review_packet.name in {'shared-content.json', 'shared-source.json'}:\n            continue\n        errors.extend(validate_review_packet_artifact(root, review_packet, True))\n    for reviewer_report in root.glob('examples/**/Docs/agentsflow/runs/*/reviewer-report*.json'):\n        errors.extend(validate_reviewer_report_artifact(root, reviewer_report))\n    for probe_report in root.glob('examples/**/Docs/agentsflow/runs/*/evidence-probe-report*.json'):\n        errors.extend(validate_evidence_probe_report_artifact(root, probe_report))\n\n    for provider_config in list(root.rglob('external-review-provider.yaml')) + list(root.rglob('claude-code.yaml')):\n        errors.extend(validate_external_review_provider(provider_config))\n\n    for report_path in sorted(root.glob('examples/pr-merge-readiness/**/pr-merge-readiness-report.json')):\n        errors.extend(validate_pr_merge_readiness_report(root, report_path))\n\n    for rel in REQUIRED_FILES:\n        if not (root / rel).exists():\n            errors.append(f'missing required file: {rel}')\n    if not (root / 'docs' / 'enforcement-boundary.md').exists():\n        errors.append('missing required file: docs/enforcement-boundary.md')\n\n    for project_rel in [\n        'examples/project-overlay',\n        'examples/e2e/minimal-python-project',\n    ]:\n        errors.extend(\n            validate_project_overlay_example(\n                root,\n                project_rel,\n                project_schema,\n                workflow_binding_schema,\n                reviewer_role_names,\n            )\n        )\n\n    for wf in (root / 'workflows').glob('*/workflow.yaml'):\n        data = parse_yaml(wf) or {}\n        if not isinstance(data, dict):\n            errors.append(f'Workflow {wf} is not a mapping')\n            continue\n        errors.extend(validate_against_schema(wf, data, wf_schema))\n        errors.extend(validate_workflow_default_strictness(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_test_framed_implementation(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_project_initialization_operating_decisions(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_project_initialization_human_interaction(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_project_initialization_expert_assessment_contract(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_project_initialization_intent_mode_policy(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_big_feature_plan_gate_policy(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_supported_review_topologies(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_upstream_review_cycle_policy(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_v02_review_control_phase_policy(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_required_review_gate_order(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_review_fusion_validation_order(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_v02_review_control_materiality_policy(wf, data))\n        errors.extend(validate_phase_skills_declared(wf, data, skill_manifests))\n        errors.extend(validate_phase_scripts_declared(wf, data))\n        uses = data.get('uses', {}) or {}\n        for s in uses.get('skills', []) or []:\n            if s not in skills:\n                errors.append(f'{wf}: missing skill reference: {s}')\n            else:\n                compatible = skill_manifests.get(s, {}).get('compatible_workflows') or []\n                if compatible and data.get('name') not in compatible:\n                    errors.append(\n                        f'{wf}: skill {s} compatible_workflows missing {data.get(\"name\")}'\n                    )\n        for s in uses.get('scripts', []) or []:\n            if s not in scripts:\n                errors.append(f'{wf}: missing script reference: {s}')\n        for t in uses.get('templates', []) or []:\n            if t not in templates:\n                errors.append(f'{wf}: missing template reference: {t}')\n        for pack in uses.get('packs', []) or []:\n            if pack not in packs:\n                errors.append(f'{wf}: missing pack reference: {pack}')\n        review = data.get('review', {}) or {}\n        topology = review.get('topology')\n        if topology and topology not in topologies:\n            errors.append(f'{wf}: unknown review topology: {topology}')\n        errors.extend(validate_enabled_review_minimum(wf, review, 'review', reviewer_role_names))\n        for gid in review.get('gates', []) or []:\n            if gid not in gates:\n                errors.append(f'{wf}: missing gate manifest referenced by review.gates: {gid}')\n        for phase in data.get('phases', []) or []:\n            if not isinstance(phase, dict):\n                continue\n            gid = phase.get('gate')\n            if gid and gid not in gates:\n                errors.append(f'{wf}: phase {phase.get(\"id\", phase.get(\"name\"))} references missing gate: {gid}')\n            if (phase.get('kind') in {'gate', 'verification'} or str(phase.get('id', '')).endswith('_gate')) and not gid:\n                errors.append(f'{wf}: gate/verification phase {phase.get(\"id\", phase.get(\"name\"))} must declare a gate manifest via \\'gate:\\'')\n            if phase.get('kind') == 'review' and phase.get('scripts'):\n                errors.append(f'{wf}: review phase {phase.get(\"id\", phase.get(\"name\"))} must not list scripts; use tool exceptions only if explicitly declared')\n\n    return errors\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    ap.add_argument('--root', default='.', help='repository root')\n    args = ap.parse_args()\n    errors = validate_repository(Path(args.root))\n    if errors:\n       "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\ndef validate_workflow_default_strictness(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    default = data.get(\"default_strictness\")\n    supported = {\n        str(item)\n        for item in ((data.get(\"supported_profiles\") or {}).get(\"strictness\") or [])\n    }\n    if default is None:\n        return [f\"{path}: workflow must declare default_strictness\"]\n    if supported and str(default) not in supported:\n        return [\n            f\"{path}: default_strictness {default} must be listed in supported_profiles.strictness\"\n        ]\n    return []\n\n\ndef validate_test_framed_implementation(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    phases = data.get(\"phases\", []) or []\n    if not isinstance(phases, list):\n        return errors\n    for idx, phase in enumerate(phases):\n        if not isinstance(phase, dict) or phase.get(\"kind\") != \"implementation\":\n            continue\n        phase_id = phase.get(\"id\", phase.get(\"name\", f\"#{idx}\"))\n        previous = phases[idx - 1] if idx > 0 and isinstance(phases[idx - 1], dict) else {}\n        following = phases[idx + 1] if idx + 1 < len(phases) and isinstance(phases[idx + 1], dict) else {}\n        valid_previous = previous.get(\"test_framing\") == \"red_capture\" or (\n            previous.get(\"test_framing\") == \"baseline_capture\"\n            and phase.get(\"change_type\") == \"refactor\"\n        )\n        if not valid_previous:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: implementation phase {phase_id} must be immediately preceded by a red-capture phase \"\n                \"with test_framing: red_capture, or by baseline_capture for change_type: refactor\"\n            )\n        elif previous.get(\"kind\") not in {\"verification\", \"gate\"} or not previous.get(\"gate\"):\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: implementation phase {phase_id} framing phase must be verification/gate with gate\"\n            )\n        if following.get(\"test_framing\") != \"green_verify\":\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: implementation phase {phase_id} must be immediately followed by a green-verify phase \"\n                \"with test_framing: green_verify\"\n            )\n        elif following.get(\"kind\") not in {\"verification\", \"gate\"} or not following.get(\"gate\"):\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: implementation phase {phase_id} green phase must be verification/gate with gate\"\n            )\n        elif previous.get(\"gate\") and following.get(\"gate\") and previous.get(\"gate\") != following.get(\"gate\"):\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: implementation phase {phase_id} framing phases should use the same gate\"\n            )\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_big_feature_plan_gate_policy(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    if data.get(\"name\") != \"big-feature-contract-first\":\n        return errors\n    phases = [\n        phase\n        for phase in data.get(\"phases\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(phase, dict)\n    ]\n    phase_by_id = {str(phase.get(\"id\")): phase for phase in phases if phase.get(\"id\")}\n    technical_plan = phase_by_id.get(\"technical_plan\")\n    plan_gate = phase_by_id.get(\"plan_gate\")\n    contract_acceptance = phase_by_id.get(\"contract_acceptance\")\n    red_capture = phase_by_id.get(\"red_capture\")\n    allowed_pause_phases = {\n        str(item)\n        for item in ((data.get(\"human_interaction\") or {}).get(\"allowed_pause_phases\") or [])\n    }\n    required_plan_artifacts = {\n        \"repository-grounding-report.md\",\n        \"plan.md\",\n        \"task-breakdown.md\",\n        \"decision-contract.md\",\n    }\n    if not isinstance(technical_plan, dict):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: big-feature-contract-first must include technical_plan phase before plan_gate\")\n    else:\n        outputs = set(str(item) for item in technical_plan.get(\"outputs\", []) or [])\n        missing_outputs = sorted(required_plan_artifacts - outputs)\n        if missing_outputs:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: technical_plan phase missing outputs: {', '.join(missing_outputs)}\")\n        applies = set(str(item) for item in technical_plan.get(\"applies_to_strictness\", []) or [])\n        if applies != {\"L3\", \"L4\"}:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: technical_plan phase must apply exactly to effective strictness values L3 and L4\")\n    if not isinstance(plan_gate, dict):\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: big-feature-contract-first must include plan_gate phase\")\n    else:\n        if plan_gate.get(\"kind\") not in {\"gate\", \"verification\"}:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: plan_gate phase must be kind gate or verification\")\n        if plan_gate.get(\"gate\") != \"plan_gate\":\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: plan_gate phase must bind gate plan_gate\")\n        applies = set(str(item) for item in plan_gate.get(\"applies_to_strictness\", []) or [])\n        if applies != {\"L3\", \"L4\"}:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: plan_gate phase must apply exactly to effective strictness values L3 and L4\")\n        runs_after = set(str(item) for item in plan_gate.get(\"runs_after\", []) or [])\n        if \"technical_plan\" not in runs_after:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: plan_gate phase must run after technical_plan\")\n    if not isinstance(contract_acceptance, dict):\n        errors.append(\n            f\"{path}: big-feature-contract-first must include contract_acceptance phase\"\n        )\n    else:\n        if contract_acceptance.get(\"kind\") != \"decision\":\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: contract_acceptance phase must be kind decision\")\n        human_interaction = contract_acceptance.get(\"human_interaction\", {}) or {}\n        if human_interaction.get(\"required\") is not True:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: contract_acceptance human_interaction.required must be true\"\n            )\n        runs_after = set(str(item) for item in contract_acceptance.get(\"runs_after\", []) or [])\n        if \"plan_gate\" not in runs_after:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: contract_acceptance phase must run after plan_gate\")\n        if \"contract_acceptance\" not in allowed_pause_phases:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: human_interaction.allowed_pause_phases must include contract_acceptance\"\n            )\n        decision_review = contract_acceptance.get(\"decision_review_contract\")\n        if not isinstance(decision_review, dict):\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: contract_acceptance must declare decision_review_contract\"\n            )\n        else:\n            if decision_review.get(\"artifact\") != \"decision-contract.md\":\n                errors.append(\n                    f\"{path}: contract_acceptance decision_review_contract.artifact must be decision-contract.md\"\n                )\n            if decision_review.get(\"required_before_prompt\") is not True:\n                errors.append(\n                    f\"{path}: contract_acceptance decision_review_contract.required_before_prompt must be true\"\n                )\n            required_sections = {\n                \"open_decision_inventory\",\n                \"per_decision_options\",\n                \"tradeoffs\",\n                \"recommended_path\",\n                \"rationale\",\n                \"human_acceptance_question\",\n            }\n            declared_sections = {\n                str(item) for item in decision_review.get(\"required_sections\", []) or []\n            }\n            missing_sections = sorted(required_sections - declared_sections)\n            if missing_sections:\n                errors.append(\n                    f\"{path}: contract_acceptance decision_review_contract missing required_sections: \"\n                    + \", \".join(missing_sections)\n                )\n    if isinstance(red_capture, dict):\n        runs_after = set(str(item) for item in red_capture.get(\"runs_after\", []) or [])\n        if \"plan_gate\" not in runs_after:\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: red_capture phase must run after plan_gate\")\n        if \"contract_acceptance\" not in runs_after:\n            errors.append(\n                f\"{path}: red_capture phase must run after contract_acceptance\"\n            )\n        if red_capture.get(\"runs_after_policy\") != \"after_applicable_strictness_gate\":\n            errors.append(f\"{path}: red_capture phase must use after_applicable_strictness_gate runs_after_policy\")\n    review_gates = set(str(item) for item in ((data.get(\"review\", {}) or {}).get(\"gates\", []) or []))\n    if \"plan_gate\" not in review_gates:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: review.gates must include plan_gate\")\n    concrete_gates = set(str(item) for item in data.get(\"concrete_gates\", []) or [])\n    if \"plan_gate\" not in concrete_gates:\n        errors.append(f\"{path}: concrete_gates must include plan_gate\")\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_phase_scripts_declared(path: Path, data: dict) -> list[str]:\n    uses_scripts = set((data.get(\"uses\") or {}).get(\"scripts\", []) or [])\n    missing: set[str] = set()\n    for phase in data.get(\"phases\", []) or []:\n        if not isinstance(phase, dict):\n            continue\n        for script in phase.get(\"scripts\", []) or []:\n            if script not in uses_scripts:\n                missing.add(str(script))\n    if missing:\n        return [f\"{path}: phase scripts missing from uses.scripts: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}\"]\n    return []\n"
    },

      "path": "scripts/reviewers/README.md",
      "size_bytes": 5176,
      "content": "# External Reviewer Scripts\n\nThis directory contains the MVP external reviewer wrapper.\n\n## v0.2 MVP provider\n\n`run_external_reviewer.py` supports the first MVP provider:\n\n```text\nprovider: claude-code\nmode: subscription-local Claude Code CLI only\nAPI-key usage: forbidden\n```\n\nThe wrapper:\n\n- loads provider config;\n- validates subscription-local-only policy;\n- fails if configured forbidden Claude API/proxy environment variables are present in the process environment or Claude settings files;\n- invokes Claude Code with the v0.2 default `--model opus --effort max` unless the config already resolves to those defaults;\n- requires `execution.sandbox_mode: require_escalated` when launched from Codex\n  so Claude Code can access subscription-local auth/keychain state;\n- requires `execution.permission_mode: default`; Claude Code plan mode is not\n  used for reviewer gates because the wrapper must receive schema-bound JSON on\n  stdout, not a Claude-managed plan artifact;\n- invokes Claude Code with `--tools \"\"` so the reviewer is packet-bound and\n  cannot read files outside the prepared review packet;\n- loads a review packet;\n- invokes the provider or a mock response for smoke testing;\n- normalizes raw provider output into `reviewer-report.json`;\n- stores raw output and invocation metadata, including requested model/effort,\n  provider-reported model usage and normalization trace when available.\n\nReviewer prompts prioritize substantive review quality over native JSON\nserialization. A successful gate still requires a normalized\n`reviewer-report.json`: if a provider returns clear Markdown/text that the\nwrapper cannot auto-normalize, the raw output is evidence for explicit\norchestrator normalization or a rerun, not completed gate evidence.\nThe reviewer report records the normalization source path/hash. The normalized\nreport output hash is stored in invocation metadata, not inside the report.\n\n## Review-set runner\n\n`run_review_set.py` reads a `review-prompt-contract.yaml` with\n`reviewer_assignments`.\n\nIt is a thin gate dispatcher, not a general multi-provider runtime:\n\n- internal assignments must already have a normalized reviewer report artifact;\n- `claude-code` assignments are invoked through `run_external_reviewer.py`;\n- external assignments are started asynchronously before the runner waits for\n  their results, so two Claude reviewers in one set are dispatched without\n  serial blocking;\n- `--internal-report-wait-seconds` may be used when internal read-only\n  reviewers are launched alongside external providers and their report-present\n  artifacts may appear shortly after dispatch;\n- assignment coverage and provider/model diversity policy are checked before\n  dispatch;\n- the runner writes a review invocation set evidence JSON summarizing provider,\n  model family, report paths and invocation metadata.\n\nThe review prompt contract is the dispatch plan. The invocation set is the\ncompleted-run evidence that repo validation can use to prove assigned reviewers\nactually produced normalized reports.\nThe runner records `runner_scheduling: external-first-async` in invocation-set\nevidence for this scheduling mode.\nFor assignment-enabled gates, `inputs.review_invocation_set` must be declared\nin the contract before dispatch and `run_review_set.py --output` must match\nthat path. Run-scope provider-assigned gates also declare\n`inputs.artifact_preparation_report`, written by\n`prepare_review_set_artifacts.py`, before reviewers are invoked.\nThe runner fails before dispatch if this run-scope preparation artifact is\nmissing, does not exist, is not a completed `review_artifact_preparation`\nartifact, or no longer matches the current review prompt contract. Completed\ninvocation-set evidence records both the preparation path and\n`artifact_preparation_report_hash`.\nMock responses are for smoke tests only. External reviewer evidence used to\nclose a run must carry `execution_mode: real`.\nCompleted external evidence must also have `exit_code: 0` and invocation hashes\nthat match the current review packet, rendered prompt, prompt contract, role\ncontract, rubric, output schema, raw provider output and normalized reviewer\nreport.\nWhen model diversity is required, completed diversity is derived from evidence:\ninternal reports must declare `reviewer.model`, and Claude assignments use\n`requested_model` plus provider-reported model usage from invocation metadata.\n\nExample mixed-provider smoke:\n\n```bash\npython3 scripts/reviewers/run_review_set.py \\\n  --contract Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/review-prompt-contract.yaml \\\n  --output Docs/agentsflow/runs/<run-id>/review-invocation-set.json\n```\n\n## Smoke test without Claude\n\n```bash\npython3 scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py \\\n  --provider claude-code \\\n  --config examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/claude-code.yaml \\\n  --input examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/review-packet.architecture.json \\\n  --mock-response examples/external-reviewers/claude-code/mock-raw-output.json \\\n  --output /tmp/reviewer-report.claude-architecture.json\n```\n\n## Non-goals\n\nThis is not a general multi-provider runtime, not a CI/enterprise API-key integration, and not a write-enabled reviewer.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "scripts/reviewers/prepare_review_set_artifacts.py",
      "size_bytes": 24178,
      "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Prepare provider-assigned review artifacts.\n\nThis script is deliberately small and deterministic. It does not choose review\ntopology, reviewer roles, provider assignments or important files. It\nmaterializes already-declared review inputs and writes structured preparation\nevidence that validators can later bind to invocation evidence.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport datetime as dt\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport yaml\n\nSCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent\nSCRIPTS_DIR = SCRIPT_DIR.parent\nif str(SCRIPTS_DIR) not in sys.path:\n    sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPTS_DIR))\n\nfrom reviewers.prompt_rendering import render_review_prompt  # noqa: E402\n\n\nENVELOPE_FIELDS = {\"review_packet_path\", \"reviewer_instance_id\", \"provider\"}\n\n\ndef sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:\n    return \"sha256:\" + hashlib.sha256(path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n\n\ndef sha256_text(value: str) -> str:\n    return \"sha256:\" + hashlib.sha256(value.encode(\"utf-8\")).hexdigest()\n\n\ndef load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        raise ValueError(f\"{path} must contain a JSON object\")\n    return data\n\n\ndef load_yaml(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    data = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n        raise ValueError(f\"{path} must contain a YAML mapping\")\n    return data\n\n\ndef write_json(path: Path, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + \"\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef write_yaml(path: Path, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    path.write_text(yaml.safe_dump(data, sort_keys=False), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef validate_embedded_file_snapshots(root: Path, packet: dict[str, Any], packet_path: Path) -> None:\n    files = packet.get(\"files\")\n    if not isinstance(files, list):\n        return\n    for idx, entry in enumerate(files):\n        if not isinstance(entry, dict):\n            continue\n        ref = entry.get(\"path\")\n        if not isinstance(ref, str) or not ref:\n            continue\n        path = resolve_ref(root, ref, f\"{packet_path}.files[{idx}].path\")\n        if not path.is_file():\n            raise ValueError(f\"{packet_path}: embedded file snapshot does not exist: {ref}\")\n        content = entry.get(\"content\")\n        if isinstance(content, str):\n            actual = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n            if content != actual:\n                raise ValueError(f\"{packet_path}: embedded file snapshot is stale for {ref}\")\n        size_bytes = entry.get(\"size_bytes\")\n        if isinstance(size_bytes, int) and size_bytes != path.stat().st_size:\n            raise ValueError(f\"{packet_path}: embedded file size is stale for {ref}\")\n\n\ndef resolve_ref(root: Path, ref: object, label: str) -> Path:\n    if not ref:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{label} is required\")\n    path = Path(str(ref))\n    if path.is_absolute():\n        resolved = path.resolve()\n    else:\n        if \"..\" in path.parts:\n            raise ValueError(f\"{label} must be relative and non-escaping: {ref}\")\n        resolved = (root / path).resolve()\n    try:\n        resolved.relative_to(root.resolve())\n    except ValueError as exc:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{label} must stay inside root: {ref}\") from exc\n    return resolved\n\n\ndef rel_ref(root: Path, path: Path) -> str:\n    return path.resolve().relative_to(root.resolve()).as_posix()\n\n\ndef normalize_rel(ref: str) -> str:\n    path = Path(ref)\n    if path.is_absolute() or \"..\" in path.parts:\n        raise ValueError(f\"path must be relative and non-escaping: {ref}\")\n    return path.as_posix()\n\n\ndef parse_exclusions(values: list[str]) -> dict[str, str]:\n    exclusions: dict[str, str] = {}\n    for value in values:\n        if \"=\" not in value:\n            raise ValueError(\"--exclude must use path=reason\")\n        path, reason = value.split(\"=\", 1)\n        path = normalize_rel(path.strip())\n        reason = reason.strip()\n        if not reason:\n            raise ValueError(\"--exclude must include a non-empty reason\")\n        exclusions[path] = reason\n    return exclusions\n\n\ndef collect_worktree_status(root: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain=v1\", \"--untracked-files=all\"],\n        cwd=root,\n        text=True,\n        capture_output=True,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if result.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"git status failed: {result.stdout}{result.stderr}\")\n    entries: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n    for line in result.stdout.splitlines():\n        if not line:\n            continue\n        status = line[:2].strip() or line[:2]\n        path = line[3:] if len(line) > 3 else \"\"\n        if \" -> \" in path:\n            path = path.split(\" -> \", 1)[1]\n        entries.append({\"path\": path, \"status\": status})\n    return entries\n\n\ndef fail_on_uncovered_dirty_paths(\n    status_entries: list[dict[str, str]],\n    covered_paths: set[str],\n    exclusions: dict[str, str],\n) -> None:\n    uncovered = [\n        entry[\"path\"]\n        for entry in status_entries\n        if entry.get(\"path\") and entry[\"path\"] not in covered_paths and entry[\"path\"] not in exclusions\n    ]\n    if uncovered:\n        raise ValueError(\"uncovered dirty worktree path(s): \" + \", \".join(sorted(uncovered)))\n\n\ndef reviewer_by_id(contract: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:\n    return {\n        str(item.get(\"instance_id\")): item\n        for item in contract.get(\"reviewer_set\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"instance_id\")\n    }\n\n\ndef prompt_by_reviewer(contract: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:\n    return {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): item\n        for item in contract.get(\"rendered_prompts\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\")\n    }\n\n\ndef assignment_by_reviewer(contract: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:\n    return {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): item\n        for item in contract.get(\"reviewer_assignments\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\")\n    }\n\n\ndef packet_entry_by_reviewer(contract: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:\n    return {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): item\n        for item in ((contract.get(\"inputs\") or {}).get(\"review_packets\") or [])\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\")\n    }\n\n\ndef packet_shared_payload(packet: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    return {key: value for key, value in packet.items() if key not in ENVELOPE_FIELDS}\n\n\ndef normalize_forbidden_actions(values: object) -> list[str]:\n    if not isinstance(values, list):\n        return []\n    aliases = {\n        \"run_tests\": \"Do not run tests.\",\n        \"run_scripts\": \"Do not execute scripts.\",\n        \"modify_files\": \"Do not modify files.\",\n        \"create_patch\": \"Do not produce patches.\",\n        \"update_evidence\": \"Do not update evidence.\",\n    }\n    normalized: list[str] = []\n    seen: set[str] = set()\n    for value in values:\n        text = str(value)\n        action = aliases.get(text, text)\n        if action not in seen:\n            normalized.append(action)\n            seen.add(action)\n    return normalized\n\n\ndef artifact_entry(root: Path, ref: object, kind: str, required_by: list[str] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    path = resolve_ref(root, ref, kind)\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise ValueError(f\"{kind} must be a file artifact: {ref}\")\n    entry: dict[str, Any] = {\n        \"path\": rel_ref(root, path),\n        \"kind\": kind,\n        \"hash\": sha256_file(path),\n    }\n    if required_by:\n        entry[\"required_by\"] = required_by\n    return entry\n\n\ndef collect_input_artifacts(\n    root: Path,\n    contract: dict[str, Any],\n    shared_packet_path: Path,\n    include_paths: list[str],\n) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    inputs = contract.get(\"inputs\", {}) or {}\n    artifacts: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    seen: set[str] = set()\n\n    def add(ref: object, kind: str, required_by: list[str] | None = None) -> None:\n        if not ref:\n            return\n        entry = artifact_entry(root, ref, kind, required_by)\n        if entry[\"path\"] not in seen:\n            artifacts.append(entry)\n            seen.add(entry[\"path\"])\n\n    add(rel_ref(root, shared_packet_path), \"shared_review_packet_source\", [\"review_packet_generation\"])\n    add(inputs.get(\"task_contract\"), \"task_contract\", [\"review_context\"])\n    add(inputs.get(\"verification_gate_report\"), \"verification_gate_report\", [\"review_context\"])\n    add(inputs.get(\"review_packet_schema\"), \"schema\", [\"review_packet_generation\"])\n    add(inputs.get(\"output_schema\"), \"schema\", [\"reviewer_output\"])\n    for include in include_paths:\n        kind = \"agent_instructions\" if Path(include).name == \"AGENTS.md\" else \"declared_input\"\n        add(include, kind, [\"explicit_include\"])\n    return artifacts\n\n\ndef apply_common_packet_fields(\n    packet: dict[str, Any],\n    contract: dict[str, Any],\n    contract_ref: str,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    identity = contract.get(\"identity\", {}) or {}\n    inputs = contract.get(\"inputs\", {}) or {}\n    packet = dict(packet)\n    packet.setdefault(\"agentsflow_version\", \"0.2\")\n    for key in [\"workflow\", \"run_id\", \"review_profile\", \"composition\"]:\n        if identity.get(key) is not None:\n            packet[key] = identity[key]\n    packet[\"prompt_policy\"] = contract.get(\"prompt_policy\", {}) or {}\n    packet[\"review_prompt_contract\"] = {\n        \"path\": contract_ref,\n        \"schema\": \"schemas/review-prompt-contract.schema.json\",\n    }\n    if contract.get(\"context_policy\"):\n        packet[\"context_policy\"] = contract[\"context_policy\"]\n    permission_policy = contract.get(\"permission_policy\", {}) or {}\n    if permission_policy.get(\"forbidden_actions\"):\n        packet[\"forbidden_actions\"] = normalize_forbidden_actions(permission_policy[\"forbidden_actions\"])\n    if contract.get(\"risk_surface_profile\"):\n        packet[\"risk_surface_profile\"] = contract[\"risk_surface_profile\"]\n    if contract.get(\"failure_path_matrix\"):\n        packet[\"failure_path_matrix\"] = contract[\"failure_path_matrix\"]\n    if contract.get(\"known_blockers\")
