has external assignments\")\n    if provider_policy.get(\"require_model_diversity\") is True:\n        minimum = int(provider_policy.get(\"min_distinct_provider_model_families\", 2))\n        if len(provider_model_families) < minimum:\n            raise ValueError(\"model diversity requirement is not satisfied by reviewer_assignments\")\n\n    for assignment in assignments:\n        provider = str(assignment.get(\"provider\", \"\"))\n        if provider not in SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS:\n            raise ValueError(f\"unsupported review provider: {provider}\")\n        for key in [\"reviewer\", \"model_family\", \"packet_path\", \"report_path\"]:\n            if not assignment.get(key):\n                raise ValueError(f\"reviewer assignment missing {key}: {assignment}\")\n        if provider == \"claude-code\":\n            for key in [\"provider_config\", \"raw_output_path\", \"invocation_metadata_path\"]:\n                if not assignment.get(key):\n                    raise ValueError(f\"claude-code reviewer assignment missing {key}: {assignment}\")\n\n\ndef validate_invocation_set_output_path(contract: dict[str, Any], output_path: Path, root: Path) -> Path | None:\n    inputs = contract.get(\"inputs\", {}) or {}\n    invocation_set = inputs.get(\"review_invocation_set\")\n    if not invocation_set:\n        raise ValueError(\"reviewer_assignments require inputs.review_invocation_set path\")\n    evidence_report = inputs.get(\"evidence_report\")\n    if evidence_report and resolve_path(evidence_report, root).resolve() == resolve_path(invocation_set, root).resolve():\n        raise ValueError(\"inputs.evidence_report must not match inputs.review_invocation_set\")\n    artifact_preparation_report: Path | None = None\n    if contract.get(\"artifact_scope\", \"run\") == \"run\":\n        preparation_ref = inputs.get(\"artifact_preparation_report\")\n        if not preparation_ref:\n            raise ValueError(\"run reviewer_assignments require inputs.artifact_preparation_report path\")\n        artifact_preparation_report = resolve_path(preparation_ref, root)\n        if not artifact_preparation_report.is_file():\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"run reviewer_assignments require existing inputs.artifact_preparation_report: \"\n                f\"{artifact_preparation_report}\"\n            )\n    expected_output = resolve_path(invocation_set, root).resolve()\n    if output_path.resolve() != expected_output:\n        raise ValueError(\"--output must match inputs.review_invocation_set path\")\n    return artifact_preparation_report\n\n\ndef require_hash_match(path: Path, label: str, declared: object, artifact_path: Path) -> None:\n    if not isinstance(declared, str) or not declared.startswith(\"sha256:\"):\n        raise ValueError(f\"{path}: {label} hash is required\")\n    digest = declared.removeprefix(\"sha256:\")\n    if len(digest) != 64 or any(char not in \"0123456789abcdef\" for char in digest):\n        raise ValueError(f\"{path}: {label} hash must be a concrete sha256\")\n    actual = sha256_file(artifact_path)\n    if declared != actual:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{path}: {label} hash must match current artifact\")\n\n\ndef resolve_prepared_artifact_path(preparation_path: Path, ref: object, root: Path, label: str) -> Path:\n    if not ref:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: {label} path is required\")\n    path = resolve_path(ref, root)\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: {label} must be an existing file: {ref}\")\n    return path\n\n\ndef validate_preparation_artifact(\n    preparation_path: Path,\n    contract_path: Path,\n    root: Path,\n    contract: dict[str, Any],\n    output_path: Path,\n) -> str:\n    preparation = load_json(preparation_path)\n    try:\n        import jsonschema\n    except ImportError as exc:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"jsonschema is required for review-set validation\") from exc\n    schema = load_json(root / \"schemas\" / \"review-artifact-preparation.schema.json\")\n    errors = sorted(jsonschema.Draft202012Validator(schema).iter_errors(preparation), key=lambda err: list(err.path))\n    if errors:\n        first = errors[0]\n        location = \".\".join(str(part) for part in first.path) or \"<root>\"\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: preparation artifact schema validation failed at {location}: {first.message}\")\n    if preparation.get(\"status\") != \"completed\":\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: preparation artifact status must be completed\")\n    prompt_contract = preparation.get(\"review_prompt_contract\", {}) or {}\n    prepared_contract_ref = prompt_contract.get(\"path\")\n    if not prepared_contract_ref:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: review_prompt_contract.path is required\")\n    prepared_contract_path = resolve_path(prepared_contract_ref, root).resolve()\n    if prepared_contract_path != contract_path.resolve():\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: review_prompt_contract.path must match --contract\")\n    prepared_contract_hash = prompt_contract.get(\"hash\")\n    require_hash_match(preparation_path, \"review_prompt_contract\", prepared_contract_hash, contract_path)\n\n    inputs = contract.get(\"inputs\", {}) or {}\n    assignments = {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): item\n        for item in contract.get(\"reviewer_assignments\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\")\n    }\n    reviewers = {\n        str(item.get(\"instance_id\"))\n        for item in contract.get(\"reviewer_set\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"instance_id\")\n    }\n    assigned_reviewers = set(assignments)\n    if reviewers and assigned_reviewers != reviewers:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: reviewer_assignments must cover reviewer_set exactly\")\n\n    prep_assignments = {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): item\n        for item in preparation.get(\"reviewer_assignments\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\")\n    }\n    if set(prep_assignments) != assigned_reviewers:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: reviewer_assignments must cover reviewer_set exactly\")\n    for reviewer, assignment in assignments.items():\n        prepared_assignment = prep_assignments[reviewer]\n        for key in [\"provider\", \"model_family\", \"packet_path\", \"report_path\"]:\n            if str(prepared_assignment.get(key)) != str(assignment.get(key)):\n                raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: reviewer_assignments[{reviewer}].{key} must match contract\")\n\n    generated = preparation.get(\"generated_artifacts\", {}) or {}\n    prep_invocation = generated.get(\"review_invocation_set\", {}) or {}\n    if not prep_invocation.get(\"path\"):\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: generated_artifacts.review_invocation_set.path is required\")\n    prep_invocation_path = resolve_path(prep_invocation.get(\"path\"), root).resolve()\n    if prep_invocation_path != output_path.resolve():\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: generated_artifacts.review_invocation_set.path must match --output\")\n\n    packet_paths = {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): resolve_path(item.get(\"path\"), root)\n        for item in inputs.get(\"review_packets\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\") and item.get(\"path\")\n    }\n    for reviewer, assignment in assignments.items():\n        packet_paths.setdefault(reviewer, resolve_path(assignment.get(\"packet_path\"), root))\n    prep_packets = {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): item\n        for item in generated.get(\"review_packets\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\")\n    }\n    if set(prep_packets) != assigned_reviewers:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: generated review_packets must cover reviewer_set exactly\")\n    for reviewer, packet_entry in prep_packets.items():\n        prepared_packet_path = resolve_prepared_artifact_path(\n            preparation_path,\n            packet_entry.get(\"path\"),\n            root,\n            f\"generated_artifacts.review_packets[{reviewer}]\",\n        )\n        expected_packet_path = packet_paths[reviewer].resolve()\n        if prepared_packet_path.resolve() != expected_packet_path:\n            raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: review packet path must match contract for {reviewer}\")\n        require_hash_match(preparation_path, f\"review packet for {reviewer}\", packet_entry.get(\"hash\"), expected_packet_path)\n\n    prompt_paths = {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): resolve_path(item.get(\"prompt_path\"), root)\n        for item in contract.get(\"rendered_prompts\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\") and item.get(\"prompt_path\")\n    }\n    prep_prompts = {\n        str(item.get(\"reviewer\")): item\n        for item in generated.get(\"rendered_prompts\", []) or []\n        if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get(\"reviewer\")\n    }\n    if prompt_paths and set(prep_prompts) != assigned_reviewers:\n        raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: generated rendered_prompts must cover reviewer_set exactly\")\n    for reviewer, prompt_entry in prep_prompts.items():\n        prepared_prompt_path = resolve_prepared_artifact_path(\n            preparation_path,\n            prompt_entry.get(\"path\"),\n            root,\n            f\"generated_artifacts.rendered_prompts[{reviewer}]\",\n        )\n        if reviewer in prompt_paths and prepared_prompt_path.resolve() != prompt_paths[reviewer].resolve():\n            raise ValueError(f\"{preparation_path}: rendered prompt path must match contract for {reviewer}\")\n        require_hash_match(preparation_path, f\"rendered prompt for {reviewer}\", prompt_entry.get(\"hash\"), prepared_prompt_path)\n\n    for idx, artifact in enumerate(preparation.get(\"input_artifacts\", []) or []):\n        if not isinstance(artifact, dict):\n            continue\n        artifact_path = resolve_prepared_artifact_path(\n            preparation_path,\n            artifact.get(\"path\"),\n            root,\n            f\"input_artifacts[{idx}]\",\n        )\n        require_hash_match(preparation_path, f\"input_artifacts[{idx}]\", artifact.get(\"hash\"), artifact_path)\n    return sha256_file(preparation_path)\n\n\ndef build_entry(assignment: dict[str, Any], root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    report_path = resolve_path(assignment[\"report_path\"], root)\n    return {\n        \"reviewer\": str(assignment[\"reviewer\"]),\n        \"provider\": str(assignment[\"provider\"]),\n        \"model_family\": str(assignment[\"model_family\"]),\n        \"packet_path\": str(resolve_path(assignment[\"packet_path\"], root)),\n        \"report_path\": str(report_path),\n    }\n\n\ndef validate_internal_assignment(\n    assignment: dict[str, Any],\n    entry: dict[str, Any],\n    root: Path,\n    require_model_diversity: bool,\n    wait_seconds: float,\n    progress_interval_seconds: float,\n) -> None:\n    reviewer = str(assignment[\"reviewer\"])\n    model_family = str(assignment[\"model_family\"])\n    report_path = resolve_path(assignment[\"report_path\"], root)\n    entry[\"checked_at\"] = now_utc()\n    if not report_path.exists() and wait_seconds > 0:\n        entry[\"status\"] = \"waiting-for-report\"\n        deadline = dt.datetime.now(dt.timezone.utc).timestamp() + wait_seconds\n        last_progress = time.monotonic()\n        while not report_path.exists() and dt.datetime.now(dt.timezone.utc).timestamp() < deadline:\n            now = time.monotonic()\n            if progress_interval_seconds > 0 and now - last_progress >= progress_interval_seconds:\n                print(\n                    f\"review-set progress: waiting for internal reviewer {reviewer} report\",\n                    file=sys.stderr,\n                    flush=True,\n                )\n                last_progress = now\n            time.sleep(0.25)\n        entry[\"checked_at\"] = now_utc()\n    if not report_path.exists():\n        raise ValueError(f\"internal reviewer report is missing for {reviewer}: {report_path}\")\n    report = validate_report(report_path, root)\n    validate_report_identity(report, reviewer, report_path)\n    packet_path = resolve_path(assignment[\"packet_path\"], root)\n    packet = load_json(packet_path)\n    validate_report_review_context(report, packet, packet_path, reviewer, report_path, root)\n    report_model = str(((report.get(\"reviewer\") or {}).get(\"model\") or \"\"))\n    if require_model_diversity:\n        if not report_model:\n            raise ValueError(f\"internal reviewer report model is required for {reviewer}\")\n        if report_model != model_family and model_family.lower() not in report_model.lower():\n            raise ValueError(f\"internal reviewer report model must match assignment model_family for {reviewer}\")\n        entry[\"evidence_model_family\"] = model_family\n    entry[\"status\"] = \"report-present\"\n\n\ndef build_external_command(\n    assignment: dict[str, Any],\n    root: Path,\n    mock_responses: dict[str, Path],\n) -> list[str]:\n    reviewer = str(assignment[\"reviewer\"])\n    cmd = [\n        sys.executable,\n        str(root / \"scripts\" / \"reviewers\" / \"run_external_reviewer.py\"),\n        \"--provider\",\n        \"claude-code\",\n        \"--config\",\n        str(resolve_path(assignment[\"provider_config\"], root)),\n        \"--input\",\n        str(resolve_path(assignment[\"packet_path\"], root)),\n        \"--output\",\n        str(resolve_path(assignment[\"report_path\"], root)),\n        \"--raw-output\",\n        str(resolve_path(assignment[\"raw_output_path\"], root)),\n        \"--invocation-output\",\n        str(resolve_path(assignment[\"invocation_metadata_path\"], root)),\n    ]\n    if reviewer in mock_responses:\n        cmd.extend([\"--mock-response\", str(resolve_path(mock_responses[reviewer], root))])\n    return cmd\n\n\ndef start_external_assignment(\n    assignment: dict[str, Any],\n    entry: dict[str, Any],\n    root: Path,\n    mock_responses: dict[str, Path],\n    progress_interval_seconds: float,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    raw_output_path = resolve_path(assignment[\"raw_output_path\"], root)\n    invocation_path = resolve_path(assignment[\"invocation_metadata_path\"], root)\n    entry[\"raw_output_path\"] = str(raw_output_path)\n    entry[\"invocation_metadata_path\"] = str(invocation_path)\n    entry[\"status\"] = \"running\"\n    entry[\"dispatch_started_at\"] = now_utc()\n    cmd = build_external_command(assignment, root, mock_responses)\n    dispatch_started_monotonic = time.monotonic()\n    if progress_interval_seconds > 0:\n        print(\n            f\"review-set progress: dispatched external reviewer {assignment['reviewer']}\",\n            file=sys.stderr,\n            flush=True,\n        )\n    process = subprocess.Popen(  # noqa: S603\n        cmd,\n        cwd=root,\n        text=True,\n        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n    )\n    return {\n        \"assignment\": assignment,\n        \"entry\": entry,\n        \"process\": process,\n        \"raw_output_path\": raw_output_path,\n        \"invocation_path\": invocation_path,\n        \"dispatch_started_monotonic\": dispatch_started_monotonic,\n        \"last_progress_monotonic\": dispatch_started_monotonic,\n    }\n\n\ndef finish_external_assignment(\n    run: dict[str, Any],\n    root: Path,\n    require_model_diversity: bool,\n) -> None:\n    assignment = run[\"assignment\"]\n    entry = run[\"entry\"]\n    process = run[\"process\"]\n    reviewer = str(assignment[\"reviewer\"])\n    model_family = str(assignment[\"model_family\"])\n    report_path = resolve_path(assignment[\"report_path\"], root)\n    raw_output_path = run[\"raw_output_path\"]\n    invocation_path = run[\"invocation_path\"]\n\n    stdout, stderr = process.communicate()\n    entry[\"dispatch_finished_at\"] = now_utc()\n    entry[\"stdout\"] = stdout\n    entry[\"stderr\"] = stderr\n    entry[\"exit_code\"] = process.returncode\n    if process.returncode != 0:\n        entry[\"status\"] = \"failed\"\n        entry[\"error\"] = f\"external reviewer {reviewer} failed\"\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"external reviewer {reviewer} failed: {stdout}{stderr}\")\n    report = validate_report(report_path, root)\n    validate_report_identity(report, reviewer, report_path)\n    if invocation_path.exists():\n        invocation = load_json(invocation_path)\n        for key in [\n            \"requested_model\",\n            \"requested_effort\",\n            \"execution_mode\",\n            \"provider_models_used\",\n            \"provider_total_cost_usd\",\n            \"provider_service_tier\",\n            \"provider_speed\",\n            \"raw_output_path\",\n            \"raw_output_hash\",\n            \"normalized_output_hash\",\n        ]:\n            if key in invocation:\n                entry[key] = invocation[key]\n        requested_model = str(invocation.get(\"requested_model\") or \"\")\n        if require_model_diversity:\n            if not requested_model:\n                raise ValueError(f\"external reviewer requested_model is required for {reviewer}\")\n            if requested_model != model_family:\n                raise ValueError(f\"external reviewer requested_model must match assignment model_family for {reviewer}\")\n            if not provider_models_include_family(invocation.get(\"provider_models_used\"), model_family):\n                raise ValueError(\n                    f\"external reviewer provider_models_used must include assignment model_family for {reviewer}\"\n                )\n            entry[\"evidence_model_family\"] = requested_model\n    elif require_model_diversity:\n        raise ValueError(f\"external reviewer invocation metadata is required for {reviewer}\")\n    entry[\"status\"] = \"invoked\"\n\n\ndef collect_external_assignments(\n    external_runs: list[dict[str, Any]],\n    root: Path,\n    require_model_diversity: bool,\n    timeout_seconds: float,\n    progress_interval_seconds: float,\n) -> list[str]:\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    pending = [run for run in external_runs if run[\"entry\"].get(\"status\") == \"running\"]\n    while pending:\n        progress = False\n        now = time.monotonic()\n        for run in list(pending):\n            process = run[\"process\"]\n            entry = run[\"entry\"]\n            reviewer = str(run[\"assignment\"][\"reviewer\"])\n            elapsed = now - float(run[\"dispatch_started_monotonic\"])\n            last_progress = float(run.get(\"last_progress_monotonic\", run[\"dispatch_started_monotonic\"]))\n            if process.poll() is None and timeout_seconds > 0 and elapsed < timeout_seconds:\n                if progress_interval_seconds > 0 and now - last_progress >= progress_interval_seconds:\n                    entry[\"last_progress_at\"] = now_utc()\n                    print(\n                        f\"review-set progress: external reviewer {reviewer} still running after {elapsed:.0f}s\",\n                        file=sys.stderr,\n                        flush=True,\n                    )\n                    run[\"last_progress_monotonic\"] = now\n                continue\n            if process.poll() is None:\n                process.kill()\n                stdout, stderr = process.communicate()\n                message = f\"external reviewer {reviewer} timed out after {timeout_seconds:g} seconds\"\n                entry[\"stdout\"] = stdout\n                entry[\"stderr\"] = stderr\n                entry[\"status\"] = \"timed-out\"\n                entry[\"error\"] = message\n                entry[\"dispatch_finished_at\"] = now_utc()\n                if progress_interval_seconds > 0:\n                    print(f\"review-set progress: {message}\", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)\n                errors.append(message)\n                pending.remove(run)\n                progress = True\n                continue\n            try:\n                finish_external_assignment(run, root, require_model_diversity)\n                if progress_interval_seconds > 0:\n                    print(\n                        f\"review-set progress: external reviewer {reviewer} completed\",\n                        file=sys.stderr,\n                        flush=True,\n                    )\n            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n                if entry.get(\"status\") == \"running\":\n                    entry[\"status\"] = \"failed\"\n                entry[\"error\"] = str(exc)\n                if progress_interval_seconds > 0:\n                    print(\n                        f\"review-set progress: external reviewer {reviewer} failed: {exc}\",\n                        file=sys.stderr,\n                        flush=True,\n                    )\n                errors.append(str(exc))\n            pending.remove(run)\n            progress = True\n        if pending and not progress:\n            time.sleep(0.25)\n    return errors\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    parser.add_argument(\"--contract\", required=True, help=\"review-prompt-contract.yaml\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--output\", required=True, help=\"review invocation set JSON\")\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--mock-response\",\n        action=\"append\",\n        default=[],\n        help=\"Mock external provider response as reviewer=path.\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--internal-report-wait-seconds\",\n        type=float,\n        default=0.0,\n        help=\"Seconds to wait for internal-agent report-present artifacts after external reviewers are dispatched.\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--external-reviewer-timeout-seconds\",\n        type=float,\n        default=900.0,\n        help=\"Per external reviewer timeout after dispatch. Timed-out reviewers are killed and recorded as evidence.\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--progress-interval-seconds\",\n        type=float,\n        default=30.0,\n        help=\"Emit external reviewer progress heartbeat lines to stderr at this interval. Use 0 to disable.\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    root = Path.cwd()\n    contract_path = Path(args.contract)\n    output_path = Path(args.output)\n    started = now_utc()\n    mock_responses = parse_mock_responses(args.mock_response)\n    reviewers: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    external_runs: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    status = \"completed\"\n\n    try:\n        contract = load_yaml(contract_path)\n        assignments = contract.get(\"reviewer_assignments\", []) or []\n        if not isinstance(assignments, list) or not assignments:\n            raise ValueError(\"review prompt contract must declare reviewer_assignments\")\n        validate_assignments(assignments, contract)\n        artifact_preparation_report = validate_invocation_set_output_path(contract, output_path, root)\n        artifact_preparation_report_hash: str | None = None\n        if artifact_preparation_report:\n            artifact_preparation_report_hash = validate_preparation_artifact(\n                artifact_preparation_report,\n                resolve_path(contract_path, root),\n                root,\n                contract,\n                output_path,\n            )\n        require_model_diversity = (contract.get(\"provider_policy\", {}) or {}).get(\"require_model_diversity\") is True\n        internal_runs: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]] = []\n\n        for assignment in assignments:\n            provider = str(assignment[\"provider\"])\n            entry = build_entry(assignment, root)\n            reviewers.append(entry)\n            if provider == \"internal-agent\":\n                internal_runs.append((assignment, entry))\n            elif provider == \"claude-code\":\n                external_runs.append(\n                    start_external_assignment(\n                        assignment,\n                        entry,\n                        root,\n                        mock_responses,\n                        max(0.0, args.progress_interval_seconds),\n                    )\n                )\n        for assignment, entry in internal_runs:\n            validate_internal_assignment(\n                assignment,\n                entry,\n                root,\n                require_model_diversity,\n                max(0.0, args.internal_report_wait_seconds),\n                max(0.0, args.progress_interval_seconds),\n            )\n        external_errors = collect_external_assignments(\n            external_runs,\n            root,\n            require_model_diversity,\n            max(0.0, args.external_reviewer_timeout_seconds),\n            max(0.0, args.progress_interval_seconds),\n        )\n        if external_errors:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"; \".join(external_errors))\n    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n        status = \"failed\"\n        if external_runs:\n            collection_errors = collect_external_assignments(\n                external_runs,\n                root,\n                locals().get(\"require_model_diversity\", False),\n                max(0.0, args.external_reviewer_timeout_seconds),\n                max(0.0, args.progress_interval_seconds),\n            )\n            if collection_errors:\n                exc = RuntimeError(f\"{exc}; external collection errors: {'; '.join(collection_errors)}\")\n        for entry in reviewers:\n            if not entry.get(\"status\"):\n                entry[\"status\"] = \"failed\"\n                entry[\"error\"] = str(exc)\n        output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        failed_output = {\n            \"artifact_kind\": \"review_invocation_set\",\n            \"review_prompt_contract\": str(contract_path),\n            \"status\": status,\n            \"started_at\": started,\n            \"finished_at\": now_utc(),\n            \"runner_scheduling\": \"external-first-async\",\n            \"provider_model_families\": [],\n            \"reviewers\": reviewers,\n            \"error\": str(exc),\n        }\n        artifact_preparation_report = (contract.get(\"inputs\", {}) or {}).get(\"artifact_preparation_report\") if \"contract\" in locals() else None\n        if artifact_preparation_report:\n            failed_output[\"artifact_preparation_report\"] = str(artifact_preparation_report)\n        if \"artifact_preparation_report_hash\" in locals() and artifact_preparation_report_hash:\n            failed_output[\"artifact_preparation_report_hash\"] = artifact_preparation_report_hash\n        output_path.write_text(\n            json.dumps(failed_output, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + \"\\n\",\n            encoding=\"utf-8\",\n        )\n        print(f\"review set failed: {exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return 2\n\n    provider_model_families = sorted(\n        {\n            f\"{entry['provider']}/{entry.get('evidence_model_family') or entry['model_family']}\"\n            for entry in reviewers\n            if entry.get(\"provider\") and entry.get(\"model_family\")\n        }\n    )\n    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    completed_output = {\n        \"artifact_kind\": \"review_invocation_set\",\n        \"review_prompt_contract\": str(contract_path),\n        \"status\": status,\n        \"started_at\": started,\n        \"finished_at\": now_utc(),\n        \"runner_scheduling\": \"external-first-async\",\n        \"provider_model_families\": provider_model_families,\n        \"reviewers\": reviewers,\n    }\n    artifact_preparation_report = (contract.get(\"inputs\", {}) or {}).get(\"artifact_preparation_report\")\n    if artifact_preparation_report:\n        completed_output[\"artifact_preparation_report\"] = str(artifact_preparation_report)\n    if \"artifact_preparation_report_hash\" in locals() and artifact_preparation_report_hash:\n        completed_output[\"artifact_preparation_report_hash\"] = artifact_preparation_report_hash\n    output_path.write_text(\n        json.dumps(completed_output, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + \"\\n\",\n        encoding=\"utf-8\",\n    )\n    print(f\"Review invocation set written to {output_path}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"
    },

      "path": "scripts/validate_repo.py",

      "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Validate AgentsFlow repository integrity.\n\nThis module is intentionally a thin compatibility facade. Domain-specific\nvalidators live under scripts/repo_validation/; tests and external callers may\nstill import validate_repo.* while the CLI entrypoint remains unchanged.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom repo_validation.behavior_bindings import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.collect import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.common import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.external_reviewers import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.gates import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.project_initialization import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.project_overlay import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.pr_merge_readiness import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.review import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.runner import main, validate_repository\nfrom repo_validation.workflow_runs import *  # noqa: F401,F403\nfrom repo_validation.workflows import *  # noqa: F401,F403\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"

    {
      "path": "skills/decision-contracting/SKILL.md",
      "size_bytes": 1749,
      "content": "# Decision Contracting\n\nReusable AgentsFlow skill for the Specification / Plan phase pattern.\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis skill helps create or validate planning/specification artifacts without implementing code.\n\nFor `project-initialization.prepare-workflow`, it also helps normalize run-level\ntarget workflow decisions before readiness: missing gate, review, evidence,\nrisk-surface, Failure Path Matrix, authority, scope or workflow-design decisions\nare captured as structured decision artifacts rather than hidden in chat.\n\n## Rules\n\n- Stay read-only unless a workflow explicitly assigns implementation authority to a different actor class.\n- Produce structured artifacts, not vague chat notes.\n- For design review or phase acceptance, do not ask for one blanket approval\n  before showing the decision structure.\n- Start with an open decision inventory.\n- For each blocking or material decision, present options, tradeoffs, a\n  recommended path, rationale and the exact human acceptance question.\n- Record acceptance, change requests or explicit deferrals per decision; do not\n  collapse unresolved blocking decisions into a single aggregate approval.\n- Surface assumptions, unknowns, and required evidence.\n- Keep scope boundaries explicit.\n- For prepare-workflow readiness, classify each missing or disputed decision as\n  blocking-material, nonblocking-follow-up, nonblocking-known-limitation or\n  out-of-scope.\n- Record selected options, defaults, deferrals, residual risk and affected\n  artifacts in `human-decisions.yaml` or the declared target workflow decision\n  packet.\n- Do not promote run-level prepare-workflow decisions into persistent\n  `project-operating-decisions.yaml` unless the human explicitly chooses\n  onboarding or policy activation.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "skills/decision-contracting/skill.yaml",
      "size_bytes": 581,
      "content": "name: decision-contracting\ntype: skill\nversion: 0.1\ninputs:\n  - workflow_context\n  - accepted_decisions\n  - domain_pack\n  - strictness\n  - target_workflow_context\n  - risk_surface_profile\n  - failure_path_matrix\noutputs:\n  - structured_artifact\n  - open_decision_inventory\n  - option_tradeoff_matrix\n  - per_decision_acceptance_questions\n  - target_workflow_decision_packet\n  - readiness_decision_gaps\ncompatible_workflows:\n  - project-initialization\n  - new-project-spec-first\n  - big-feature-contract-first\n  - agentic-system-hardening\n  - prompt-behavior-eval\n  - safe-refactor\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "skills/evidence-reporting/skill.yaml",
      "size_bytes": 1163,
      "content": "name: evidence-reporting\ntype: skill\nversion: 0.1\npurpose: \"Produce an acceptance proof for a task, implementation, review, or spec workflow, including scenario coverage, FPM coverage, structured command evidence, and evidence freshness.\"\n\ninputs:\n  - contract\n  - verification_results\n  - changed_files\n  - review_reports\n  - risk_surface_profile\n  - failure_path_matrix\n  - behavior_bindings\n  - material_change_log\n\noutputs:\n  - evidence_report\n  - failure_path_matrix_coverage\n  - evidence_freshness_summary\n  - known_limitations\n  - follow_up_items\n\ndepends_on:\n  []\ncompatible_workflows:\n  - new-project-spec-first\n  - big-feature-contract-first\n  - agentic-system-hardening\n  - prompt-behavior-eval\n  - safe-refactor\n  - bugfix-regression-capture\n  - review-only-fusion\n  - pr-merge-readiness\n\nquality_bar:\n  - Evidence is auditable.\n  - Claims are tied to commands, files, or reviews.\n  - Evidence freshness after the latest material change is explicit.\n  - FPM coverage is visible when risk surfaces were selected.\n\nanti_patterns:\n  - Saying “all tests pass” without commands/results.\n  - Using stale evidence as acceptance proof without marking it.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "skills/fusion-synthesis/SKILL.md",
      "size_bytes": 3579,
      "content": "# Skill: fusion-synthesis\n\n## Purpose\n\nSynthesize independent reviewer reports into consensus, disagreements, candidate blockers, and recommended verdict. Fusion is decision support; it does not turn reviewer findings into authoritative truth.\n\n## Inputs\n\n- `contract`\n- `review_reports`\n- `evidence_report`\n- `risk_surface_profile`\n- `failure_path_matrix`\n- `evidence_freshness`\n\n\n## Outputs\n\n- `fusion_report`\n- `recommended_verdict`\n- `human_decision_items`\n\n\n## Procedure\n\n1. Run mechanical intake: confirm expected reviewer reports exist, are\n   schema-valid, fresh for the latest material change and match the declared\n   reviewer assignment, provider, role and topic where applicable.\n2. Extract canonical finding metadata while preserving source findings: source\n   report, provider, model, topic, role, severity, evidence references, risk\n   surface and Failure Path Matrix row when available.\n3. Group findings as duplicate, related, or conflict. True duplicate groups keep\n   the highest candidate severity until relevance validation.\n4. Compare reviewer findings, including topic-pair comparison when a topology\n   mirrors providers or roles over the same topic.\n5. Identify consensus.\n6. Identify disagreements.\n7. Surface any P0/P1 candidate blocker.\n8. Surface missing or stale risk-surface/FPM evidence as candidate mandatory\n   evidence gaps when reviewers report it.\n9. Preserve relevance questions and disagreements.\n10. Assign recommended verdict and proposed required changes.\n11. Hand off candidate findings for main-agent relevance validation.\n\n\n## Quality bar\n\n- No plausible candidate blocker is hidden by majority vote.\n- Disagreements are preserved.\n- Findings remain candidate findings until the main/orchestrating agent validates relevance.\n- FPM coverage and freshness gaps are preserved as evidence issues, not\n  averaged away.\n\n\n## Anti-patterns\n\n- Averaging away blocking issues.\n- Reclassifying missing selected risk-path evidence as nonblocking without\n  main-agent relevance validation.\n\n\n## Handoff\n\nUse deterministic scripts for checks when possible. Produce artifacts that can be consumed by workflows and review/fusion stages.\n\n\n## Fusion execution rule\n\nFusion is read-only and runs after verification and reviewer reports exist. It must\nnot run tests, execute scripts, modify artifacts, erase candidate blocking issues by\nmajority vote, or claim that reviewer findings are accepted truth before relevance\nvalidation.\n\n## Review cycle handoff\n\nFusion must explicitly hand off candidate blockers to the main/orchestrating agent\nfor relevance validation. Fusion may recommend a review-cycle decision, but the\ndefault exit condition is satisfied only after validation shows no validated\nblocking findings and no mandatory evidence gaps.\n\nFusion must not trigger repeated review cycles for non-blocking findings alone.\n\n## Orchestration boundary\n\nFusion is not the workflow orchestrator. It does not launch reviewers, run verification gates, run tests, call tools, or modify artifacts by default. It may recommend additional review or verification, but the main/orchestrating agent owns the decision to run another cycle.\n\n## Authority boundary\n\nFusion is not an automatic acceptance gate and does not own human-mediated\ndecisions. Deterministic automation may validate reviewer-report structure,\nschema, freshness and evidence references. Fusion provides decision support.\nThe main/orchestrating agent validates candidate findings. Human-mediated gates\nremain human-owned and require normalized human decisions before acceptance is\nclaimed.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "skills/fusion-synthesis/skill.yaml",
      "size_bytes": 1331,
      "content": "name: fusion-synthesis\ntype: skill\nversion: 0.1\npurpose: Synthesize independent reviewer reports into consensus, disagreements, blockers,\n  and final verdict.\ninputs:\n- contract\n- review_reports\n- evidence_report\n- risk_surface_profile\n- failure_path_matrix\n- evidence_freshness\noutputs:\n- mechanical_intake_summary\n- canonical_finding_groups\n- fusion_report\n- final_verdict\n- human_decision_items\ndepends_on: []\ncompatible_workflows:\n- big-feature-contract-first\n- agentic-system-hardening\n- review-only-fusion\n- pr-merge-readiness\nquality_bar:\n- No plausible blocker is hidden by majority vote.\n- Disagreements are preserved.\n- FPM coverage and freshness gaps are preserved as candidate evidence issues.\n- Duplicate, related, and conflict groups preserve source findings and max candidate severity until relevance validation.\n- Human-mediated decisions remain human-owned; fusion provides decision support only.\nanti_patterns:\n- Averaging away blocking issues.\n- Reclassifying missing selected risk-path evidence as nonblocking without relevance validation.\nactor_class: fusion_agent\nmode: read_only\nruns_after:\n- verification_gate\n- review\nforbidden_actions:\n- run_tests\n- run_scripts\n- modify_files\n- generate_patch\nrole: read_only_synthesis\nmay_launch_reviewers: false\nmay_run_gates: false\nmay_request_additional_review: true\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "skills/reviewer-adversarial/skill.yaml",
      "size_bytes": 1421,
      "content": "name: reviewer-adversarial\ntype: skill\nversion: 0.1\npurpose: Look for scope creep, prompt/policy bypasses, hidden failure modes, and false\n  completion.\ninputs:\n- contract\n- artifact\n- evidence_report\n- domain_pack\n- risk_surface_profile\n- failure_path_matrix\n- known_blockers\noutputs:\n- adversarial_review_report\n- risk_notes\ndepends_on: []\ncompatible_workflows:\n- big-feature-contract-first\n- agentic-system-hardening\n- prompt-behavior-eval\n- review-only-fusion\n- pr-merge-readiness\nquality_bar:\n- Findings are plausible and testable.\n- Speculative concerns are labeled as such.\n- Bypass/failure concerns identify concrete surfaces, path classes, or triggers.\nanti_patterns:\n- Blocking on vague suspicion without a concrete failure mode.\n- Expanding scope by inventing unselected risk surfaces without contract or policy support.\nmode: read_only\nactor_class: review_agent\nruns_after:\n- verification_gate\nforbidden_actions:\n- run_tests\n- run_scripts\n- modify_files\n- generate_patch\nfinding_lifecycle:\n  initial_status: candidate-unvalidated\n  requires_relevance_validation_by: main_orchestrating_agent\n  allowed_validation_statuses:\n  - accepted-relevant\n  - rejected-irrelevant\n  - needs-more-evidence\n  - duplicate\n  - human-decision-required\ndefault_permissions:\n  read: true\n  write: false\n  run_tests: false\n  run_verification_instruments: false\n  run_tools: false\ntool_exceptions_allowed: true\ntool_exceptions: []\n"
    },

      "path": "skills/reviewer-architecture/skill.yaml",
      "size_bytes": 1368,
      "content": "name: reviewer-architecture\ntype: skill\nversion: 0.1\npurpose: Independently review artifacts for architecture consistency, modularity,\n  scope, and ADR drift.\ninputs:\n- contract\n- diff_or_artifact\n- adrs\n- evidence_report\n- risk_surface_profile\n- failure_path_matrix\n- review_topology_rationale\noutputs:\n- architecture_review_report\ndepends_on: []\ncompatible_workflows:\n- big-feature-contract-first\n- review-only-fusion\n- pr-merge-readiness\nquality_bar:\n- Review is grounded in contract and ADRs.\n- Blocking issues are explicit.\n- Architecture risk-surface findings are tied to contract, FPM, evidence, or ADR context.\nanti_patterns:\n- Reviewing by taste without contract reference.\n- Choosing a different topology instead of reporting inconsistencies in the recorded rationale.\nmode: read_only\nactor_class: review_agent\nruns_after:\n- verification_gate\nforbidden_actions:\n- run_tests\n- run_scripts\n- modify_files\n- generate_patch\nfinding_lifecycle:\n  initial_status: candidate-unvalidated\n  requires_relevance_validation_by: main_orchestrating_agent\n  allowed_validation_statuses:\n  - accepted-relevant\n  - rejected-irrelevant\n  - needs-more-evidence\n  - duplicate\n  - human-decision-required\ndefault_permissions:\n  read: true\n  write: false\n  run_tests: false\n  run_verification_instruments: false\n  run_tools: false\ntool_exceptions_allowed: true\ntool_exceptions: []\n"

      "path": "skills/reviewer-verification/skill.yaml",
      "size_bytes": 1333,
      "content": "name: reviewer-verification\ntype: skill\nversion: 0.1\npurpose: Independently review test adequacy, impact map, evidence quality, and regression\n  coverage.\ninputs:\n- contract\n- impact_map\n- test_results\n- evidence_report\n- risk_surface_profile\n- failure_path_matrix\n- behavior_bindings\n- evidence_freshness\noutputs:\n- verification_review_report\ndepends_on: []\ncompatible_workflows:\n- big-feature-contract-first\n- safe-refactor\n- review-only-fusion\n- pr-merge-readiness\nquality_bar:\n- Missing tests are concrete.\n- Evidence gaps are actionable.\n- FPM coverage and freshness gaps identify affected risk surfaces and path classes.\nanti_patterns:\n- Accepting vague evidence.\n- Treating stale evidence or unbound FPM rows as sufficient verification.\nmode: read_only\nactor_class: review_agent\nruns_after:\n- verification_gate\nforbidden_actions:\n- run_tests\n- run_scripts\n- modify_files\n- generate_patch\nfinding_lifecycle:\n  initial_status: candidate-unvalidated\n  requires_relevance_validation_by: main_orchestrating_agent\n  allowed_validation_statuses:\n  - accepted-relevant\n  - rejected-irrelevant\n  - needs-more-evidence\n  - duplicate\n  - human-decision-required\ndefault_permissions:\n  read: true\n  write: false\n  run_tests: false\n  run_verification_instruments: false\n  run_tools: false\ntool_exceptions_allowed: true\ntool_exceptions: []\n"

    {
      "path": "templates/decision-contract.md",
      "size_bytes": 1851,
      "content": "# Design Decision Review: <title>\n\n## Purpose\n\nExplain what design boundary this packet controls and what phase transition, if\nany, human acceptance authorizes. Do not ask for blanket approval without a\ndecision inventory.\n\n## Review Procedure\n\n1. Present the full open decision inventory.\n2. Review each decision independently.\n3. For each decision, show viable options, tradeoffs, recommendation and\n   rationale.\n4. Record explicit human acceptance, change requests or approved deferrals.\n5. Proceed only when blocking decisions are accepted, changed or explicitly\n   deferred with residual risk.\n\n## Open Decision Inventory\n\n| ID | Decision | Blocking before phase exit? | Recommended path |\n|---|---|---:|---|\n| DDR-001 | <decision name> | yes | <recommended option> |\n\n## DDR-001: <Decision Name>\n\n### Problem\n\nWhat must be decided, and why does it matter now?\n\n### Options\n\n| Option | Description | Strengths | Costs / risks |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A | <option> | <strengths> | <costs/risks> |\n| B | <option> | <strengths> | <costs/risks> |\n\n### Recommendation\n\nState the recommended path.\n\n### Rationale\n\nExplain why this recommendation best fits the contract, accepted decisions,\nevidence, risk posture and current workflow phase.\n\n### Human Acceptance Question\n\nState the exact question the human must answer for this decision.\n\n## Aggregate Recommendation\n\nState which decisions must be accepted before the workflow may leave the current\nhuman-mediated gate, and which decisions, if any, may be deferred with explicit\nresidual risk.\n\n## Evidence\n\n- <artifact path or evidence source>\n\n## Freshness / Revisit Triggers\n\n- dependency major version changed\n- failed verification gate\n- reviewer flags drift\n- new requirement invalidates assumption\n\n## Rollback / Migration Notes\n\nDescribe how to undo the selected design path if it proves wrong.\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "templates/external-review-provider.yaml",
      "size_bytes": 1764,
      "content": "# External reviewer provider configuration template.\n# Project overlays may adapt this file under `.agentsflow/external-reviewers/`.\n\nprovider: claude-code\nkind: external_reviewer_provider\nversion: 1\n\nbilling:\n  expected_mode: subscription-local\n  forbid_api_key_usage: true\n  fail_if_env_present:\n    - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\n    - ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN\n    - ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL\n    - CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK\n    - CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX\n  notes: >\n    API-key based Claude usage and alternate Claude API/proxy routes are\n    forbidden for the v0.2 MVP because they can create uncontrolled cost. This\n    provider is intended for local subscription Claude Code CLI usage only.\n\nexecution:\n  command: claude\n  mode: non_interactive_print\n  sandbox_mode: require_escalated\n  output_format: json\n  permission_mode: default\n  tools: \"\"\n  model: opus\n  effort: max\n  timeout_seconds: 900\n  max_turns: 3\n  use_bare_mode: false\n  no_session_persistence: true\n\ncontext_policy:\n  start_mode: fresh_context\n  fork_conversation_context: false\n  session_persistence: false\n  input_mode: review_packet\n\npermissions:\n  read_packet_only: true\n  write_files: false\n  run_tests: false\n  run_verification_instruments: false\n  run_tools: false\n  may_request_additional_verification: true\n\ninputs:\n  review_packet_schema: schemas/review-packet.schema.json\n\noutputs:\n  reviewer_report_schema: schemas/reviewer-report.schema.json\n  invocation_metadata_schema: schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json\n\nnormalization:\n  force_finding_status: candidate-unvalidated\n  require_schema_validation: true\n  # Safe default: do not persist raw provider output unless the project has\n  # explicitly classified the review packet/output as non-sensitive.\n  preserve_raw_output: false\n  record_trace: true\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "templates/finding-validation-report.md",
      "size_bytes": 5398,
      "content": "# Finding Relevance Validation Report\n\nContract: `<path>`\nArtifact/Diff: `<path or description>`\nGate Report: `<path>`\nReview/Fusion Reports: `<paths>`\nValidator: `<main/orchestrating agent or human>`\n\n## Rule\n\nReviewer findings are candidate findings. They become accepted issues only after relevance validation.\n\n## Authority Boundary\n\nThis report is the main/orchestrating agent's relevance validation record. It is\nnot an automatic gate verdict and does not replace human-mediated decisions.\nDeterministic checks may validate report structure and evidence references, but\naccepted human decisions must still be recorded in the declared human decision\nartifact when the workflow requires them.\n\n## Validation Inputs Checked\n\n- [ ] task contract / reviewed artifact brief\n- [ ] diff or artifact under review\n- [ ] verification gate report\n- [ ] evidence bundle / logs\n- [ ] relevant ADRs / accepted decisions\n- [ ] workflow profile / effective strictness / topology\n- [ ] scope and non-goals\n\n## Validation Table\n\n| Finding ID | Source | Severity | Candidate blocker? | Candidate finding | Relevance status | Reason | Evidence checked | Decision impact |\n|---|---|---:|---:|---|---|---|---|---|\n| F-001 | reviewer-architecture | P1 | yes | ... | accepted-relevant / rejected-irrelevant / needs-more-evidence / duplicate / human-decision-required | ... | contract, diff, gate report, ADR | ... |\n\n## Canonical Finding Groups Checked\n\nUse this section when fusion grouped findings before validation.\n\n| Group ID | Group type | Finding IDs | Max candidate severity | Validation status | Reason |\n|---|---|---|---:|---|---|\n| G-001 | duplicate / related / conflict | F-001, F-002 | P1 | ... | ... |\n\n## Decision Matrix\n\n| Condition | Validation status | Blocking? | Default action |\n|---|---|---:|---|\n| Finding is supported by contract/evidence and severity is P0/P1 | accepted-relevant | yes | Fix/revise, then rerun verification gate and relevant review cycle. |\n| Finding is supported by contract/evidence but severity is P2/P3/NOTE | accepted-relevant | no | Record follow-up; no review rerun by default. |\n| Finding may be valid but required evidence is missing | needs-more-evidence | yes if mandatory evidence or P0/P1 | Produce evidence through the verification gate or a narrow evidence-probe objective; rerun review only if evidence materially changes. |\n| Finding concerns an explicit non-goal or out-of-scope preference | rejected-irrelevant | no | Record reason; no rerun. |\n| Finding is factually contradicted by contract/diff/evidence | rejected-irrelevant | no | Record contradiction; no rerun. |\n| Finding duplicates an already validated issue | duplicate | inherits original | Link to original; no rerun. |\n| Finding conflicts with accepted ADR or requires changing an accepted decision | human-decision-required | yes until resolved | Escalate to human / ADR workflow. |\n| Reviewers disagree on a P0/P1 issue and evidence is insufficient | needs-more-evidence / human-decision-required | yes | Produce/refresh evidence, run a narrow evidence probe when appropriate, or escalate. |\n| Fusion surfaces a candidate blocker from one reviewer only | needs-more-evidence / accepted-relevant / rejected-irrelevant / duplicate / human-decision-required | depends | Majority cannot erase it; validate explicitly. |\n\n## Evidence Probe Batches\n\nUse this section only when `needs-more-evidence` requires a targeted probe.\nProbe reports are evidence only; they do not accept, reject, downgrade, or close\nfindings.\n\n| Probe ID | Objective | Finding IDs | Report | Result summary | Remaining gaps |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| probe-001 | ... | F-001, F-002 | `evidence-probe-report.probe-001.json` | ... | ... |\n\n## Collision-Control Batches\n\nUse this section when the main/orchestrating agent rejects or downgrades one or\nmore P0/P1 candidate findings. Collision-control is batched per review cycle, not\nper finding.\n\n| Collision Batch ID | Finding IDs | Orchestrator collision reason | Control reviewer count | Control reports | Final triage |\n|---|---|---|---:|---|---|\n| collision-001 | F-001, F-003 | ... | 2 | `reviewer-report.control-a.md`, `reviewer-report.control-b.md` | ... |\n\n## P0/P1 Handling\n\nFor every P0/P1 candidate finding, record one of:\n\n- accepted as blocker;\n- rejected as irrelevant with evidence-based reason;\n- downgraded with reason;\n- needs more evidence;\n- escalated to human decision.\n\n## Post-Fix Materiality\n\nClassify every fix made after review before deciding whether to rerun reviewers.\n\n| Fix ID | Finding IDs | Changed artifacts | Material? | Reason | Required next action |\n|---|---|---|---:|---|---|\n| fix-001 | F-001 | `schemas/...`, `docs/...` | yes/no | ... | rerun-verification / rerun-review / no-rerun |\n\nA fix is material when it changes contracts, schemas, validators, workflow or\ngate policy, project bindings, mandatory evidence, verification output, reviewed\nbehavior, or examples used as evidence. Editorial/report-only cleanup is\nnon-material by default.\n\n## Review Cycle Decision\n\nDefault exit criterion:\n\n```text\nno_validated_blocking_findings\n```\n\nRepeated review agents are not rerun when all P0/P1 candidate findings have been\nvalidated and no validated blockers or mandatory evidence gaps remain.\n\n## Final Triage Decision\n\n<pass|pass-with-notes|needs-changes|needs-verification-evidence|human-decision-required|blocked>\n\n## Notes\n\n- ...\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "templates/fusion-report.md",
      "size_bytes": 3186,
      "content": "# Fusion Report\n\nContract: `<path>`\nReview Topology: `<topology>`\nVerification Gate Report: `<path>`\n\n## Recommended Verdict\n\n<pass|pass-with-notes|needs-changes|blocked|human-decision-required>\n\nThis is a recommendation. It becomes a final workflow decision only after main-agent/human relevance validation when the workflow requires it.\n\n## Authority Boundary\n\nFusion is decision support, not an automatic gate verdict and not a\nhuman-mediated decision. Deterministic automation may validate structure,\nschema, freshness and evidence references; the main/orchestrating agent validates\ncandidate findings; human-mediated gates remain human-owned.\n\n## Mechanical Intake\n\n| Expected report | Present? | Schema valid? | Fresh? | Assignment/provider/topic match? | Notes |\n|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|\n| ... | yes/no | yes/no | yes/no | yes/no | ... |\n\n## Canonical Finding Extraction\n\n| Canonical ID | Source finding | Source report | Provider/model | Topic/role | Severity | Evidence refs | Risk/FPM refs |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---:|---|---|\n| CF-001 | ... | ... | ... | ... | P1 | ... | ... |\n\n## Duplicate / Related / Conflict Groups\n\n| Group ID | Group type | Finding IDs | Max candidate severity | Shared claim or conflict | Fusion handling |\n|---|---|---|---:|---|---|\n| G-001 | duplicate / related / conflict | CF-001, CF-002 | P1 | ... | ... |\n\n## Topic-Pair Comparison\n\nUse this section when the review topology mirrors providers or roles over the\nsame topic.\n\n| Topic pair | Reviewer reports | Agreement | Disagreement | Fusion handling |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Consensus\n\nPoints all reviewers agree on.\n\n## Disagreements\n\n| Topic | Reviewer A | Reviewer B | Reviewer C | Fusion handling |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Candidate Blocking Issues\n\nFusion must surface any plausible P0/P1 candidate issue, even if only one reviewer found it. Fusion must not treat the candidate issue as proven truth; it must preserve it for relevance validation.\n\n| Finding ID | Source reviewer(s) | Severity | Candidate issue | Why it may block | Required validation |\n|---|---|---:|---|---|---|\n| F-001 | reviewer-adversarial | P1 | ... | ... | ... |\n\n## Candidate Non-blocking Issues\n\n- ...\n\n## Proposed Required Changes\n\nThese are proposed changes until the main/orchestrating agent validates the underlying findings.\n\n- ...\n\n## Human Decision Required\n\n- ...\n\n## Confidence\n\n<low|medium|high>\n\n## Notes\n\n- ...\n\n## Relevance Validation Handoff\n\nFor each candidate finding that could affect acceptance, the main/orchestrating agent should record:\n\n| Finding | Source reviewer(s) | Fusion classification | Relevance status | Reason | Decision impact |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| ... | ... | candidate blocker / concern | accepted-relevant / rejected-irrelevant / needs-more-evidence / duplicate / human-decision-required | ... | ... |\n\n## Review Cycle Exit Check\n\nDefault exit condition:\n\n```text\nno_validated_blocking_findings\n```\n\nFusion should recommend exiting the review cycle only when there are no unresolved\ncandidate blockers, no validated blockers, and no mandatory evidence gaps after\nmain-agent relevance validation.\n"
    },
